generated_requirements.text: The system shall support distinct user roles including Police Officer, Investigator, Administrator, Help-Desk Personnel, and External Auditor, each with a uniquely defined set of permissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Distinct roles are the foundation of the RBAC model required to protect sensitive case information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that each user can only view, create, modify, or delete records for which their assigned role explicitly grants permission.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC is the primary mechanism for ensuring sensitive case data is accessible only to authorised personnel.

generated_requirements.text: Police Officers shall be permitted to create new case records, add suspect information, attach evidence, and view cases assigned to their jurisdiction, but shall not be permitted to delete or permanently close case records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Granular permission boundaries for Police Officers prevent accidental or unauthorised destruction of case data.

generated_requirements.text: Investigators shall be permitted to view, create, and modify all case records, suspect profiles, and evidence items within cases assigned to them, and shall be permitted to escalate or reassign cases to other investigators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Investigators require broader read-write access to conduct thorough case management and collaboration.

generated_requirements.text: Administrators shall be permitted to create, modify, deactivate, and delete user accounts; assign and revoke roles; configure system-wide settings; and access all case records across all jurisdictions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Administrators need full system control to manage users and maintain operational continuity.

generated_requirements.text: Help-Desk Personnel shall be permitted to view system defect reports and enhancement requests, create and update help-desk tickets, and access anonymised or redacted case metadata solely for the purpose of reproducing reported defects.
generated_requirements.rationale: Help-Desk access must be scoped to support functions without exposing sensitive investigative content.

generated_requirements.text: External Auditors shall be permitted to export audit trail records in read-only format and shall have no permission to view, create, modify, or delete any case, suspect, or evidence records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restricting External Auditors to audit trail exports only ensures oversight without compromising investigative data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a permanent, append-only audit trail that records every create, read, update, delete, export, and login/logout action performed on any record classified as critical, including the user identity, timestamp, action type, affected record identifier, and before/after field values.
generated_requirements.rationale: A comprehensive audit trail is essential for accountability, forensic review, and legal admissibility of case records.

generated_requirements.text: Critical records shall be defined as: case records, suspect profiles, evidence items, witness statements, arrest records, charge sheets, and user account configuration changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Explicit definition of critical records ensures consistent and complete audit coverage across the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce immutability of audit trail entries by storing them in a write-once data store, cryptographically hashing each entry with a chained hash (e.g., blockchain-style or HMAC chain), and rejecting any attempt to modify or delete an existing audit record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cryptographic chaining ensures that any tampering with audit records is detectable, preserving evidentiary integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow External Auditors to export audit trail data in structured formats (CSV, JSON, and PDF) via a dedicated, access-controlled export interface, without granting access to the underlying case management database.
generated_requirements.rationale: A dedicated export interface decouples audit access from operational data, preventing integrity compromise during audits.

generated_requirements.text: Each audit trail export shall be itself logged as an audit event, recording the exporting user's identity, the date/time range of exported records, the export format, and a cryptographic hash of the exported file.
generated_requirements.rationale: Logging export events creates a meta-audit layer that detects unauthorised or excessive data extraction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide Help-Desk Personnel with a defect and enhancement tracking module that supports ticket creation, categorisation (defect or enhancement), priority assignment, status tracking (open, in-progress, resolved, closed), and linkage to affected system modules.
generated_requirements.rationale: A structured tracking module enables systematic resolution of system issues and continuous improvement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate configurable alerts and notifications for case-related events, including case assignment, status changes, evidence additions, approaching deadlines, and escalations, delivered via in-application notifications and email.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely alerts ensure that responsible officers and investigators are aware of critical case developments without manual polling.

generated_requirements.text: Each user shall be able to configure their personal alert preferences, including which event types trigger notifications, preferred delivery channels (in-app, email), and notification frequency (immediate, digest).
generated_requirements.rationale: User-configurable alerts reduce notification fatigue while ensuring relevant events are not missed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide context-sensitive help that is accessible from every screen and workflow step, displaying guidance relevant to the current action, available fields, and permitted operations for the user's role.
generated_requirements.rationale: Context-sensitive help reduces training burden and supports users of varying technical proficiency in completing tasks correctly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a customisable graphical user interface that allows users to rearrange dashboard widgets, configure default views, adjust font sizes, and select colour themes, within boundaries set by the Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Interface customisation accommodates diverse user preferences and operational contexts, improving efficiency and satisfaction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support advanced case search and filtering, allowing users to search by case number, suspect name, date range, case status, assigned officer, jurisdiction, and evidence type, with results filtered according to the requesting user's RBAC permissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Powerful, permission-aware search is essential for efficient case retrieval in a large law enforcement database.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the attachment of digital evidence files (images, video, audio, documents) to case records, enforcing file type validation, virus scanning, and a maximum configurable file size limit per attachment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Controlled evidence attachment ensures data quality and protects the system from malicious file uploads.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a full version history of every case record, suspect profile, and evidence item, allowing authorised users to view previous versions and the diff between any two versions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Version history supports accountability and enables recovery of information inadvertently overwritten.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via multi-factor authentication (MFA), requiring at minimum a password and a time-based one-time password (TOTP) or hardware token before granting access.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA significantly reduces the risk of unauthorised access to sensitive law enforcement data through compromised credentials.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between client and server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher, and all data at rest shall be encrypted using AES-256 or an equivalent approved algorithm.
generated_requirements.rationale: End-to-end encryption protects sensitive criminal justice data from interception and unauthorised disclosure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate an authenticated session after a configurable period of inactivity (default: 15 minutes), requiring re-authentication before access is restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic session timeout prevents unauthorised access from unattended workstations in shared law enforcement environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock a user account after five consecutive failed authentication attempts and require an Administrator or automated unlock process (with MFA verification) to restore access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout mitigates brute-force credential attacks against the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce the principle of least privilege, ensuring that no user role is granted permissions beyond the minimum necessary to perform its defined functions, and that permission sets are reviewed and re-certified by Administrators at least annually.
generated_requirements.rationale: Least-privilege enforcement limits the blast radius of compromised accounts or insider threats.

generated_requirements.text: The audit trail store shall be logically and physically separated from the operational case management database, with no application-layer write or delete access to the audit store from any user role.
generated_requirements.rationale: Physical separation of the audit store prevents even privileged users from tampering with the evidentiary record.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any user-initiated search query and return results within 3 seconds for result sets of up to 10,000 records under a concurrent load of 500 active users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast search response is critical for operational efficiency in time-sensitive law enforcement scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 concurrent authenticated users without degradation of response times beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability to high concurrency ensures the platform remains usable during peak operational periods such as major incidents.

generated_requirements.text: Page load times for any screen within the application shall not exceed 2 seconds on a standard broadband connection (≥10 Mbps) under normal load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive page loads reduce friction for field officers who may be accessing the system under time pressure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with ISO 9241-11 (usability) and ISO 9241-171 (accessibility) standards, ensuring that all interface elements are operable, perceivable, understandable, and robust for users with varying abilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: ISO 9241 compliance ensures the platform is legally and ethically accessible to all law enforcement personnel regardless of disability.

generated_requirements.text: The system's graphical interface shall support screen reader compatibility (ARIA landmarks and labels), keyboard-only navigation, and a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for all text elements, in conformance with WCAG 2.1 Level AA.
generated_requirements.rationale: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance operationalises ISO 9241-171 accessibility requirements for users with visual or motor impairments.

generated_requirements.text: A new user with no prior CCTNS experience shall be able to complete core tasks (create a case, add a suspect, attach evidence) within 30 minutes of initial system access, aided only by the built-in context-sensitive help.
generated_requirements.rationale: Learnability benchmarks ensure the system is practical for officers with limited IT training.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% (measured monthly), excluding pre-announced maintenance windows, with unplanned downtime not exceeding 43.8 minutes per month.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is critical for a law enforcement platform where downtime can directly impede active investigations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement automated failover to a geographically redundant standby environment, with a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of ≤15 minutes and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of ≤5 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Aggressive RTO/RPO targets minimise data loss and operational disruption in the event of a primary system failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated, encrypted backups of all operational data and audit trail data at least every 4 hours, with backup integrity verified by automated restoration tests conducted monthly.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular verified backups ensure data can be recovered accurately after hardware failure, corruption, or ransomware events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be architected using modular, loosely coupled components with documented APIs, enabling individual modules (e.g., alerting, audit, help-desk) to be updated or replaced without requiring full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future enhancements and defect fixes.

generated_requirements.text: All source code shall adhere to a defined coding standard, include inline documentation for all public interfaces, and achieve a minimum unit test coverage of 80% as measured by automated tooling in the CI/CD pipeline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Code quality standards and test coverage reduce regression risk and accelerate onboarding of new development team members.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on-premises, in a government-approved private cloud, or in a hybrid configuration, with deployment scripts and infrastructure-as-code templates provided for each supported topology.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deployment flexibility accommodates the diverse infrastructure constraints of different law enforcement agencies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose a documented RESTful API that allows authorised external agency systems (e.g., courts, forensic labs, immigration databases) to query case status and submit evidence references, subject to the same RBAC rules applied to human users.
generated_requirements.rationale: An interoperability API enables seamless data exchange with partner agencies while maintaining access control integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all data inputs against defined schemas and business rules at both the client and server layers, rejecting malformed inputs and displaying descriptive, role-appropriate error messages without exposing internal system details.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dual-layer input validation prevents injection attacks and ensures data quality without leaking exploitable system information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide Administrators with a real-time dashboard displaying system health metrics including CPU utilisation, memory usage, active session count, database query latency, and audit trail write queue depth.
generated_requirements.rationale: A health dashboard enables proactive identification and resolution of performance or capacity issues before they impact users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Administrators to define and manage user groups, assign cases to groups, and configure group-level permissions that supplement individual role permissions without overriding RBAC constraints.
generated_requirements.rationale: Group-level management supports task-force and multi-agency collaboration scenarios common in complex investigations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support configurable case lifecycle workflows, allowing Administrators to define case statuses, permitted status transitions, and the roles authorised to perform each transition.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable workflows accommodate the procedural variations of different jurisdictions and case types.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate pre-built and ad-hoc reports for Administrators and Investigators, including case volume by jurisdiction, case resolution rates, average time-to-close, and evidence chain-of-custody reports, exportable in PDF and CSV formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reporting capabilities support operational oversight, resource planning, and compliance with statutory reporting obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable data retention policy, automatically archiving case records that have exceeded their active retention period and permanently deleting records only after explicit approval by an Administrator, with the deletion event recorded in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Controlled retention and deletion ensures compliance with data protection legislation while preserving the audit record of all lifecycle events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and alert Administrators in real time when any anomalous access pattern is identified, including access to an unusually high volume of records within a short time window, access outside normal working hours, or repeated access to records outside a user's assigned jurisdiction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Anomaly detection provides an early-warning layer against insider threats and compromised account misuse.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support internationalisation (i18n) and localisation (l10n), enabling the interface, help content, and notifications to be rendered in multiple languages as configured by the Administrator, with date, time, and number formats adapted to the selected locale.
generated_requirements.rationale: Localisation support ensures the platform is usable by law enforcement personnel across different linguistic regions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a sandbox or training environment that mirrors production functionality but is fully isolated from live case data, allowing new users to practise workflows without risk to operational records.
generated_requirements.rationale: A training environment reduces the risk of accidental data corruption by inexperienced users during onboarding.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all API calls made by external systems, including the calling system's identity, endpoint accessed, parameters supplied (excluding sensitive field values), response code, and timestamp, and include these logs in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: API-level audit logging extends accountability to machine-to-machine interactions, closing a potential audit gap.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo a third-party penetration test at least once per year and after any major release, with all critical and high-severity findings remediated within 30 days of the test report being issued.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular independent penetration testing ensures that security controls remain effective against evolving threat vectors.

They also require additional user actions for closing unwanted windows.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Vertical scrolling should be minimized. This may be done by placing important information at the top and providing links to information that is further down the page. Horizontal scrolling should be avoided wherever possible. Vertical scrolling should be minimized. ground_truth_requirements.text: Designing for input device independence: User interfaces should be designed to allow activation of controls by a variety of input devices. The ability to choose between different input devices for activating controls such as links, fields and buttons is important both for users who prefer a certain input mode, mobile users and users with disabilities. In general, device independence can be achieved if the functionality is operable via a keyboard. ground_truth_requirements.text: Making user interfaces robust: User interfaces should be designed to be as robust as possible in the face of changing technology. ground_truth_requirements.text: Acceptable opening / download times: Application pages should be designed and implemented so that there are acceptable opening times and download times for the expected range of technical contexts of use (e.g. bandwidth between the application and the user). ground_truth_requirements.text: Providing clear error messages: The content of error messages shown on the pages or special error pages should clearly state the reason why the error occurred and, if possible, actions the user can take to resolve the error. ground_truth_requirements.text: Selecting appropriate page lengths The length of a page should be selected so as to support the primary purpose and use of the page. Longer pages can be more appropriate when users want to read the content without interruptions or when the page needs to match a paper counterpart. In such cases, pages can usually be grouped in different categories, using one layout scheme for each category consistently. ground_truth_requirements.text: Observing principles of human perception When designing application pages, the general principles of human perception should be taken into account. The International Standards mentioned below shall be consulted for guidance. Practical guidelines for presenting information to the user are to be found in ISO 9241-12. Guidance on selecting and using different forms of interaction techniques is to be found in ISO 9241-14 to ISO 9241-17. ISO 9241-14 gives guidance about menus, ISO 9241-15 about command dialogues, ISO 9241-16 about direct manipulation and ISO 9241-17 about forms. In addition, when designing multimedia information presentations, the design principles and recommendations described in ISO 14915-1 to ISO 14915-3 should be taken into account. Appropriate content presentation also plays a key role in accessibility. generated_requirements.text: The system shall accept credit card payments and process them through a PCI-DSS-compliant payment gateway, supporting at minimum Visa, Mastercard, and American Express.
generated_requirements.rationale: Credit card payment is the primary transaction mechanism described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated fraud detection on every credit card transaction and flag or block transactions that exceed a configurable risk threshold before authorization.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated fraud detection protects both the retailer and customers from fraudulent transactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send automated email notifications to customers upon order placement, payment confirmation, shipment dispatch, and delivery.
generated_requirements.rationale: Email notifications keep customers informed throughout the order lifecycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow vendors to add, edit, and deactivate product listings including title, description, images, price, SKU, and stock quantity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vendors must be able to manage their own inventory within the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically decrement inventory stock levels upon successful order placement and trigger a low-stock alert to the vendor when stock falls below a configurable threshold.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time inventory management prevents overselling and supports vendor operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide vendors with an order management interface to view, accept, reject, and update the fulfillment status of orders assigned to them.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vendors need visibility and control over orders for their products.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide administrators with a system configuration interface to manage platform settings including tax rates, supported currencies, shipping zones, and payment gateway credentials.
generated_requirements.rationale: Administrators require centralized control over platform-wide operational parameters.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with distinct permission sets for the roles of Customer, Vendor, Manager, and Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC ensures each user type can only access functionality appropriate to their role.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support installation and deployment from a Yoggie Corporation USB hardware appliance without requiring any additional server infrastructure.
generated_requirements.rationale: The USB-appliance deployment model is a core architectural constraint of Project Gamma J.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administrator interface to install, enable, disable, and remove plugins without requiring system downtime.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hot-pluggable plugin management supports extensibility and operational continuity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support over-the-air (OTA) software updates that can be applied through the administrator interface and take effect without full system reinstallation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Straightforward update mechanisms are required for the hardware-appliance deployment model.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with an external shipping cost estimation API to retrieve real-time carrier rates and estimated delivery windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accurate shipping estimates depend on live data from carrier or aggregator APIs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with an intrusion detection system (IDS) that monitors inbound and outbound network traffic and generates alerts for suspicious activity.
generated_requirements.rationale: IDS integration is explicitly required to protect the platform against network-level attacks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an online help system with context-sensitive guidance accessible from every major page, targeting administrators, managers, vendors, and customers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contextual help reduces support burden and improves operator and user proficiency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide comprehensive administrator and manager documentation covering installation, configuration, plugin management, and troubleshooting procedures.
generated_requirements.rationale: Complete documentation is essential for the hardware-appliance deployment and ongoing maintenance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all credit card numbers and sensitive payment data at rest using AES-256 encryption and in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of sensitive payment data at rest and in transit is a fundamental PCI-DSS and data-protection requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce HTTPS for all web traffic and reject any HTTP connections with a redirect to the secure endpoint.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mandatory HTTPS prevents eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks on all user sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include a hardware or software firewall on the Yoggie USB appliance that blocks all inbound ports except those explicitly required for web and administrative access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Firewall protection is explicitly called out as a required security measure for the appliance deployment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall hash all user passwords using a salted cryptographic algorithm (bcrypt or Argon2) and shall never store or transmit passwords in plaintext.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure password storage protects user accounts in the event of a data breach.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock a user account for a configurable duration after five consecutive failed authentication attempts and notify the account owner by email.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout mitigates brute-force credential attacks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit log of all administrative actions, payment events, and security-relevant events, retaining logs for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logs support forensic investigation, compliance, and accountability for sensitive operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 concurrent user sessions without degradation in response time beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly requires the platform to serve large numbers of concurrent users reliably.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return product search results within 2 seconds for 95% of queries under peak load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast search response is critical to customer experience and conversion rates in a retail platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the end-to-end payment authorization and order confirmation flow within 5 seconds for 95% of transactions under normal load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Slow checkout flows increase cart abandonment and erode customer trust.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load any product detail page within 3 seconds on a standard broadband connection for 95% of page requests.
generated_requirements.rationale: Page load performance directly impacts customer engagement and SEO ranking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum uptime of 99.9% measured on a rolling 30-day basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is explicitly required to serve large concurrent user loads reliably.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned failure and restore full service within 15 minutes (RTO), with a maximum data loss of 5 minutes of transactions (RPO).
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined RTO and RPO targets ensure business continuity for a retail platform handling financial transactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated daily backups of all order, customer, and configuration data and store backups in an encrypted location separate from the primary appliance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular backups are essential for recovery from hardware failure or data corruption on the USB appliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be fully functional on the latest two major versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge without requiring browser plugins or extensions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Broad browser compatibility is required to ensure accessibility through standard web browsers as stated in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on the Yoggie Corporation USB appliance without modification to the appliance firmware, relying only on the appliance's documented runtime environment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Constraining deployment to the documented appliance API ensures hardware-agnostic portability within the target platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards to ensure usability for customers with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance broadens the customer base and meets legal obligations in many jurisdictions.

generated_requirements.text: A new customer shall be able to complete a first purchase, from product search to order confirmation, within 10 minutes without consulting external help.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ease of first-time purchase is a key usability benchmark for retail platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display clear, human-readable error messages for all user-facing validation failures, payment errors, and system errors, avoiding technical jargon.
generated_requirements.rationale: Understandable error messages reduce user frustration and support self-service resolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render correctly and be fully operable on mobile devices with screen widths from 320px to 428px using a responsive layout.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mobile responsiveness ensures broad accessibility for customers using smartphones.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a documented modular architecture with separation of concerns, enabling a developer unfamiliar with the system to locate and modify a feature within 2 hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular, well-documented code reduces the cost and risk of future enhancements and bug fixes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum unit and integration test coverage of 80% across all core business logic modules, with automated tests executed on every build.
generated_requirements.rationale: High automated test coverage reduces regression risk during updates and plugin changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose a versioned internal plugin API so that third-party plugins remain compatible across minor system updates without requiring plugin code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: A stable plugin API reduces maintenance overhead for both the platform team and plugin developers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all application errors with stack traces, timestamps, and contextual metadata to a centralized log store accessible to administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralized error logging accelerates diagnosis and resolution of production issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide administrators with a real-time dashboard displaying key health metrics including CPU usage, memory utilization, active sessions, transaction throughput, and error rates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational visibility is necessary for administrators to proactively manage the hardware appliance and platform health.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow managers to generate reports on sales revenue, order volumes, top-selling products, and inventory levels for configurable date ranges.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reporting capabilities support business decision-making by managers as described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all user-supplied input on both the client and server sides to prevent injection attacks including SQL injection, XSS, and command injection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Input validation is a foundational defense against the most common web application attack vectors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) protection on all state-changing HTTP endpoints.
generated_requirements.rationale: CSRF protection prevents attackers from tricking authenticated users into performing unintended actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to create and manage accounts, including registration, login, password reset, and profile management (name, address book, payment methods).
generated_requirements.rationale: Customer account management is a prerequisite for persistent cart, order history, and personalized experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide customers with a full order history view showing order status, itemized details, and a self-service option to initiate returns or cancellations within policy limits.
generated_requirements.rationale: Order history and self-service returns reduce customer support load and improve post-purchase experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally by supporting load-balanced deployment across multiple Yoggie USB appliance nodes, with session state shared via a distributed cache.
generated_requirements.rationale: Horizontal scalability is required to sustain performance under large concurrent user loads as the business grows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide three distinct user roles — Observing Astronomer, Operations Staff, and Software Developer — each with a uniquely defined set of permissions governing access to telescope hardware, scheduling queues, and instrument configurations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control is the primary governance mechanism described in the use case for all user interactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that Observing Astronomers cannot directly command telescope hardware, Operations Staff can manage hardware configurations and scheduling queues, and Software Developers hold the highest privilege level for installation, testing, and maintenance operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Privilege boundaries between roles must be strictly enforced to protect safety-critical telescope hardware.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user session using unique credentials before granting access to any system function, and shall re-authenticate upon privilege escalation requests.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication is a prerequisite for enforcing RBAC and preventing unauthorised access to safety-critical controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit log of all user actions, privilege escalations, hardware commands, and configuration changes, retaining entries for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit trails are essential for accountability, incident investigation, and regulatory compliance in a multi-user observatory system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an automated sequencer that allows Observing Astronomers to define, submit, and execute observation sequences without issuing direct hardware commands.
generated_requirements.rationale: The automated sequencer is the primary interface for astronomers and must abstract away low-level hardware control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a queue-based scheduling system that allows Operations Staff to add, remove, reorder, and prioritise observation programs in the scheduling queue.
generated_requirements.rationale: Queue management is a core operational function enabling flexible and efficient use of telescope time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support simultaneous queue-based, service, and remote observing modes, allowing the active observing mode to be switched by Operations Staff without interrupting ongoing observations where possible.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple flexible observing modes are explicitly required to accommodate diverse operational scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multiple simultaneous user sessions from on-site, remote, and base-facility locations, providing each session with a consistent, network-transparent user interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Network transparency across physical locations is a fundamental architectural requirement of the distributed system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide real-time status monitoring displays accessible to all authorised users, showing the current state of the telescope, instruments, detectors, and scheduling queue, updated at a minimum refresh rate of 1 Hz.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-point status monitoring is required for all user roles to supervise ongoing observations effectively.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform on-line quick-look quality assessment of science data immediately after detector readout, presenting results to the Observing Astronomer within 60 seconds of readout completion.
generated_requirements.rationale: Near-real-time data quality assessment is explicitly required to allow astronomers to evaluate science data during the observation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall manage the complete data lifecycle from detector readout through on-line processing, quick-look display, and archiving, ensuring no science data frames are lost between pipeline stages.
generated_requirements.rationale: End-to-end data lifecycle management is a core functional responsibility of the control software.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall archive all acquired science data and associated metadata (including instrument configuration, telescope pointing, and environmental conditions) to a persistent data store with write-once semantics to prevent post-hoc modification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Archival consistency and data integrity require immutable storage of science data and its provenance metadata.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall coordinate the configuration and operation of multiple simultaneously mounted instruments, ensuring that only one instrument at a time receives active detector readout commands unless the system is explicitly configured for parallel readout.
generated_requirements.rationale: Coordination logic for multiple mounted instruments is required to prevent conflicting hardware commands.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the integration and operation of visitor instruments by providing a defined hardware and software interface that visitor instrument controllers must implement, without requiring modification to the core control software.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visitor instrument support is explicitly mentioned and requires a stable integration interface to maintain system integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Software Developers to install, update, and roll back software components on any node in the distributed system without requiring a full system shutdown, subject to safety interlocks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Maintenance operations at the highest privilege level must be possible with minimal operational disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement safety interlocks that automatically prevent or abort any hardware command — regardless of the issuing user role — that would place the telescope, instruments, or detectors in an unsafe state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Safety-critical constraints must be enforced at the system level, independent of user privilege, to protect hardware.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect hardware faults or communication failures in any distributed node and automatically execute a predefined fail-safe procedure, notifying Operations Staff within 5 seconds of fault detection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fail-safe behaviour upon fault detection is essential for protecting telescope hardware and ongoing observations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide Operations Staff with the ability to manually override the automated sequencer and issue direct hardware commands, subject to confirmation prompts and audit logging.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operations Staff require direct hardware control capability for maintenance, emergency response, and configuration management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support observation planning tools that allow Observing Astronomers to define observation programs — including target coordinates, instrument configurations, exposure parameters, and constraints — prior to queue submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-observation planning is a prerequisite for effective queue-based scheduling and science program execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate observation program definitions against current instrument capabilities and telescope constraints at submission time, rejecting invalid programs with descriptive error messages.
generated_requirements.rationale: Early validation prevents invalid programs from entering the queue and causing runtime failures during observations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a consistent graphical user interface that presents identical functionality and layout to users regardless of whether they are accessing the system from on-site, remote, or base-facility locations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Network-transparent UI consistency is explicitly required to support all physical access locations equally.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall propagate all state changes across all active user sessions within 2 seconds of the change occurring, ensuring a consistent view of system state for all simultaneous users.
generated_requirements.rationale: State synchronisation latency directly impacts the ability of multiple simultaneous users to make correct operational decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver telescope and instrument status telemetry to all monitoring displays with an end-to-end latency not exceeding 1 second from the time of measurement to display update, under normal network conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low-latency telemetry is critical for real-time monitoring and timely detection of anomalies during observations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the transition from detector readout completion to archived data record creation within 5 minutes for a standard science frame, excluding network transfer time to off-site archives.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely archiving ensures data is preserved promptly and available for quality assessment without delaying subsequent observations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 20 simultaneous authenticated user sessions without degradation of response time beyond 10% of baseline single-user performance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability under concurrent load is required to support multi-user operations across all facility locations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an operational availability of at least 99.5% during scheduled observing nights, measured as the fraction of scheduled observing time during which the control software is fully functional.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during observing nights is critical to maximise science output and telescope utilisation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from a single-node software failure and resume normal operations within 60 seconds without loss of queued observation programs or archived data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid recovery from node failures minimises observing time lost and protects data integrity in the distributed system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall replicate all queued observation programs and in-progress observation state to a hot-standby node, such that failover can be completed within 30 seconds with no data loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hot-standby replication ensures continuity of operations and prevents loss of science programs during hardware or software failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all network communications between distributed nodes and user interface clients using TLS 1.3 or higher, with certificate-based mutual authentication for inter-node connections.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of distributed communications protects against interception and tampering, especially for remote and base-facility connections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce the principle of least privilege, ensuring that each user session is granted only the minimum permissions required for the user's assigned role, with no ability to self-escalate privileges.
generated_requirements.rationale: Least-privilege enforcement reduces the attack surface and limits the impact of compromised credentials.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate idle user sessions after a configurable inactivity timeout (default: 30 minutes), requiring re-authentication to resume.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorised access via unattended terminals at remote or on-site locations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and block brute-force authentication attempts by locking an account after five consecutive failed login attempts and alerting Operations Staff.
generated_requirements.rationale: Brute-force protection is a baseline security control for any multi-user system accessible from remote locations.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall be operable by an occasional visiting astronomer with no prior system-specific training within 2 hours of initial orientation, as measured by task-completion rate in usability testing.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly identifies occasional visitors as users, requiring the interface to be accessible to non-expert operators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual help and inline documentation for all user interface controls, accessible without leaving the current workflow.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline help reduces the learning curve for occasional users and supports safe operation without external documentation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present all error messages and status alerts in plain language that clearly identifies the affected component, the nature of the fault, and the recommended corrective action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clear error messaging is essential for operators to respond quickly and correctly to faults during time-critical observations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be decomposed into independently deployable software components with well-defined interfaces, such that any single component can be updated or replaced without modifying other components.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture is required to support Software Developer maintenance operations and long-term system evolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a comprehensive test harness and simulation mode that allows Software Developers to test all system functions — including hardware command sequences — without commanding real telescope hardware.
generated_requirements.rationale: A simulation mode is essential for safe testing and maintenance without risking damage to telescope hardware.

generated_requirements.text: All system interfaces between distributed components shall be documented using a machine-readable interface definition language, and interface versions shall be tracked to ensure backward compatibility across software updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Documented, versioned interfaces reduce integration risk during maintenance and support long-term system evolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate on standard POSIX-compliant operating systems and shall not depend on proprietary hardware-specific libraries for any function above the hardware abstraction layer.
generated_requirements.rationale: Portability across standard platforms reduces vendor lock-in and supports long-term maintainability of the observatory system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use open, documented network protocols (e.g., TCP/IP, standard RPC frameworks) for all inter-component communication, ensuring interoperability with external systems such as proposal management and archive retrieval tools.
generated_requirements.rationale: Open protocols ensure the system can interoperate with external observatory systems and future technology upgrades.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall guarantee the integrity of all archived science data by computing and storing a cryptographic checksum (SHA-256 or stronger) for every data file at the time of archiving, and verifying the checksum upon every retrieval.
generated_requirements.rationale: Checksum verification ensures that archived science data has not been corrupted during storage or transfer.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all inter-component messages and hardware commands to a centralised, fault-tolerant logging service, with each log entry timestamped to a precision of 1 millisecond using a synchronised time source.
generated_requirements.rationale: Precise, centralised logging is required for fault diagnosis, audit, and synchronisation analysis in the distributed system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronise all distributed node clocks to a common time reference (e.g., GPS-disciplined NTP) with a maximum offset of 10 milliseconds between any two nodes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clock synchronisation is critical for correlating events across distributed nodes and ensuring accurate observation timestamps.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow both Gemini telescopes to be controlled from a single unified interface, with clear visual indication of which telescope each command or status display pertains to, and with independent safety interlocks for each telescope.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly covers control of one or both Gemini telescopes, requiring unified yet clearly differentiated multi-telescope support.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide Operations Staff with the ability to suspend, resume, or abort any active observation sequence without data loss of already-acquired frames, and with automatic re-queuing of the interrupted program.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational control over active sequences is required for responding to changing conditions, faults, or priority changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support dynamic re-prioritisation of the observation queue in response to changes in observing conditions (e.g., seeing, cloud cover, wind speed), automatically promoting or demoting programs based on their defined observing constraints.
generated_requirements.rationale: Condition-responsive queue management maximises science output by matching programs to prevailing observing conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a remote observing mode in which a geographically remote Observing Astronomer has the same functional capabilities as an on-site astronomer, with the exception of physical hardware access, and with no additional latency-induced functional restrictions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Remote observing is an explicitly required mode and must be functionally equivalent to on-site observing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall degrade gracefully under partial network failure, maintaining local control of telescope hardware and continuing to acquire and buffer science data even when connectivity to remote or base-facility users is lost.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation ensures that network outages do not halt observations or risk hardware safety.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configuration management database (CMDB) that records the current and historical hardware and software configuration of every system component, accessible to Software Developers for maintenance and audit purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: A CMDB is essential for managing the complexity of a distributed, multi-component observatory control system over its operational lifetime.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a maximum command-to-hardware-acknowledgement round-trip latency of 500 milliseconds for all safety-critical hardware commands (e.g., telescope stop, instrument shutter close) under normal operating network conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low latency for safety-critical commands is essential to ensure timely hardware response in emergency or fault scenarios.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Definition Observing astronomers are the end users of the system. They range greatly in experience from occasional users of the system to very experienced users such as staff astronomers at the Gemini 8m Telescopes site and service observers. Purpose of work Whatever their level of experience, observing astronomers will wish to concentrate on the efficient acquisition of astronomical data and on-line assessment of data quality, rather than on the details of controlling the telescope and instruments. To allow observing astronomers to achieve this objective, the system must offer to a user an interface which, while fulfilling the various operational requirements in the different modes and offering status information both automatically and on request at any required level, is still simple to learn and secure in its use. Privileges Observing astronomers shall have no privileges as far as the direct control of the telescope is concerned. They shall not be able to send control commands directly but they must be able to enquire about the status of the telescope or any subsystem at any time. The intent is not to restrict the capabilities of the observing astronomer in any way but rather to establish a single point of control and responsibility. Programs, as opposed to observers, may have the capability of direct control of the telescope. This would allow the observer to create an observing program which requested a telescope control function but would not allow the observer to enter (for instance) a command to slew the mount. Astronomers are typically given control access to instruments, however. Operation mode Traditional interactive operation shall normally be replaced by operation via an automatic sequencer. This is essential to support operational requirements such as service observing and flexible scheduling (as defined later in Section 2.7 on page 2-12). A certain degree of interaction shall be provided, meaning in this case that the user will interact with the scheduler program, rather than with the control programs directly. Observing commands will normally be submitted via the User interface to a queue for later execution. It must also be possible to break and resequence this queue. e.g. as a result of the quality assessment of previous data. In particular, Operations staff will be able to enable direct interactive operation, but this shall not be considered as the normal operation mode for the reasons explained in Section 2.7 on page 2-12. It is evident that, for some functions (such as adjustment of spectrograph slit width for seeing conditions) it must be necessary to include interactive capability. However each instance of such a function should be examined as a candidate for automation - such as focussing. The existence of the scheduling queue shall be transparent to the on-site observer during the initial phases of telescope operation. Only after experience has been gained with the system will the existence of the queue become evident to the on site observer.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Definition Currently, operations staff includes 'night assistants' and staff of the 'operations group'. Depending on the operations implemented on the Gemini 8m Telescopes, they may become telescope operators, operations supervisors of the Gemini 8m Telescopes telescope, and instrument operators and have to overview the Gemini 8m Telescopes operation. These users will be very experienced users of the system. They will be very familiar with the Gemini 8m Telescopes and/or its instruments. Purpose of work Operations staff will control the Gemini 8m Telescopes indirectly via a scheduler program or directly via commands. They will supervise telescope operation and will be able to advise observing astronomers on what they have to do to use telescope and instruments efficiently. They shall also monitor general performance and system safety. Privileges Operations staff shall have privileges to access all commands and maintenance procedures in case of problems. This includes direct control of physical units. However, they shall not have access to subsystems while these are in normal operation. If they need to access other parts of the system appropriate reconfiguration procedures have to be run. Operation mode Operations staff shall have access to operation tables in update mode, while observing astronomers will have access to them only in read mode (for example the list of filters mounted on an instrument). Again, the intent is not to restrict the capabilities of the observing astronomer in any way but rather to establish a single point of control and responsibility. At the present time it is not clear how to handle visitor instrument requirements in this area. They shall be able to change the operational status of units according to the results of tests performed on such units (e.g. to see if a faulty unit can be declared as operational again and redefined as part of the environment in use).
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Definition Software development and maintenance are staff: •based either at the Gemini 8m Telescopes site or base facility, or •based at the Gemini 8m Telescopes remote operations facility, or •non Gemini staff from a contract company or from an associated Institute. These users will have a deep understanding of the way the Gemini 8m Telescopes software works. Purpose of work Software development and maintenance staff intervene when there is a major problem to be solved or an upgrade to be installed. They also perform system generation and installation of new software packages or new releases, according to established test and validation procedures. Privileges Software development and maintenance staff need the highest privilege in order to be able to modify everything in the system. Nevertheless strict configuration control guidelines must be followed to prevent interference with ongoing normal operation. Operation mode Software development and maintenance staff usually work at the test level (see Section 3.2.1.3 on page 3-4) for the part of the software under test. Other parts of the Gemini 8m Telescopes may also need to run in test mode to support integration tests. This means that a number of commands are available which allow the hardware concerned to be tested (test commands) and are not accessible to observing astronomers.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Normally three scientiﬁc instruments are mounted on the cassegrain focus of the telescope. The Gemini 8m Telescopes are thus characterized all the time as in a multi-instrument context. Parallel access to all the mounted instruments shall be provided, though only one instrument has access to the telescope beam (active instrument).Instruments which feed two detectors by using beam splitters or ﬁeld splitters is considered as a single active instrument. Feeding two separate instruments via beam splitters or ﬁeld splitters and providing separate control nodes is not a requirement. Various situations are possible for the other (inactive) instruments: Normally three scientiﬁc instruments are mounted on the cassegrain focus of the telescope. The Gemini 8m Telescopes are thus characterized all the time as in a multi-instrument context. Parallel access to all the mounted instruments shall be provided, though only one instrument has access to the telescope beam (active instrument).Instruments which feed two detectors by using beam splitters or ﬁeld splitters is considered as a single active instrument. Feeding two separate instruments via beam splitters or ﬁeld splitters and providing separate control nodes is not a requirement. Various situations are possible for the other (inactive) instruments: they shall be able to take calibration or ﬂat ﬁeld exposures in parallel they shall be able to prepare for an exposure to start as soon as the telescope beam is switched back to them (in this case, they are in a hot standby situation) they shall be able to work; at all foreseen operation levels (observing, maintenance, test) (see Section 3.2.1 on page 3-3).
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The requirement to provide for support, installation, and operation of outside instruments brought by the observer has several implications for the system. Due to the specialized nature of visitor instrumentation it is unlikely that complete integration into the Gemini environment is either feasible or warranted. In this case a subset of the available functionality must be made available through a standardized interface. The Gemini Telescopes view all instruments as operating as servers, responding to commands from the upper levels of the system. Visitor instruments must be capable of operating in this mode to be adequately supported. It should be the goal of this interface that it be a subset of the existing instrumentation interface (rather than a separate system). At a minimum this interface should support: The requirement to provide for support, installation, and operation of outside instruments brought by the observer has several implications for the system. Due to the specialized nature of visitor instrumentation it is unlikely that complete integration into the Gemini environment is either feasible or warranted. In this case a subset of the available functionality must be made available through a standardized interface. The Gemini Telescopes view all instruments as operating as servers, responding to commands from the upper levels of the system. Visitor instruments must be capable of operating in this mode to be adequately supported. It should be the goal of this interface that it be a subset of the existing instrumentation interface (rather than a separate system). At a minimum this interface should support: acquisition of status information of instrument by other systems capability to enter preprogrammed observing sequences capability to offset the telescope position and focus
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The requirements to have instruments operated as single units imply that several user stations will be active at the same time on the Gemini 8m Telescopes. On these user stations, different kinds of users may be working at the same time with the Gemini 8m Telescopes software. Independently of the location of users at the telescope site, they shall be able to access (according to their privileges) any part of the whole setup with a simple logon and conﬁguration operation. In other words. any subsection of the whole Gemini 8m Telescopes system should be accessible and controllable from any single point (but of course with protection ensuring security and safety). A particularly simple case of multi-use of the system is multi-point monitoring. By this it is meant that, while some (active) user is in control of the Gemini 8m Telescopes, someone else can follow what they are doing by monitoring all the relevant data from telescope and instruments. This will typically be needed by the operation supervisor. All other users wishing to monitor Gemini 8m Telescopes operations have to go through the procedures set up by Operations and get permission to do so. The multi-point monitoring mode might also be important when certain difﬁcult or rare problems occur, when expert advice is needed and can only be obtained from colleagues situated remotely. Multi-point monitoring allows them to follow directly the results of tests performed and investigate how the system is working (e.g. by selecting different display pages with the up-to-date status information on different parts of the system). Multi-point monitoring also allows a local observer to be monitored and advised by a remote supervisor. Monitoring shall exist both in the form of automatic displays of status information at different locations, and in the form of explicit access to the required status information from any point. Monitoring shall not affect the performance of ongoing observations. On these user stations, different kinds of users may be working at the same time with the Gemini 8m Telescopes software. Independently of the location of users at the telescope site, they shall be able to access (according to their privileges) any part of the whole setup with a simple logon and conﬁguration operation.
