generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow producers to submit new XML schemas and related metadata artifacts through an online web-based interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Online submission is a core workflow for producers contributing structured data resources to the registry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow producers to submit XML schemas and metadata artifacts through an offline process when network connectivity is unavailable or degraded.
generated_requirements.rationale: Forward-deployed units operating under degraded communications must still be able to contribute XML artifacts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow producers to modify previously submitted XML schemas and metadata artifacts, maintaining a full version history of all changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Version history ensures traceability and auditability of schema evolution over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow producers to assemble composite XML schemas from existing registered XML tag structures and artifacts stored in the registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reuse of registered components promotes semantic consistency and reduces duplication across defense applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow consumers to browse the XML registry through a web-based interface, navigating available schemas, tag sets, and metadata by category and domain.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browsing capability enables consumers to discover available XML resources without requiring prior knowledge of specific identifiers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow consumers to perform keyword and structured searches across all registered XML tags, schemas, and metadata artifacts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Search functionality is essential for consumers to efficiently locate relevant XML resources within a large registry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow consumers to validate XML documents against registered schemas and tag structures via the web-based registry interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Validation ensures that consumer-produced documents conform to authoritative registered schemas before operational use.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow consumers to download registered XML tags, schemas, and related documents from the registry for local use.
generated_requirements.rationale: Download capability supports offline use and integration of registry artifacts into consumer applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow managers to define, enforce, and update naming conventions for all XML tags and schemas registered in the system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consistent naming conventions are critical for semantic interoperability across the defense information enterprise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow managers to create, modify, suspend, and delete user accounts, and assign roles (producer, consumer, manager) with associated permissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: User lifecycle management is required to govern access and maintain registry integrity across organizational changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce Discretionary Access Control (DAC), allowing resource owners to grant or revoke read, write, and execute permissions on individual XML artifacts to specific users or groups.
generated_requirements.rationale: DAC enables fine-grained, owner-driven access management consistent with standard defense information security policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce Mandatory Access Control (MAC), restricting access to XML artifacts based on security classification labels (e.g., Unclassified, Confidential, Secret, Top Secret) assigned to both users and resources.
generated_requirements.rationale: MAC is required to protect sensitive defense information in accordance with military classification standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with COTS Relational Database Management System (RDBMS) products as the underlying data store for the XML registry, using standard SQL interfaces.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration with existing COTS RDBMS infrastructure leverages proven technology and reduces deployment cost.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose standard APIs (e.g., REST or SOAP over HTTPS) for programmatic access to registry functions including submission, search, retrieval, and validation of XML artifacts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standard APIs enable heterogeneous defense applications to integrate with the registry without custom interfaces.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support data replication of the XML registry across geographically distributed nodes to ensure availability at forward-deployed and fixed installations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Replication ensures that registry data remains accessible even when primary nodes are unreachable due to operational conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement transaction management to guarantee atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability (ACID) for all registry write operations, including submissions, modifications, and deletions.
generated_requirements.rationale: ACID compliance prevents data corruption and ensures registry integrity during concurrent or interrupted operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the Common Operating Environment (COE) architecture standards, including COE component interfaces, packaging, and registration requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: COE compliance is a mandatory architectural constraint for integration within the Defense Information Infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support operation across the full military readiness spectrum, including peacetime training, maintenance, crisis, and wartime operational modes, with mode-specific configuration profiles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Different operational modes impose distinct performance, security, and availability requirements that the system must accommodate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a conflict resolution mechanism for registry data that has diverged across replicated nodes during periods of network partition, applying deterministic merge rules upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict resolution is essential to restore registry consistency after degraded-communications episodes common in military operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and maintain a complete audit log of all user actions, including logins, submissions, modifications, downloads, searches, and administrative changes, with timestamps and user identifiers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logs are required for accountability, forensic investigation, and compliance with defense information assurance policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify producers via configurable alerts (email or system message) when a submitted artifact has been approved, rejected, or requires revision by a manager.
generated_requirements.rationale: Workflow notifications keep producers informed of registry governance decisions without requiring manual status polling.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support XML Schema Definition (XSD) and, where applicable, Document Type Definition (DTD) formats for schema registration and validation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Support for both XSD and DTD ensures compatibility with the range of XML standards used across legacy and modern defense systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow managers to approve or reject producer-submitted XML artifacts before they are published and made discoverable in the registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: A manager approval workflow enforces governance and prevents non-compliant or erroneous schemas from entering the authoritative registry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support role-based access control (RBAC) as a layer above DAC and MAC, restricting access to system functions (e.g., schema approval, user management) based on assigned roles.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC provides a structured, manageable access model that complements DAC and MAC for function-level authorization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a mechanism for producers to package XML schemas and associated artifacts into versioned bundles for batch offline submission and subsequent synchronization.
generated_requirements.rationale: Batch bundling supports efficient offline workflows for units that accumulate submissions during periods of communications blackout.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to consumer search queries and schema retrieval requests within 3 seconds under normal operating conditions with up to 500 concurrent users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive search and retrieval are critical for operational tempo in time-sensitive military decision-making environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% (approximately 8.7 hours downtime per year) for the primary registry service at fixed installations.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability at fixed installations ensures continuous access to authoritative XML resources for peacetime and crisis operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain registry read and search functionality at forward-deployed nodes even when connectivity to the primary registry node is completely unavailable, using locally replicated data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Degraded-communications resilience is a fundamental operational requirement for forward-deployed military units.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between system components, including API calls, replication traffic, and user sessions, shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive defense information from interception on potentially hostile or untrusted networks.

generated_requirements.text: All data stored in the registry database, including XML artifacts, metadata, and audit logs, shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or an equivalent FIPS 140-2 validated algorithm.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest protects classified and sensitive registry content from unauthorized physical or logical access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via multi-factor authentication (MFA), supporting Common Access Card (CAC) or equivalent PKI-based credentials as the primary authentication mechanism.
generated_requirements.rationale: CAC-based MFA is the standard authentication mechanism for DoD systems and is required to meet information assurance mandates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate idle user sessions after a configurable inactivity timeout not exceeding 15 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access through unattended authenticated sessions on shared terminals.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale to support a minimum of 10,000 registered XML schemas and 1,000,000 individual XML tag definitions without degradation in search or retrieval performance.
generated_requirements.rationale: The defense information enterprise encompasses a large and growing number of data standards that the registry must accommodate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be portable across the major operating system platforms used in the DII environment, including Red Hat Enterprise Linux, Windows Server, and Solaris, without requiring platform-specific code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Portability across heterogeneous OS platforms is required to support the diverse hardware and software environments of defense installations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be compatible with at least three COTS RDBMS products (e.g., Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, PostgreSQL) through a database abstraction layer, requiring no application code changes to switch between them.
generated_requirements.rationale: RDBMS portability prevents vendor lock-in and supports deployment across installations with different existing database infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned failure and restore full registry service within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 30 minutes and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 5 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict RTO and RPO targets ensure minimal data loss and rapid restoration of registry services during operational incidents.

generated_requirements.text: The system's web-based interface shall conform to usability standards such that a new user with basic XML knowledge can successfully locate, validate, and download a registered schema within 10 minutes without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ease of use for consumers reduces training burden and accelerates adoption across diverse military organizations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual help, tooltips, and inline documentation within the web interface for all major producer and consumer workflows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline guidance reduces user errors and dependency on external documentation in time-constrained operational settings.

generated_requirements.text: The system's source code and configuration shall be structured to allow a developer unfamiliar with the codebase to locate, understand, and modify any functional module within 4 hours, supported by up-to-date technical documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Maintainability is critical for a long-lived defense system that must be updated to reflect evolving XML standards and security requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support zero-downtime rolling upgrades for software patches and minor version updates, ensuring registry availability is uninterrupted during maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Zero-downtime upgrades are essential for a system that must remain operational across continuous military readiness cycles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce referential integrity constraints such that no XML tag or schema artifact can be deleted from the registry if it is referenced by another registered artifact, unless a cascade or override is explicitly authorized by a manager.
generated_requirements.rationale: Referential integrity prevents orphaned dependencies that could break consumer applications relying on registered schemas.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all submitted XML schemas for syntactic correctness and conformance to registered naming conventions before accepting them into the registry workflow.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-acceptance validation prevents malformed or non-compliant schemas from entering the governance workflow and consuming manager review time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide managers with a dashboard displaying registry health metrics, including total registered artifacts, pending approvals, replication status across nodes, and recent audit events.
generated_requirements.rationale: A management dashboard enables proactive governance and rapid identification of registry integrity or availability issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support internationalization (i18n) of the user interface, enabling display of labels, messages, and help text in multiple languages as required by coalition partner deployments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Coalition operations require the system to be usable by non-English-speaking partner nation personnel.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all failed authentication attempts and automatically lock an account after five consecutive failed login attempts, notifying the responsible manager.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout and alerting mitigate brute-force credential attacks against the registry in hostile network environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support synchronization of offline-submitted artifact bundles with the primary registry upon restoration of network connectivity, with duplicate detection and conflict flagging.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reliable synchronization ensures that offline submissions from forward-deployed units are accurately integrated into the authoritative registry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process and ingest an offline submission bundle of up to 500 XML artifacts within 10 minutes of synchronization initiation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely ingestion of offline bundles ensures that registry data is current and available to consumers shortly after reconnection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a documented, versioned API specification (e.g., OpenAPI 3.0) for all external interfaces, and shall maintain backward compatibility for at least two prior major API versions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned API documentation and backward compatibility protect consumer applications from breaking changes during system upgrades.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and alert managers to potential semantic conflicts or duplicate tag definitions between newly submitted schemas and existing registered artifacts using automated similarity analysis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated duplicate detection supports registry integrity and promotes reuse by preventing redundant tag proliferation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo and pass a formal security accreditation process compliant with DoD Risk Management Framework (RMF) requirements before deployment in any operational environment.
generated_requirements.rationale: RMF accreditation is a mandatory DoD requirement for all information systems operating within the Defense Information Infrastructure.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Environmental Mode: This is the real-time operations that must react to varying degrees of readiness to full scale wartime operations such as crisis planning with the use of heterogeneous data types and sources, transfer capabilities, data management services. COE Compliance. XML Services shall be segmented. COE sponsors shall adhere to compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS. In the fixed (static) mode of operation (base or data processing megacenter), the data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel to adjust for varying workloads and sizes of associated databases. These workloads and databases are expected to change more frequently and to a greater extent than for processing associated with deployed units In a changing (dynamic) environment, such as with deployed units, the workload and database sizes may be more predetermined (given a more precise mission) and require access to fewer data management administrative capabilities than needed in a fixed environment. The XS shall have the ability to redefine or reset names of connect descriptors to database server instances. Connect descriptors are fully qualified object names and include address (protocol/host/port) and instance name. In a degraded communications environment, there is a need, for example, to be able to reset session time-out values if the data management services are being accessed by users affected by the communications degradation. At a minimum, the session time-out values shall be user definable and be able to be reset prior to initialization of a user session. The goal is to provide the option of dynamically changing session time values based on current communications performance identified by capabilities of the network management or DBMS. End User Mode: Portion of XS services shall be used by various classes of users: data consumers, data and database managers, network information infrastructure resource managers. Some of these uses of the data management services will entail unique requirements that shall be fulfilled within the capability of XS services. Maintenance Mode: This mode includes modification and/or addition of application data segments, user permission, privileges, and restructuring storage and memory areas. In addition, maintenance also shall pertain to shutdown, open not-mounted and online/off-line implementations, modifications, upgrade, or other related actions. The data management services shall support managing various types of data, database architectures and platforms that includes hardware and software at the specified sites. Training Mode. In support of training activities, the data management services shall provide for the same processing as would be encountered in a production environment. However, access to the database may be via a training application access to the DBMS rather than from the production mission application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: COE Software will be qualified through formal validation tests of the SRS level requirements. The Qualification Methods applied to the software shall include test, demonstration, analysis, and inspection (T, D, A, I). COE Software will be qualified through formal validation tests of the SRS level requirements. The Qualification Methods applied to the software shall include test, demonstration, analysis, and inspection (T, D, A, I). TEST. A qualification method that is carried out by operation of the item/component/I/F (or some part of the computer S/W configuration item, etc.) and that relies on the collection and subsequent examination of data. DEMONSTRATIONS A qualification method that is carried out by operation of the item/component/I/F (or some part of the computer S/W configuration item, etc.), and that relies on observable functional operation not requiring the use of elaborate instrumentation or special test equipment. ANALYSIS A qualification method that is carried out by the processing of accumulated data. An example of accumulated data is the compilation of data obtained from other qualification methods. Examples of the processing of accumulated data are interpretations or extrapolations made from the data. INSPECTION A qualification method that is carried out by visual examination, physical manipulation, or measurement to verify that the requirements have been satisfied

ground_truth_requirements.text: Automatically: Indicates processing initiated during execution of other processes, but dependent on information and/or parameters to be generated or supplied to these other processes. The information / parameters may be data dependent, or application dependent, or dependent on a manual process/human intervention. It will include controls qualifying the processing involved. Business Rule: A narrative description of policies, procedures, or principles within an organization. Business rules can be divided in to four categories: definitions, facts, constraints, and derivations. Definitions are business rules that define entities and attributes. Facts are either links (relationships) between entities or associations between an entity and attributes Constraints are conditions about the data that must always be true. They are the integrity rules that protect the data in the eventual database. Derivations are business rules that materialize a new piece of information (often attribute values) from other pieces of information. For example, a mathematical derivation might specify that you can obtain a person's age by subtracting his or her birth date from the current date. Commit/Rollback: An individual transaction is processed (commit) or discarded (rollback) by the proponent maintainer of the data items involved. Discretionary Access Controls (DAC): A means of restricting access to objects based on the identity of subjects or groups to which they belong. The controls are discretionary in the sense that a subject with a certain access permission is capable of passing that permission on to any other subject. Dynamically Generated Processing: Indicates processing initiated during execution of other processes, but dependent upon information and/or parameters to be generated or supplied to these other processes. The information/parameters may be data dependent, or application dependent, or dependent on a manual process/human intervention. It will include controls qualifying the processing involved. Location Transparency: occurs when the physical location of data is transparent to the applications and users of the database system. For example, a view that joins table data from several databases provides location transparency because the user of the view does not need to know where the data originates from. Mandatory Access Control (MAC): Imediates access to an object based on the clearance level of the subject (user) and the sensitivity label of the object. (These controls are always enforced above any discretionary control implemented by users). Mirrored Databases: Replication and maintenance of a database on a transaction basis for the purpose of rapid error or failure recovery as supported by the resident COTS RDBMS own system utilities and operating system. Object: A passive entity that contains or receives information. Access to an object potentially implies access to the information it contains. Examples of objects are records, blocks, pages, segments, files, directories, directory trees, and programs, as well as bits, bytes, words, fields, processors, video displays, keyboards, clocks, printers, and network nodes. Proponent Scheme: Describes the sites at which databases are replicated and also who owns and has update authority with respect to the data at each site. It refers to proponency at the source and record level. Redundant Array of Inexpensive Disks (RAID): A RAID system appears as one very large, reliable disk to the CPU. The main reason for using RAID storage is its reliability. RAID has the same advantages as shadowing and striping at a lower cost. There are several types/levels of RAID implementations, including: RAID 0 (known as disk striping), RAID 1 (known as disk shadowing), RAID 3 (in which data is distributed in small increments across all data disks and adds a parity value to a separate disk for recovery if any disk fails, RAID 4 (in which data is distributed in large chunks across all data disks and also has a single parity disk. RAID 4 intended to overcome performance penalties of RAID 3 for small transfers. RAID 5 (in which parity over RAID 3 or RAID 4 implementations), and RAID 6 ( in which two parity disks in addition to data disks are used in an attempt to further improve performance). In a RAID 5 implementation, the data is stored as are check sums and other information about the contents of each disk in the array. If one disk is lost, the others can use the check sums and other stored information to recreate the lost data. Storage system vendors may provide additional enhancements to RAID level implementations to improve performance and reliability. Remote Data Access (RDA): is an ISO (9579) application layer interoperability standard (protocol and formats) to support access by an application to a (remote) DBMSs over an OSI network. The goal of RDA is to allow interoperability between applications (clients) and databases (servers) of different manufacturers so that an application is able to read and update data in remote databases via well defined standards. RDA defines a set of client and server standards and a mapping of SQL commands to these services. RDA also defines an interface to ISO (transaction processing) two phase commit TP services in the case where updates to multiple remote databases need to be coordinated. RDA does not yet define interoperability between server databases (i.e. it is not yet a standard for distributed database management). Replication Scheme: Information that precisely identifies DBs, or partitions of DBs, to be copied and/or distributed, replication schedules, and master/remote sites that are to receive the copies. Spatial DBMS: Geographic information system that organizes and maintains spatial data (i.e. data with graphical attributes) in terms of type, scale, location(s), extent, topology and geometry. Supports queries of spatial data where the selection criteria are defined by spatial attributes. SRI: A Standing Request For Information (SRI) is a capability in which CASS monitors for the occurrence of conditions established by an application program, and notifies the calling or establishing application program when the conditions are satisfied. An SRI may be one of three types: timer-based, data-based, or message-based. Subject: An active entity, generally in the form of a person, process, or device that causes information to flow among objects or changes the system state. Technically, a process/domain pair. Transaction Journalling: Individual messages or database transactions are stored in a journal file, which may be a linear log file or a circular file.

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the term is well recognized outside its container term, designate it as an element. Example: CTRY_CD, CTRY_NM, CTRY_ABBRD_NM, CTRY_OFF_NM, CTRY_SCP_NT_TX, and CTRY_PSTL_NM are all characteristics (ER-attributes) for the entity CTRY. In the relational world, they are columns within the table ctry, attributes of the entity ctry in er-modeling, and member attributes in object modeling. It would be expected that a submitter would identify them as attributes rather than elements. But if they were identified as attributes of the element ctry, then the additional baggage (other attributes) must be carried, or submitted as separate elements. We wish to limit the proliferation of tags, so we strongly urge folks to use XML_Attributes sparingly. If the term is well recognized outside its container term, designate it as an element. generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide a graphical mission planning interface that allows operators to define UAV flight routes, waypoints, altitudes, loiter patterns, and mission objectives prior to launch.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mission planning is a core operational workflow required before any UAV sortie can be executed.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall allow operators to upload approved mission plans to the UAV flight management system and receive confirmation of successful upload before flight execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: Confirmation of plan upload ensures the UAV executes the intended mission and prevents unintended flight behavior.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support real-time modification of active UAV flight plans, including dynamic re-routing, waypoint insertion or deletion, and altitude changes, during flight operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Battlefield conditions change rapidly and operators must be able to adapt UAV missions in real time.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide automated flight behavior management modes, including autonomous loiter, orbit, return-to-home, and lost-link contingency behaviors, configurable per UAV type.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated behaviors ensure safe UAV operation during degraded or lost data link conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide a standardized graphical user interface (GUI) that presents a consistent layout, control paradigm, and symbology across all supported UAV types, requiring no more than four hours of type-specific retraining for qualified operators transitioning between UAV platforms.
generated_requirements.rationale: A standardized interface reduces operator retraining burden and supports rapid cross-platform transitions in multi-service environments.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support simultaneous command and control of at least four UAVs from a single operator station.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-UAV control from a single station increases operational efficiency and reduces personnel requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide real-time display of UAV telemetry data, including position, altitude, airspeed, heading, fuel/battery status, engine health, and data link signal strength, updated at a minimum rate of 1 Hz.
generated_requirements.rationale: Continuous telemetry monitoring is essential for operator situational awareness and aircraft safety.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall continuously monitor data link health and display link quality metrics, including signal strength, latency, and packet loss rate, with visual and audible alerts when thresholds are exceeded.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data link health monitoring enables operators to take corrective action before loss of control occurs.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide operators with direct control of UAV payload sensors, including camera gimbal orientation, zoom level, sensor mode (EO/IR), and image capture commands.
generated_requirements.rationale: Payload sensor control is a primary mission function enabling intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) operations.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall receive, process, and display full-motion video (FMV) and still imagery from UAV payload sensors in real time with a maximum end-to-end display latency of 2 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low-latency imagery display is critical for time-sensitive targeting and situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall allow operators to annotate imagery with geospatial coordinates, target designations, and tactical symbols before dissemination to external systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Annotated imagery provides actionable intelligence context required by downstream C4I and intelligence consumers.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall disseminate processed imagery and telemetry data to connected C4I and intelligence networks, including JSTARS, TAMPS, and Trojan Spirit II, using applicable military data standards and interface protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Interoperability with named external systems is a primary mission requirement for battlespace data sharing.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall exchange tactical messages with JSTARS using the Joint Variable Message Format (JVMF) and support receipt and display of JSTARS ground surveillance track data.
generated_requirements.rationale: JSTARS integration requires adherence to JVMF to enable interoperable tactical data exchange.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall interface with TAMPS to import and export mission route data, threat overlays, and airspace deconfliction information using TAMPS-compatible data formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: TAMPS integration enables pre-mission planning data exchange and airspace coordination.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall interface with Trojan Spirit II to transmit and receive intelligence imagery, signals intelligence (SIGINT) cues, and associated metadata using applicable NSA/DoD-approved protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Trojan Spirit II integration enables dissemination of ISR products to national-level intelligence networks.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall implement role-based access control (RBAC), defining distinct operator roles (e.g., Pilot Operator, Payload Operator, Mission Commander, System Administrator) with permissions enforced at the function and data level.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC ensures that only authorized personnel can execute safety-critical or sensitive functions.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall authenticate all users via DoD Common Access Card (CAC) or equivalent PKI-based credential before granting access to any system function.
generated_requirements.rationale: CAC-based authentication enforces identity assurance consistent with DoD information assurance policy.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall maintain a tamper-evident audit log of all operator actions, system events, and data exchanges, retaining logs for a minimum of 90 days and making them accessible only to authorized administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logging supports accountability, incident investigation, and compliance with DoD security requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support degraded-mode operations, maintaining basic UAV command and control and telemetry display functions when one or more external network interfaces are unavailable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Degraded-mode operation ensures mission continuity when communications infrastructure is partially disrupted.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall automatically execute a pre-configured lost-link contingency plan for each UAV when the data link is lost for more than a configurable timeout period (default: 30 seconds).
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated lost-link response prevents uncontrolled UAV flight and reduces risk of aircraft loss or airspace violation.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide a modular software architecture that allows individual subsystems (e.g., mission planning, payload control, data dissemination) to be updated, replaced, or extended without requiring full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modularity enables rapid integration of new UAV types, payloads, and external systems with minimal system-wide impact.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support configuration profiles for each supported UAV type, enabling operators to load type-specific parameters, control mappings, and display templates from a centrally managed profile library.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configuration profiles enable rapid platform transitions and ensure correct system behavior for each UAV type.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide a system health monitoring dashboard displaying the operational status of all hardware and software subsystems, with color-coded indicators and drill-down fault diagnostics.
generated_requirements.rationale: System health monitoring enables operators and maintainers to detect and respond to faults before they affect mission execution.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall generate and transmit standardized operational reports (e.g., mission summaries, imagery exploitation reports) to designated C4I nodes upon mission completion or on operator demand.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated reporting reduces operator workload and ensures timely dissemination of mission results to command elements.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support import of digital terrain elevation data (DTED) and geospatial map layers to enable terrain-aware mission planning and obstacle avoidance route generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Terrain data integration is essential for safe low-altitude flight planning in diverse operational environments.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall enforce airspace deconfliction by alerting operators when planned or active UAV routes conflict with restricted airspace, no-fly zones, or other registered air traffic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Airspace deconfliction prevents mid-air collisions and unauthorized airspace violations during operations.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall respond to all operator control inputs (e.g., flight command submission, payload slew commands) within 500 milliseconds under nominal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sub-second control response is required to maintain effective real-time command of UAVs in dynamic tactical environments.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall process and display incoming telemetry streams from all concurrently controlled UAVs with a maximum processing latency of 500 milliseconds from data receipt to screen update.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low telemetry processing latency ensures operators have current situational awareness for safety-critical decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall achieve a system availability of no less than 99.5% during scheduled operational periods, excluding planned maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is essential for a mission-critical military system that may be required to operate continuously during sustained operations.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall recover from any single software subsystem failure and restore full operational capability within 60 seconds without operator intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid automated recovery minimizes mission disruption caused by software faults during active operations.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall achieve a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of no less than 2,000 hours for the complete integrated system under field operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A high MTBF ensures the system can sustain extended operational deployments without unplanned downtime.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall encrypt all data in transit between the ground control station and UAVs, and between the TCS and external C4I systems, using NSA-approved cryptographic algorithms at a minimum of AES-256.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of all data links prevents adversarial interception and manipulation of command, control, and intelligence data.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall encrypt all data at rest, including stored mission plans, imagery, telemetry logs, and audit records, using FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of stored data protects sensitive operational and intelligence information in the event of physical compromise of the system.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall enforce mandatory session timeout, automatically locking the operator interface after no more than 15 minutes of inactivity and requiring re-authentication to resume.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access to an unattended operator station in a field environment.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall be certified and operated in compliance with DoD Risk Management Framework (RMF) at Impact Level 4 (IL4) or higher, as determined by the system security classification.
generated_requirements.rationale: RMF compliance is a mandatory DoD requirement for systems processing Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) or classified data.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS GUI shall conform to MIL-STD-1472 human engineering design criteria, ensuring that all critical controls and alerts are accessible and legible under field lighting conditions, including direct sunlight and low-light environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: MIL-STD-1472 compliance ensures the interface is usable by operators in the full range of field environmental conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall present all safety-critical alerts (e.g., lost link, airspace violation, low fuel) using distinct visual (red flashing) and audible alarm modalities that are distinguishable from non-critical notifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Distinct multi-modal alerting ensures operators do not miss safety-critical events in high-workload or noisy environments.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall enable a trained operator to complete a full mission plan for a single UAV, from system login to plan upload confirmation, within 20 minutes under nominal conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Efficient mission planning workflows reduce time-to-launch and support rapid response to emerging tactical requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS hardware and software shall be deployable and fully operational on Army, Navy, Marine Corps, and Air Force standard ground vehicle platforms and shelter configurations without hardware modification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-service portability is a core requirement for a joint-use tactical system deployed across diverse service branch platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS hardware shall meet MIL-STD-810H environmental requirements for temperature, humidity, vibration, shock, dust, and altitude, supporting operation in temperature ranges from -32°C to +60°C.
generated_requirements.rationale: Field-rugged environmental compliance ensures reliable operation across the full range of global deployment environments.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS software architecture shall be scalable to support deployment configurations ranging from a single-operator, single-UAV forward operating base setup to a multi-operator, multi-UAV battalion-level operations center without requiring software redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability across deployment sizes ensures the system meets the varying operational needs of different unit types and mission profiles.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall be maintainable such that a trained field-level technician can diagnose and replace any line-replaceable unit (LRU) within 30 minutes using only standard military tool sets.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid field-level maintenance minimizes system downtime during sustained operations where depot-level support is unavailable.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS software shall be structured such that integration of a new UAV type requires modification of only the UAV-specific adapter module, with no changes required to core mission planning, telemetry, or dissemination subsystems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Isolated UAV-type integration reduces the cost and risk of adding new platforms to the system over its lifecycle.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide built-in self-test (BITE) capabilities that execute automatically at system startup and on operator demand, reporting pass/fail status for all major hardware and software components within 5 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: BITE capabilities enable rapid fault isolation and reduce mean time to repair (MTTR) in field environments.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall implement redundant data link interfaces, automatically switching to a backup communication channel within 5 seconds of primary link failure without loss of UAV command authority.
generated_requirements.rationale: Redundant data links prevent loss of UAV control due to single-point communication failures in contested electromagnetic environments.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall perform continuous integrity checking of all received telemetry and command acknowledgment data, flagging and discarding corrupted or replayed messages and alerting the operator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Message integrity checking prevents adversarial injection or replay of false telemetry or command data from compromising UAV operations.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support interoperability with NATO STANAG 4586 UAV control system standards, enabling command and control of STANAG 4586-compliant UAVs without platform-specific customization.
generated_requirements.rationale: STANAG 4586 compliance enables coalition interoperability and broadens the range of UAV platforms the TCS can control.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support Link 16 tactical data link messaging for exchange of air and ground track data, fire control coordination, and situational awareness information with joint and coalition forces.
generated_requirements.rationale: Link 16 is the primary NATO tactical data link standard and is required for joint and coalition interoperability.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall enforce a two-person integrity (TPI) rule for safety-critical commands, such as arming payloads or overriding lost-link contingency plans, requiring confirmation from a second authenticated operator before execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: Two-person integrity for safety-critical commands reduces the risk of accidental or unauthorized execution of high-consequence actions.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall store all mission data, imagery, and telemetry logs in a format compliant with the National Imagery Transmission Format Standard (NITFS) to ensure long-term archival compatibility and interoperability with DoD intelligence exploitation systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: NITFS compliance ensures that TCS-generated data products are exploitable by the full range of DoD and intelligence community systems.

ground_truth_requirements.text: To the extent possible, the TCS will use standard DoD components in order to achieve commonality. The TCS will use the computing hardware specified by the service specific procurement contracts. The individual armed services will identify TCS computing hardware, the desired level of TCS functionality, the battlefield C4I connectivity, and the particular type of air vehicle and payloads to be operated depending upon the deployment concept and area of operations. TCS hardware must be scaleable or modular to meet varying Service needs. TCS hardware will permit long range communications from one TCS to another, data storage expansion, access to other computers to share in processing capability, and multiple external peripherals. To the extent possible, the TCS will use standard DoD components in order to achieve commonality. The TCS will use the computing hardware specified by the service specific procurement contracts. The individual armed services will identify TCS computing hardware, the desired level of TCS functionality, the battlefield C4I connectivity, and the particular type of air vehicle and payloads to be operated depending upon the deployment concept and area of operations. TCS hardware must be scaleable or modular to meet varying Service needs. TCS hardware will permit long range communications from one TCS to another, data storage expansion, access to other computers to share in processing capability, and multiple external peripherals.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Upon the selection of a shutdown command the TCS shall enter the Shutdown State, which will cause the TCS to be placed in a condition where power can be removed without impacting operations as well as causing damage to the system, and from which restart of the system can be accomplished normally. [SSS043] Shutdown of the TCS shall include storage as well as deletion, as specified by the operator, of mission data files [SSS044], shutdown of appropriate functions [SSS045], shutdown of HCIs [SSS046], and proper termination of all active interfaces [SSS047]. There shall be no modes of operation in the shutdown state. There shall be no modes of operation in the shutdown state. [SSS048]

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall display the AV status, to include but not be limited to the AV location and flight and avionics system status. [SSS143] When the data link is interrupted, the TCS shall present the last known AV status values and the time at which the last values were reported. [SSS144] The TCS shall be capable of displaying fuel parameters to the operator to include as a minimum, remaining fuel, flow rate, and bingo fuel. [SSS145] Bingo fuel is the minimum amount of fuel necessary to return to the designated Recovery site with reserve fuel. The TCS shall compute the estimated position of the AV during Loss of Link (LOL) based upon the last known AV position and anticipated flight path based on current flight mode (e.g. flight route plan mode, emergency flight route plan mode, maintain current heading mode). [SSS146] TCS shall display a LOL timer to the operator initiating a LOL onset. [SSS536] The TCS shall display the AV status, to include but not be limited to the AV location and flight and avionics system status. [SSS143] When the data link is interrupted, the TCS shall present the last known AV status values and the time at which the last values were reported. [SSS144] The TCS shall be capable of displaying fuel parameters to the operator to include as a minimum, remaining fuel, flow rate, and bingo fuel. [SSS145] Bingo fuel is the minimum amount of fuel necessary to return to the designated Recovery site with reserve fuel. The TCS shall compute the estimated position of the AV during Loss of Link (LOL) based upon the last known AV position and anticipated flight path based on current flight mode (e.g. flight route plan mode, emergency flight route plan mode, maintain current heading mode). [SSS146] TCS shall display a LOL timer to the operator initiating a LOL onset. [SSS536]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall permit the operator to control the payload using all methods supported by the payload installed in the selected AV. [SSS152] The TCS shall provide a graphical user interface display for the purpose of controlling the payload. [SSS153] The TCS shall provide override of payload automated as well as preprogrammed inputs. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall display the current search footprint and the search history of the payload on the map. [SSS157] The TCS shall display the current search footprint and the search history of the payload on the map. [SSS157]

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide automatic pointing commands for directional antennas [SSS167], and shall allow for the manual pointing of directional antennas when desired by the operator [SSS168]. The TCS shall be capable of positioning antennas to maintain LOS as well as SATCOM. [SSS170] The TCS shall incorporate antenna pedestal 3 -axis stabilization to compensate for platform (e.g. ship, or HMMWV) motion, if applicable. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall receive, process, and present status data to the operator so that the status of the data terminal and the supported AV data link can be monitored. [SSS177] The TCS shall be capable of monitoring and displaying the signal strength of the received and transmitted signals for the selected data terminal. [SSS178] The TCS shall be capable of monitoring and displaying the signal quality of the received and transmitted signals for the selected data terminal. [SSS179] The TCS shall be capable of presenting to the operator a visual depiction of the minimum and maximum data link operational ranges. [SSS180] The TCS shall receive, process, and present status data to the operator so that the status of the data terminal and the supported AV data link can be monitored. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall have the functionality to process payload product data from Electro Optical (EO), Infrared (IR), and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) payloads. [SSS182] This functionality, as a minimum, shall include: formatting, storing, internally routing, and recording the video [SSS190]; creating and storing a freeze frame of the video [SSS191]; retrieving and displaying the video [SSS192]; printing a hard copy of freeze frame video [SSS193]; and processing digital imagery for export and dissemination [SSS194]. Payload data includes the digital and analog imagery and associated telemetry sent to the TCS from each of these payloads. The TCS shall be able to store up to 24 hours of payload data. [SSS184] External storage can be utilized for this purpose. The TCS shall be in compliance with Common Imagery Ground Surface Station (CIGSS), United States Imagery Standards (USIS), Video Working Group Standards Architecture, National Imagery Transmission Format (NITF) Version 2.0, and Global Command Control Systems (GCCS) when processing payload imagery data. [SSS185] The NITF 2.0 imagery files generated by the TCS shall contain the necessary telemetry and support data to permit subsequent imagery exploitation by C4I systems. [SSS186] The TCS shall have a built-in text entry capability including the ability to annotate textual information on imagery. [SSS187] The TCS shall be capable of receiving secondary HAE UAV payload imagery. [SSS188] Payload telemetry data shall be available to support other TCS functions as required. [SSS189] The TCS shall have the functionality to process payload product data from Electro Optical (EO), Infrared (IR), and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) payloads. [SSS182] This functionality, as a minimum, shall include: formatting, storing, internally routing, and recording the video [SSS190]; creating and storing a freeze frame of the video [SSS191]; retrieving and displaying the video [SSS192]; printing a hard copy of freeze frame video [SSS193]; and processing digital imagery for export and dissemination [SSS194]. Payload data includes the digital and analog imagery and associated telemetry sent to the TCS from each of these payloads. The TCS shall be able to store up to 24 hours of payload data. [SSS184] External storage can be utilized for this purpose. The TCS shall be in compliance with Common Imagery Ground Surface Station (CIGSS), United States Imagery Standards (USIS), Video Working Group Standards Architecture, National Imagery Transmission Format (NITF) Version 2.0, and Global Command Control Systems (GCCS) when processing payload imagery data. [SSS185] The NITF 2.0 imagery files generated by the TCS shall contain the necessary telemetry and support data to permit subsequent imagery exploitation by C4I systems. [SSS186] The TCS shall have a built-in text entry capability including the ability to annotate textual information on imagery. [SSS187] The TCS shall be capable of receiving secondary HAE UAV payload imagery. [SSS188] Payload telemetry data shall be available to support other TCS functions as required. [SSS189]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall display live and recorded imagery data, with as well as without annotation and overlay, upon operator request. [SSS195] Annotation includes operator generated comments as well as graphics which are superimposed on the imagery. Overlays consist of information obtained from external sources that is selected by the operator for presentation with the imagery. The TCS shall provide the capability to simultaneously view imagery as well as data from more than one payload, when applicable. [SSS537] The TCS operator shall be able to select the content of the overlay information. [SSS196] The TCS shall have the capability to select and deselect several types of cross hairs (or other similar ICON) to identify a selected point on a target. [SSS197] RS170A video and digital imagery shall be routed to TCS functions and displayed upon operator request. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall have the functionality to conduct limited exploitation, to include voice and textual reporting for spot and mission objectives, on the payload product data. [SSS200] Limited exploitation, as a minimum, will include image enhancement and annotation. The image enhancement capability shall include contrast, brightness, edge enhancement, and sharpness. [SSS201] The TCS shall provide the capability to capture frozen-frames of imagery and store these frozen images for further review and processing. [SSS202] The TCS shall have the capability to display Near-Real Time (NRT) imagery to include, as a minimum, date/time group, target location coordinates when the target is in the center of the field of view, north seeking arrow, and AV position and heading. [SSS203] The TCS shall provide the capability to compute the range and bearing between two geographic positions located on the payload imagery display. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall implement an AV Standard Interface that will provide the proper data format to ensure communications with the selected AV. [SSS322] This interface shall allow for addition of future AVs and will provide the generic architecture to ensure interoperability. [SSS323] The uplink and downlink information passed between the TCS and the AV shall be in accordance with the associated AV documentation. [SSS324] The TCS shall support a concurrent uplink and downlink capability. [SSS325] The AV Standard Interface requirements and specifications will be defined in the AV Standard IDD, TCS 229 . The AV Standard Interface is designated as a mission and safety critical interface for the TCS. The TCS shall implement an AV Standard Interface that will provide the proper data format to ensure communications with the selected AV. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide sufficient cues to allow the operator to safely take-off, land and navigate under Instrument Flight Rules. [SSS347] The TCS shall provide adequate capability to allow the operator to operate each UAV within its certified operational flight envelope. [SSS348] Appropriate cautions and warnings shall be provided to the operator if the air vehicle deviates into unsafe flight regime. [SSS349] For mission planning, the TCS shall provide terrain avoidance warning and minimum reception altitude calculations for line of sight flights. [SSS350] During mission execution, the TCS shall provide the operator a cautions and warnings when the UAV system has identified a malfunction. [SSS351] The TCS shall provide the required information to allow the operator to maintain safe separation from other aircraft and a safe altitude in civilian airspace per Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) rules. [SSS352] The TCS shall be designed such that no single hardware failure results in an unsafe command to be transmitted to the air vehicle. [SSS353] The TCS shall be designed such that no single software error results in an unsafe command to be transmitted to the air vehicle. [SSS556] The TCS shall be capable of restoring power in sufficient time to avoid loss of air vehicle control during power outages. [SSS354] The TCS shall monitor the uplink and downlink to each UAV under its control. [SSS355] Upon detection of loss of link, the TCS shall attempt to reestablish communications with the air vehicle. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS design shall provide protection against injury to TCS operators and maintenance personnel. ground_truth_requirements.text: Using risk assessment procedures defined in DoD 5200.28(D), a risk index and the minimum security requirements for TCS shall be determined. [SSS363] The inputs to this procedure are the clearance or authorization of the TCS users and the sensitivities of the data that the TCS processes, stores or transfers. These requirements pertain to the TCS computer hardware and software. The TCS data sensitivities shall be determined by the data sensitivities of the systems with which it interfaces, to including the air vehicles, payloads, and C4I systems. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS software shall provide a high resolution, computer generated, graphical user interface that enables the UAV operator that is trained on one system to control different types of UAVs as well as UAV payloads with minimal additional training. [SSS404] Each control console shall have, at a minimum, the capability to display the following four display windows: (1) display to provide aircraft position, TCS position, flight path, and a waypoint graphics in the foreground which are positioned in relation to a map displayed in the background, (2) display to provide aircraft flight data or payload data in the foreground, and downlinked video in the background. (3)A display to provide graphic presentations of downlinked telemetry data, and (4) display to present the interface menus for workstation software. (3)A display to provide graphic presentations of downlinked telemetry data, and (4) display to present the interface menus for workstation software. [SSS405]

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS data latency shall not be greater than that present in the Predator ground control station or Outrider ground control station, whichever is smaller. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS reliability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system reliability (Mean Time Between Failures MTBF) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS410] The TCS reliability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system reliability (Mean Time Between Failures MTBF) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS410]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS maintainability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system maintainability (Mean Time To Repair MTTR) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS411] The Design features shall be included to: [SSS412] The TCS maintainability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system maintainability (Mean Time To Repair MTTR) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS411] The Design features shall be included to: [SSS412] minimize the number and frequency of required preventive maintenance actions based on performance requirements and lowest life cycle costs; minimize the contribution to degradation of TCS equipment reliability as a consequence of performing either preventive as well as corrective maintenance; enable the performance of all maintenance actions with safety and comparative ease by providing adequate access to all equipment components and minimizing the requirements for special tools and test equipment; minimize the requirement for specially trained maintenance personnel; improve system availability by the effective selection and incorporation of Built In Test Equipment (BITE); allow removal and replacement of replaceable units without soldering and unsoldering.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS equipment shall achieve an availability (Ao), as defined below, equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS413] Ao = (OT + ST)/(OT + ST + TPM + TCM + TALDT) where: The TCS equipment shall achieve an availability (Ao), as defined below, equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS413] Ao = (OT + ST)/(OT + ST + TPM + TCM + TALDT) where: OT denotes Operate Time ST denotes Standby Time TPM denotes Total Preventative Maintenance TCM denotes Total Corrective Maintenance TALDT denotes Total Administrative and Logistic Downtime
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The total, fully useable, addressable, physically present program instruction memory and data storage memory for each processor shall have at least 50% unused memory during the Normal Operations Mode over any 10 second period. [SSS414] The processing speed of each processor shall be such that at least 50% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over all 10 second periods and at least 20% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over one second periods regardless of the system function performed. [SSS415] The I/O channel reserve capability for each processor shall have at least a 50% reserve, addressable and useable, I/O channel capacity over any 10 second period. [SSS416] TCS software flexibility and expandability shall be provided through use of the DII COE and through use of standardized software development practices. [SSS417] The total, fully useable, addressable, physically present program instruction memory and data storage memory for each processor shall have at least 50% unused memory during the Normal Operations Mode over any 10 second period. [SSS414] The processing speed of each processor shall be such that at least 50% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over all 10 second periods and at least 20% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over one second periods regardless of the system function performed. [SSS415] The I/O channel reserve capability for each processor shall have at least a 50% reserve, addressable and useable, I/O channel capacity over any 10 second period. [SSS416] TCS software flexibility and expandability shall be provided through use of the DII COE and through use of standardized software development practices. [SSS417]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Hardware and software shall be selected for use in the TCS with the goal of providing ease of future changes to the TCS elements. [SSS418] The object of portability for the TCS is to select or develop hardware which will readily host emerging software packages and software which will be as independent of host hardware as possible. The selection of processors, interface cards for communication interfaces, disk drives, video, networking equipment, and all other hardware for use in the TCS shall be made according to standards for production of an open architecture. [SSS419] The selection of operating system and programming language for use in the TCS shall be made according to standards for development of an open architecture. [SSS428] The documentation shall contain sufficient technical detail to define the hardware and software design implemented to satisfy the system requirements. ground_truth_requirements.text: During Phase 1, control techniques to minimize electromagnetic interference, emanation, and susceptibility shall be used in the design of TCS equipment. [SSS432] This control will be inherent in the design of the TCS and the electrical and electronic equipment components and assemblies thereof. The susceptibility to coupling and the propagation of Electromagnetic Interference (EMI) will be minimized by component location, cable routing, and judicious use of shielding. There shall be neither unacceptable response nor malfunction of any TCS and associated equipment due to EMI produced by any as well as all of the TCS and equipment associated with the TCS. [SSS434] The TCS shall be compatible with the external electromagnetic environment that is typical of the service specific environment in the TCS will be operated. [SSS435] The specific electromagnetic environment values will be determined during Phase I of the TCS development. The TCS design shall ensure that personnel, fuel, and ordinance are not exposed to electromagnetic radiation as a result of operating the TCS. [SSS436] The specific radiation hazard (RADHAZ) and HERO values will be determined during Phase I of the TCS development. As TCS transitions into Phase 2, electromagnetic radiation safety and operation specifications will be invoked in the LRIP specification. [SSS438] A modular architecture shall be used by the TCS software in order to support future interoperability with multiple types of UAVs and payloads while maintaining consistent displays and user interfaces. [SSS439] Software components satisfying common planning and control functions will allow for vehicle specific components to be integrated in the future. ground_truth_requirements.text: After emplacement at the operational site, the TCS shall be capable of planning and launching a mission within 1 hour of tasking. [SSS441] Required activities include 1) mission planning of a minimum 1 waypoint mission, 2) preparing 2 AVs for flight, 3) data terminal set-up, 4) safety equipment emplaced, 5) and a single AV launched. After emplacement at the operational site, the TCS shall be capable of planning and launching a mission within 1 hour of tasking. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be capable of operating continuously in functional Operation Mode for a minimum of 72 hours. [SSS442] The TCS shall be capable of operating continuously in functional Operation Mode for a minimum of 72 hours. [SSS442]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall have ergonomically designed operator controls and displays for the 5th percentile female to 95th percentile male operator. [SSS443] The controls shall allow the air vehicle and payload operators to perform mission control, mission monitoring, and mission updates and modifications while wearing cold weather clothing and in a Mission Oriented Protective Posture. [SSS444] The TCS shall provide the operator a caution and warning diagnostic when the TCS system has identified a malfunction. [SSS445] The TCS shall have monitor(s) that allow reading of displays under direct sunlight and low light level environments. [SSS446] The TCS HCI shall be a Graphical User Interface (GUI) based on X-windows and Motif. [SSS447] When performing a given task during mission execution, the operator shall be given appropriate warning messages from other concurrently-executing subsystem tasks. [SSS448] TCS Warning messages shall be color coded and flashed based on mission criticality. The color codes and flash frequencies will follow MIL-STD 1472 guidelines. [SSS449] The TCS operator shall be required to enter an acknowledgment prior to disabling the display of critical warning flags for any AV, Payload, ADT, GDT, and TCS faults. [SSS450] A combination of visual and auditory outputs will be provided to alert the TCS operator to situations which may require operator response. Visual alerts to the TCS operator shall be in the form of a displayed message box that has a display priority greater than other existing windows to ensure that it is viewable immediately by the operator. [SSS452] The position of the displayed message window shall be easily adjustable by the operator to ensure that important mission data is not obscured. [SSS453] In addition to displayed alert messages to the TCS operator, auditory alerts to include tones shall also be provided. [SSS454] The volume of these auditory tones shall be adjustable by the operator via keyboard and trackball input to at least 20dB above the speech interference level at the operator’s ear. [SSS455] All TCS warning messages and HCI actions shall be archived for later review. [SSS456] All TCS operator inputs shall be error checked such that any erroneous operator entry will not cause current processing to terminate. [SSS457] The HCI shall prompt the operator for a valid input. [SSS458] Display jitter and flicker shall not be perceptible by the operator. [SSS459] The operational tasks to be performed concurrently by the operator during normal operation will be determined by appropriate task analysis and function allocation. The TCS shall facilitate Human-Computer Interfaces (HCIs) that support operation of all system modes, functions, and capabilities. [SSS461] The Human Computer Interface (HCI) shall be designed and implemented in accordance with the TCS HCI Specification, TCS 108.[SSS462] The HCI shall provide redundancy in all operations, so that the loss of any one HCI input device does not prohibit operation of any TCS function. [SSS463] The TCS shall provide the functionality to display all HCI elements on any available monitor on the TCS workstation. [SSS464] The TCS shall be capable of displaying a window within a window format to include, as a minimum, displaying a video window overlaid on a map screen as well as a map screen overlaid on a video screen. [SSS465] The TCS shall provide full complementary control operations from the keyset as well as the X/Y control device (e.g., trackball, mouse, joystick). [SSS466] The TCS shall provide access to the DII Style Manager so that pointing device characteristics can be modified. [SSS467] The shall provide the functionality to have a maximum delay time of 1 second from operator command to system acknowledgement and response.[SSS 559] The TCS shall provide a capability for porting an off-the-shelf, complex control joystick with multiple toggle and multi-position switches as part of the TCS hardware suite. [SSS468] The TCS shall use graphical representations to convey information, such as system status, C4I links, and AV-GDT links. [SSS469] The TCS shall provide for multi-level information display tailoring by the operator. [SSS470] The TCS shall provide automated TCS system information, control options, and logical and simple operator guidance and support for immediate and adaptive responding to crisis situations. [SSS471] The TCS shall provide maximum automated system software support to system status monitoring and alerting of the TCS operator when a preset system parameter goes under as well as over a set threshold. [SSS472] The TCS shall provide the necessary processing, display, and control capabilities to ensure dynamic situational awareness input to the TCS operator. [SSS473] The TCS shall minimize alphanumeric data display in favor of graphic, pictorial information display [SSS474] The TCS HCI shall provide unambiguous AV and payload control and status feedback indicators to ensure safe, efficient operations of two AVs and their payloads by a single TCS station. [SSS475] The TCS shall provide for a specific icon shape on a constant contrast background, as well as other visual information coding mechanisms, to cue the TCS operator regarding which UAVs are under his or her primary control. [SSS476] The TCS HCI shall provide the capability to select and amplify an object and point on a map as well as payload screen. [SSS477] The TCS HCI shall provide coarse and fine payload control capabilities directly on the payload screen. [SSS478] The TCS HCI shall display the SAR imaging swath on the map display. [SSS479] The TCS HCI shall provide the on-screen capability to select and efficiently move as well as reorient a previously defined SAR imaging swath. [SSS480] The TCS HCI shall provide the capability to lock onto and hold a coordinate point on the payload imagery window. [SSS481] The TCS HCI shall provide the capability to display operator definable “Lock Out” zones around waypoints, Launch and Recovery Point (LRP), as well as any selected point on the AV flight path. [SSS482] The TCS HCI shall provide for a rapid means to cancel aural warnings. [SSS484] The TCS HCI shall provide for separation, grouping, and visual coding of multiple categories of alerts, to include Warnings, Cautions, and Advisories. [SSS485] The TCS HCI shall provide for visual Warnings, Cautions, and Advisories to be displayed at or near the center of the field of view, i.e., within a 30o cone, of all monitors in a TCS system. [SSS486] For AV safety as well as mission-critical Warnings, the TCS HCI shall provide a default selection as well as an override option, along with a selection of adaptive responses, and the minimum information necessary to assist the operator in responding quickly and adaptively to the emergency. [SSS487] The TCS HCI shall provide for on-screen information to include, as a minimum, overlays, headers, cursors, alphanumeric annotation, waypoints, crosshairs, designed to be visible against the complete spectrum of map and payload video backgrounds. [SSS488] The TCS HCI shall provide continuously-available, on-screen control functions for time and missioncritical operations, to include as a minimum print, freeze, declassification, mark VCR, declutter, cease RF transmission. [SSS489] The TCS HCI shall provide for the capability to automatically designate target locations from the payload screen onto the map screen. [SSS490] The TCS shall have ergonomically designed operator controls and displays for the 5th percentile female to 95th percentile male operator.
