[SSS444] The TCS shall provide the operator a caution and warning diagnostic when the TCS system has identified a malfunction. [SSS445] The TCS shall have monitor(s) that allow reading of displays under direct sunlight and low light level environments. ground_truth_requirements.text: Formal training programs shall not be required for TCS Phase 1, Program definition and Risk Reduction. Trained and proficient personnel from the Original Equipment Manufacturers, Government Engineering Teams, supporting Contractors, and Military Personnel will support the operation and maintenance of the demonstration system equipment throughout Phase 1. TCS training and training support shall include the processes, procedures, techniques, training devices and equipment to train civilian, active duty and reserve military personnel to operate and support the TCS system. [SSS491] This will include: individual and crew training; new equipment training; initial, formal, and on-the-job training. TCS training will strike a balance between institutional, new equipment and unit training. The TCS system shall provide, for the operator and maintainer, an embedded or add-on interactive training courseware with self-paced instruction, duplicating UAV flight performance characteristics, capabilities, and limitations. [SSS492] The OUTRIDER TCS system shall be compatible with the U.S. Army Intelligence and Electronic Warfare Tactical Proficiency Trainer as an objective. [SSS493] The interactive courseware training capability for TCS shall be developed during Phase I and introduced to the user during scheduled demonstrations and tests. [SSS494] The training capability for performance of TCS functions shall include primary mission (flight route/payload) planning, mission control and monitoring, imagery processing, tactical communications, AV control communications and TCS system on line diagnostics. [SSS495] This capability will be qualified and improved during Phase I based upon use and feedback from qualified operators and users demonstrating TCS system equipment throughout Phase I. Instructional support materials and training courseware for classroom discussion and lecture will be developed to support institutional, new equipment training and unit training. The TCS shall provide the functional capability to train in the operation of the TCS system, performance of TCS UAV functions, and on line system troubleshooting. [SSS496] TCS system training shall include system architecture, component familiarization, and system startup, initialization, system recovery and shutdown. [SSS497] The TCS system shall not support formal Training operations concurrent with the execution of an actual mission. [SSS499] The capability for the conduct of actual communications processing concurrently with Training operations shall be provided if and only if messages are identified as training messages. [SSS500] Training shall be adequate to maintain operator and maintainer skills and proficiencies. [SSS501] TCS shall record operator and maintainer actions for self assessment and performance enhancement. [SSS502] Operator and maintainer performance shall be measurable using parameters retrievable from the TCS to determine proficiency levels. [SSS503] Formal training programs shall not be required for TCS Phase 1, Program definition and Risk Reduction. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be ground transportable. [SSS521] The TCS shall be ground transportable. [SSS521]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be air transportable by helicopter (CH-47/CH-53D) and C-130 drive-on/drive-off capable. [SSS522] The TCS shall be air transportable by helicopter (CH-47/CH-53D) and C-130 drive-on/drive-off capable. [SSS522]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be sea transportable. [SSS523] The TCS shall be sea transportable. [SSS523]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be rail transportable. [SSS524] The TCS shall be rail transportable. [SSS524]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be configurable for sea, ground, as well as air transport in 2 hours or less. [SSS525] The TCS system shall be capable of being de-configured from sea, ground, as well as air transport and ground-mobile in 2 hours or less. [SSS526] The TCS shall be configurable for sea, ground, as well as air transport in 2 hours or less. [SSS525] The TCS system shall be capable of being de-configured from sea, ground, as well as air transport and ground-mobile in 2 hours or less. [SSS526]

[SSS533] The TCS shall have an objective capability to be integrated and operated from ships. ground_truth_requirements.text: All requirements in this specification are of equal weight and criticality unless otherwise identified in the traceability Table in Appendix A. When the requirements of the Operational Requirement Document (ORD), this System/Subsystem Specification (SSS), other related requirement documents, and referenced documents are in conflict, the following precedence will apply: All requirements in this specification are of equal weight and criticality unless otherwise identified in the traceability Table in Appendix A. When the requirements of the Operational Requirement Document (ORD), this System/Subsystem Specification (SSS), other related requirement documents, and referenced documents are in conflict, the following precedence will apply: In the event of conflicting requirements within a TCS requirement document, the requirements shall be traced to the next higher precedence requirement document for clarification. If further resolution is required, the developer will notify the procuring agency. TCS ORD – The ORD shall have precedence over any other TCS documentation. TCS SSS – This SSS shall have precedence over other requirements documents except item (1.) above. Other TCS requirement documents – Other requirement documents shall have precedence over any referenced documents. Referenced documents – Documents referenced herein and in other TCS requirement documents shall have precedence over all applicable subsidiary documents referenced therein.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Quality Assurance (QA) Program is an integral part of the development process for the TCS , whereby all phases of the development of a system must be inspected and tested, as these phases occur. The objectives and processes of the QA Program applicable to this development effort are contained in the TCS Integration Program Quality Assurance Plan, NSWCDD/TR-96/XXX. The Quality Assurance (QA) Program is an integral part of the development process for the TCS , whereby all phases of the development of a system must be inspected and tested, as these phases occur. The objectives and processes of the QA Program applicable to this development effort are contained in the TCS Integration Program Quality Assurance Plan, NSWCDD/TR-96/XXX.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a graphical game board that represents the current puzzle state and updates in real time as the player interacts with it.
generated_requirements.rationale: The game board is the primary interface through which players interact with the puzzle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a menu bar containing at minimum the following top-level menus: Game, Edit, and Help.
generated_requirements.rationale: A menu bar is explicitly required and must expose core game controls in an organised structure.

generated_requirements.text: The Game menu shall include options to start a new game, view the leaderboard, and exit the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: These are the fundamental game-lifecycle actions a player must be able to trigger from the menu.

generated_requirements.text: The Edit menu shall include Undo and Redo actions, each with associated keyboard shortcuts (e.g., Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+Y respectively).
generated_requirements.rationale: Undo and Redo are core gameplay controls that must be accessible both via menu and keyboard for usability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an undo history stack that stores up to the last 1,000 player moves for the current game session.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly specifies a maximum undo/redo history of 1,000 moves.

generated_requirements.text: When the undo history reaches 1,000 entries, the system shall discard the oldest move to accommodate each new move, maintaining the 1,000-entry cap.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the edge-case behaviour when the history buffer is full, preventing unbounded memory growth.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the player to undo moves one step at a time, reverting the game board to the state it was in before the most recent undone action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Undo must correctly restore prior board states to give players full control over their gameplay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the player to redo previously undone moves one step at a time, re-applying the action and restoring the board to the state after that action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Redo complements undo and must correctly re-apply actions to support free gameplay exploration.

generated_requirements.text: When a new move is made after one or more undos, the system shall discard all redo history beyond the current position in the history stack.
generated_requirements.rationale: This is standard undo/redo branching behaviour that prevents inconsistent game state.

generated_requirements.text: The Undo menu action shall be disabled (greyed out) when there are no moves available to undo.
generated_requirements.rationale: Disabling unavailable actions prevents user confusion and communicates system state clearly.

generated_requirements.text: The Redo menu action shall be disabled (greyed out) when there are no moves available to redo.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consistent with R010, this prevents the player from attempting an invalid redo operation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall start a countdown or elapsed-time timer at the moment the player makes their first move in a new game, and continue tracking elapsed time until the puzzle is solved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Elapsed time is a component of the score and must be tracked accurately from the start of active play.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the current elapsed time in a visible area of the game window, updating at least once per second.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time display of elapsed time gives players feedback and creates competitive awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a running count of the total number of actions (moves) taken by the player during the current game session, incrementing by one for each valid move made.
generated_requirements.rationale: Action count is a score component and must be tracked accurately throughout the session.

generated_requirements.text: The action count shall not be decremented when the player uses Undo, and shall not be incremented when the player uses Redo; only forward moves shall increment the counter.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clarifies the scoring semantics of undo/redo to ensure consistent and fair score calculation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the current action count in a visible area of the game window, updating immediately after each move.
generated_requirements.rationale: Players need real-time feedback on their action count to make informed strategic decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect when the player has successfully solved the puzzle and transition to a game-completion state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic completion detection is required to stop the timer and trigger score recording.

generated_requirements.text: Upon game completion, the system shall stop the elapsed-time timer and record the final elapsed time and total action count as the player's score components.
generated_requirements.rationale: The score must be frozen at the exact moment of completion to ensure accuracy and fairness.

generated_requirements.text: Upon game completion, the system shall prompt the player to enter their name before recording the score to the leaderboard.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scores are recorded under a player's name, so the system must collect this information at completion.

generated_requirements.text: The player name entry field shall accept a non-empty string of at least 1 and at most 50 characters; the system shall reject empty submissions and prompt the player to re-enter.
generated_requirements.rationale: Input validation ensures leaderboard entries are meaningful and prevents blank-name records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall calculate a player's score by combining their elapsed time (in seconds) and total action count using a defined formula, such as Score = elapsed_seconds + action_count, documented in the system specification.
generated_requirements.rationale: A documented, deterministic score formula is required for consistent and comparable leaderboard rankings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store each completed game's score record — comprising player name, elapsed time, action count, and computed score — persistently so that leaderboard data survives application restarts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Persistent storage is necessary for the leaderboard to be meaningful across multiple play sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated leaderboard window accessible from the Game menu, displaying all recorded scores.
generated_requirements.rationale: The leaderboard window is explicitly required as a place where all players' scores can be viewed and compared.

generated_requirements.text: The leaderboard shall display each entry with at minimum: rank, player name, elapsed time, action count, and computed score.
generated_requirements.rationale: Displaying all score components allows players to understand how rankings are determined.

generated_requirements.text: The leaderboard shall be sorted in ascending order of computed score (lower score is better), with ties broken by ascending elapsed time, then ascending action count, then alphabetically by player name.
generated_requirements.rationale: A fully defined tie-breaking rule ensures a deterministic and fair ordering for all leaderboard entries.

generated_requirements.text: The leaderboard shall support an unlimited number of entries, or if a maximum is enforced, the system shall retain at least the top 100 scores and document the cap clearly.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defining leaderboard capacity prevents silent data loss and sets clear expectations for players.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall run on Windows (10 and later), macOS (11 and later), and Linux (Ubuntu 20.04 LTS and later) without requiring platform-specific code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-platform compatibility is a primary design goal, and Qt is chosen specifically to enable this.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall be built using the Qt framework (version 5.15 LTS or Qt 6.x) to ensure consistent rendering and behaviour across all supported operating systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mandating the Qt version ensures reproducible builds and avoids API incompatibilities across platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall launch and display the main game window within 3 seconds on a machine meeting the minimum hardware specification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast startup time is essential for a positive first impression and smooth user experience.

generated_requirements.text: All player interactions with the game board (moves, undo, redo) shall be processed and the UI updated within 100 milliseconds of the triggering input event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sub-100 ms response time ensures the game feels responsive and does not disrupt the gameplay flow.

generated_requirements.text: The elapsed-time display shall be accurate to within ±1 second over a continuous 60-minute game session.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timer accuracy directly affects score fairness; drift must be bounded to an acceptable tolerance.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall not crash or produce undefined behaviour when the undo/redo history reaches its 1,000-move capacity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Boundary conditions in the history buffer are a common source of defects and must be handled gracefully.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall handle unexpected termination (e.g., OS kill signal) without corrupting the persistent leaderboard data file.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data integrity of the leaderboard must be preserved even under abnormal shutdown conditions.

generated_requirements.text: Leaderboard data shall be written using an atomic write strategy (e.g., write to a temporary file then rename) to prevent partial writes from corrupting the data store.
generated_requirements.rationale: Atomic writes are the standard technique for ensuring data integrity during persistence operations.

generated_requirements.text: The leaderboard data shall be stored in a human-readable, structured format (e.g., JSON or XML) to facilitate inspection, backup, and manual recovery.
generated_requirements.rationale: A readable format reduces the effort required to diagnose data issues and perform manual corrections.

generated_requirements.text: The source code shall follow a documented coding standard (e.g., Qt Coding Conventions) and be organised into logical modules separating game logic, UI, history management, scoring, and persistence.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular, standards-compliant code reduces the cost of future changes and onboarding of new developers.

generated_requirements.text: The score calculation formula shall be implemented in a single, isolated function or class so that changes to the formula require modification in only one location.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralising the formula prevents inconsistencies and simplifies future scoring model changes.

generated_requirements.text: All user-facing strings (labels, menu items, dialog text) shall be externalised using Qt's internationalisation (tr()) mechanism to support future localisation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalising strings from the outset avoids costly refactoring when adding language support later.

generated_requirements.text: The game's graphical interface shall follow platform-native UI conventions on each supported OS (e.g., menu placement on macOS) to meet user expectations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adhering to platform conventions reduces the learning curve and improves player comfort.

generated_requirements.text: The game window shall be resizable, and all UI elements — including the game board, timer, and action counter — shall scale proportionally without loss of legibility.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive layout ensures the game is playable across a range of screen sizes and resolutions.

generated_requirements.text: The leaderboard window shall be scrollable when the number of entries exceeds the visible area, and shall support sorting by any displayed column via column header clicks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scrollability and interactive sorting make the leaderboard usable as the number of entries grows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a confirmation dialog before starting a new game if a game is currently in progress, warning the player that unsaved progress will be lost.
generated_requirements.rationale: Preventing accidental game abandonment protects the player from unintentional loss of progress.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall consume no more than 150 MB of RAM during normal gameplay, including a full 1,000-move undo/redo history.
generated_requirements.rationale: Bounding memory usage ensures the game runs acceptably on modest hardware configurations.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall have an availability of 99% during an active user session, meaning it shall not require a restart or encounter a fatal error more than once per 100 hours of cumulative play.
generated_requirements.rationale: High in-session availability is critical for player trust and a quality gaming experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the integrity of the leaderboard data file at startup and, if corruption is detected, notify the user and offer to reset the leaderboard rather than crashing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful handling of corrupted data prevents application failure and gives the user a recovery path.

generated_requirements.text: The leaderboard data file shall be stored in the OS-appropriate user data directory (e.g., %APPDATA% on Windows, ~/.local/share on Linux, ~/Library/Application Support on macOS).
generated_requirements.rationale: Using OS-standard data directories ensures correct permissions and compatibility with platform conventions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a unit-test suite covering at minimum: undo/redo history management, score calculation, game completion detection, and leaderboard sorting logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated tests for critical subsystems reduce regression risk during future development.

generated_requirements.text: The elapsed time and action count displays shall remain visible and legible at all times during an active game session and shall not be obscured by other UI elements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Constant visibility of score components allows players to monitor their performance without interruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support high-DPI displays on all platforms, rendering all graphical assets and text at the appropriate resolution without blurriness or clipping.
generated_requirements.rationale: High-DPI support is essential for a professional appearance on modern displays across all target platforms.

generated_requirements.text: When a new game is started, the system shall reset the elapsed-time timer, action count, and undo/redo history to their initial states before presenting the new puzzle board.
generated_requirements.rationale: A clean reset ensures that state from a previous game does not contaminate the new session's score or history.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall assign a globally unique, immutable event identifier to each outbreak investigation upon creation, ensuring no two events share the same identifier across all jurisdictional levels.
generated_requirements.rationale: Unique event identifiers are foundational for traceability and cross-jurisdictional coordination throughout the investigation lifecycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall assign a globally unique subject identifier to each entity (person, animal, organization, location, conveyance, object, or other living thing) enrolled in an investigation, and this identifier shall persist across all jurisdictional transfers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Persistent subject identifiers prevent duplicate records and enable accurate entity tracking across jurisdictional boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the creation, editing, and deactivation of entity records for all supported entity types: persons, animals, organizations, locations, conveyances, objects, and other living things.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive entity management is required to capture the full scope of subjects involved in a disease outbreak investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow investigators to define and record typed relationships (e.g., contact, exposure, co-location, ownership) between any two entities within or across investigations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Relationship tracing between entities is essential for identifying transmission chains and exposure pathways in outbreak investigations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture and store structured demographic data for person entities, including but not limited to name, date of birth, sex, gender identity, race, ethnicity, address, and contact information.
generated_requirements.rationale: Demographic data is required for epidemiological analysis, case matching, and public health reporting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture structured epidemiological data for each case subject, including symptom onset date, clinical status, hospitalization status, outcome, and exposure history.
generated_requirements.rationale: Epidemiological data drives case classification, outbreak characterization, and public health response decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the full specimen management lifecycle, including specimen collection recording, labeling, tracking, submission to laboratories, and receipt confirmation.
generated_requirements.rationale: End-to-end specimen tracking ensures laboratory workflows are coordinated and no samples are lost or misattributed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and maintain a chain-of-custody log for each specimen, capturing every transfer event with the transferring party, receiving party, date/time, and custody status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Chain-of-custody documentation is required for forensic integrity and legal admissibility of laboratory evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record laboratory test orders and results for each specimen, including test type, performing laboratory, result value, result units, reference range, and result interpretation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Structured laboratory result capture enables automated case classification and aggregated outbreak analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the creation and management of event-specific, configurable investigation questionnaires, allowing jurisdictions to define custom sections, questions, answer types (free text, coded, numeric, date, multi-select), and conditional display logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable questionnaires allow jurisdictions to tailor data collection to the unique epidemiological characteristics of each health event.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized jurisdiction administrators to create, modify, publish, version, and retire investigation questionnaire templates without requiring a software deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Runtime questionnaire management enables rapid response to emerging outbreaks without dependency on software release cycles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized administrators to define custom data fields, field types, validation rules, and permissible value sets for each entity type on a per-health-event basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Per-event field customization supports the diverse data requirements of different disease investigations within the same platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support updating the case diagnosis for a subject within an investigation, recording the previous diagnosis, the new diagnosis, the date of change, and the user who made the change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Diagnosis updates must be auditable to maintain the integrity of case classification history throughout an investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall aggregate investigation results (case counts, laboratory findings, exposure summaries) across all subjects linked to a single outbreak event and present them in a consolidated outbreak summary view.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-subject aggregation is essential for epidemiologists to characterize the scope and progression of an outbreak.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support linking a single subject record to multiple outbreak investigations, preserving the subject's data independently within each investigation context.
generated_requirements.rationale: A subject may be involved in multiple concurrent or sequential outbreaks, requiring independent data capture per investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC), defining distinct roles (e.g., investigator, epidemiologist, laboratory staff, jurisdiction administrator, read-only reviewer, federal coordinator) with granular permissions on data creation, viewing, editing, and deletion.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC ensures that users access only the data and functions appropriate to their professional role and jurisdictional authority.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce jurisdictional data access boundaries, ensuring that local jurisdiction users can only access records within their jurisdiction unless explicitly granted cross-jurisdictional access by a higher-level administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Jurisdictional data segregation protects sensitive public health information and complies with data-sharing agreements between governmental entities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via multi-factor authentication (MFA) and support integration with federated identity providers (e.g., SAML 2.0, OpenID Connect) used by government agencies.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA and federated identity integration reduce unauthorized access risk and align with federal government identity assurance requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, tamper-evident audit log of all create, read, update, and delete operations on sensitive data, recording the user identity, timestamp, affected record identifier, and before/after field values.
generated_requirements.rationale: A comprehensive audit trail supports forensic investigation, regulatory compliance, and accountability for all data changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all personally identifiable information (PII) and protected health information (PHI) at rest using AES-256 or equivalent, and in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of PII/PHI at rest and in transit is required to comply with HIPAA, federal data protection standards, and state privacy laws.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support inter-jurisdictional data sharing by allowing authorized users to transfer or share investigation records (or subsets thereof) between local, state, and federal jurisdiction instances, with configurable sharing rules and consent tracking.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-level jurisdictional coordination requires controlled data sharing to enable federal and state oversight of local outbreak investigations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide RESTful APIs conforming to HL7 FHIR R4 standards for exchanging case investigation data with external public health systems, laboratory information management systems (LIMS), and electronic health record (EHR) systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: FHIR-based interoperability enables seamless data exchange with the broader public health and clinical information ecosystem.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support import and export of investigation data in standard formats including HL7 v2.x, HL7 FHIR R4, CSV, and XML to facilitate integration with legacy public health systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standard format support ensures compatibility with the heterogeneous technology landscape of public health agencies at all jurisdictional levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to all interactive user interface requests (page loads, form submissions, search queries) within 3 seconds under normal load conditions (up to 500 concurrent users).
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive performance is critical during active outbreak investigations where investigators must act quickly on incoming data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 2,000 concurrent users across all jurisdictional levels without degradation of response time beyond 5 seconds for any interactive operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Large-scale outbreaks may require simultaneous access by investigators across hundreds of jurisdictions, demanding high concurrency capacity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% uptime on a monthly basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 72 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is essential because disease outbreak investigations are time-critical and may occur at any hour.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement automated failover to a geographically redundant standby environment with a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 1 hour and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 15 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid recovery from infrastructure failures ensures continuity of outbreak investigations with minimal data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configurable, role-sensitive dashboard that displays active investigations, pending tasks, recent alerts, and key epidemiological indicators relevant to the logged-in user's jurisdiction and role.
generated_requirements.rationale: A personalized dashboard reduces cognitive load and enables investigators to quickly orient to their most urgent responsibilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a global search capability allowing users to search across all entity types, investigations, and questionnaire responses using free-text and structured filter criteria, returning results within 2 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast, comprehensive search is critical for investigators to quickly locate records and identify potential links between cases.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support duplicate detection for person entities by comparing incoming records against existing records using configurable probabilistic matching algorithms (e.g., on name, date of birth, address), flagging potential duplicates for investigator review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Duplicate person records undermine case counts and epidemiological analysis, making automated detection essential for data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a visual, interactive contact and exposure network graph that displays entities as nodes and relationships as edges, supporting filtering by relationship type, date range, and entity type.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visual network graphs enable epidemiologists to rapidly identify transmission clusters and high-risk exposure nodes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce configurable data validation rules on all structured data entry fields, preventing submission of records that violate defined constraints (e.g., date ranges, required fields, coded value sets) and displaying actionable error messages.
generated_requirements.rationale: Client- and server-side validation ensures data quality at the point of entry, reducing downstream analytical errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support configurable automated notifications (email, SMS, in-app) to assigned investigators and supervisors when key investigation milestones occur, such as new case assignment, laboratory result receipt, or case status change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated notifications reduce response latency and ensure that critical investigation events are not missed by responsible parties.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configurable reporting module that allows authorized users to generate, schedule, and export standard and ad hoc epidemiological reports (e.g., case counts by jurisdiction, attack rates, epi curves) in PDF, Excel, and CSV formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Flexible reporting supports situational awareness, regulatory reporting obligations, and public health decision-making at all jurisdictional levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support versioning of all configurable elements (questionnaire templates, custom field definitions, value sets, relationship type definitions), retaining prior versions and associating each investigation record with the template version active at the time of data collection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Template versioning ensures that historical investigation data remains interpretable even after configuration changes are made.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a user interface that conforms to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards, ensuring usability for investigators with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance is required by federal law (Section 508) and ensures equitable access for all public health workforce members.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support localization and internationalization, including configurable date/time formats, number formats, and the ability to display the user interface in multiple languages as defined by jurisdiction administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-language and locale support accommodates diverse public health workforces and enables deployment across jurisdictions with different language requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline help, field-level tooltips, and a searchable knowledge base accessible from within the application to guide users through investigation workflows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Embedded guidance reduces training burden and supports correct data entry, especially for infrequently used features during large-scale events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on major cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and on-premises infrastructure, with all environment-specific configurations externalized from application code.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deployment flexibility accommodates the diverse infrastructure environments of local, state, and federal public health agencies.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a modular, service-oriented architecture with documented APIs between components, enabling individual modules (e.g., laboratory management, questionnaire engine, reporting) to be updated or replaced independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future enhancements and allows jurisdictions to adopt new capabilities incrementally.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain automated unit, integration, and end-to-end test suites with a minimum of 80% code coverage, and all tests must pass before any production deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive automated testing reduces regression risk and supports confident, frequent releases of new functionality.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support horizontal scaling of application and database tiers to accommodate sudden surges in usage during large-scale outbreak events, with auto-scaling policies triggered by CPU and request-rate thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-scaling ensures the system remains performant during unpredictable demand spikes characteristic of major public health emergencies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized administrators to define and manage user accounts, assign roles, set jurisdictional scope, and enforce password policies (minimum length, complexity, expiration, and history) through an administrative console.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralized user and policy management is required to maintain security governance across a large, distributed public health workforce.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically lock a user account after five consecutive failed authentication attempts and require administrator or self-service unlock via a verified secondary channel.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout policies mitigate brute-force credential attacks against sensitive public health data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configurable case classification engine that automatically assigns or suggests a case classification (e.g., confirmed, probable, suspect, not a case) based on defined criteria combining laboratory results, clinical data, and epidemiological linkage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated case classification reduces investigator workload and improves consistency of case definitions across jurisdictions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the association of geographic coordinates (latitude/longitude) with location entities and provide a map-based visualization of case and exposure locations within an investigation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Geospatial visualization enables investigators to identify geographic clustering of cases and potential environmental exposure sources.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all investigation data, audit logs, and associated documents for a minimum of 10 years in accordance with public health records retention requirements, with archived data remaining queryable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term data retention supports retrospective epidemiological research, legal proceedings, and regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated daily backups of all investigation data and configuration, store backups in a geographically separate location, and support point-in-time restoration to any backup within the last 30 days.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular, geographically distributed backups protect against data loss from infrastructure failures, ransomware, or accidental deletion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configurable task and workflow management module that allows supervisors to assign investigation tasks to specific users, set due dates, track completion status, and escalate overdue tasks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Structured task management ensures accountability and timely completion of investigation activities across distributed teams.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo and pass an independent security penetration test at least annually and upon any major release, with all critical and high findings remediated before deployment to production.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular penetration testing identifies and remediates vulnerabilities before they can be exploited, protecting sensitive public health data.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data about organizations (e.g., a local health department, a university, a professional association) involved in an OM investigation, including: organization name, location, and contact information. ground_truth_requirements.text: Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data about locations involved in an OM investigation, including: name (if applicable), type (e.g., floor, building, room, store), street address, city, state, zip code, country, GPS coordinates, and other specific details (e.g., a specific building on a campus, a business branch location, a local chapter’s meeting hall) Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data about locations involved in an OM investigation, including: name (if applicable), type (e.g., floor, building, room, store), street address, city, state, zip code, country, GPS coordinates, and other specific details (e.g., a specific building on a campus, a business branch location, a local chapter’s meeting hall)

ground_truth_requirements.text: Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data about any animals involved in an OM investigation, including: type (dog, monkey, etc), age, gender, owner’s name and address, color, weight, and species. A Subject ID should also be collected for animals in an OM investigation. It may be a challenge to ensure unambiguous identification because demographic details of an animal are not easily identified; therefore, animals involved in investigations may need to be tagged. Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data about any animals involved in an OM investigation, including: type (dog, monkey, etc), age, gender, owner’s name and address, color, weight, and species. A Subject ID should also be collected for animals in an OM investigation. It may be a challenge to ensure unambiguous identification because demographic details of an animal are not easily identified; therefore, animals involved in investigations may need to be tagged.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data for any object involved in an OM investigation, such as a letter, invoice, food item, or any object that cannot be classified as a “person, organization, place, or animal.” Collected data may include: name of the object, type, physical descriptors, address, identification number (e.g. serial number, package slip number), and relevant dates and times (e.g., invoice date, shipping date, packaging date). ground_truth_requirements.text: Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data about any conveyance involved in an OM investigation, including: type of conveyance, route taken (e.g., flight number), etc. Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data about any conveyance involved in an OM investigation, including: type of conveyance, route taken (e.g., flight number), etc.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data about any public or private gathering of people (e.g., church social, ball game) involved in an OM investigation, including: time, location, nature of the event, etc. Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture data about any public or private gathering of people (e.g., church social, ball game) involved in an OM investigation, including: time, location, nature of the event, etc.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Systems supporting OM must have the capability to capture an entity’s travel history to support investigations of entities infected, exposed or potentially exposed. ground_truth_requirements.text: When a health event is investigated, it must be assigned an event identifier (i.e., Event ID) that is unique within the jurisdiction. When a health event is investigated, it must be assigned an event identifier (i.e., Event ID) that is unique within the jurisdiction.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Detailed conveyance data must be collected when relevant to the investigation, including the carrier identifier, the type of conveyance (such as airplane, bus, or train, among countless others), as well as the make, model, year, and identification number (e.g., VIN) of each vehicle with which the entity was in contact (if this information is relevant to the investigation). ground_truth_requirements.text: Because attributes of both case and exposure data may describe the same entity, systems supporting OM must have the ability to avoid capturing redundant entity demographic information. ground_truth_requirements.text: Epidemiological (epi) data must be collected to assist in the case investigation of events. ground_truth_requirements.text: In the context of a case, all entities exposed to a case must be recorded and linked to the case. In the context of a case, all entities exposed to a case must be recorded and linked to the case.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Both the jurisdiction investigating the event and the jurisdiction reporting the cases and associated investigations must be captured. For example, if a person becomes ill during travel in one jurisdiction but is the resident of another, the illness will be reported by the state (jurisdiction) of residence and investigated by the jurisdiction visited. ground_truth_requirements.text: Detailed data must be collected about the source of exposure as well as the exposed entity to support contact exposure tracing. Exposure data related to both the potential source and the potential spread include the entity’s type, Subject ID, Contact ID, contact’s name and address, exposure dates and times, health status, and priority code. ground_truth_requirements.text: Specimens/samples collected for laboratory testing must be assigned an identifier (i.e., Specimen ID) that is unique within the jurisdiction. generated_requirements.text: The system shall support emergency calls initiated by train drivers, controllers, and shunting team members, with immediate preemption of all lower-priority calls.
generated_requirements.rationale: Emergency calls are safety-critical and must override all other communication to protect life and railway operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support group calls that allow a single caller to simultaneously communicate with all members of a predefined operational group.
generated_requirements.rationale: Group calls are essential for coordinating shunting teams and operational staff across a shared work area.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support broadcast calls that allow authorized controllers to transmit one-way voice messages to all subscribers within a defined geographic or functional zone.
generated_requirements.rationale: Broadcast calls enable rapid dissemination of operational instructions or safety warnings to large groups simultaneously.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support point-to-point voice calls between any two authorized subscribers, including train drivers, controllers, and fixed-network users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Point-to-point calls are the fundamental communication mode for direct operational coordination between individuals.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a call priority hierarchy with at least the following levels in descending order: emergency calls, railway emergency calls, group emergency calls, high-priority operational calls, and standard calls.
generated_requirements.rationale: A well-defined priority hierarchy ensures that safety-critical communications are never blocked by routine traffic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall preempt and terminate an active lower-priority call when a higher-priority call request is received and no additional radio resources are available.
generated_requirements.rationale: Resource preemption guarantees that high-priority and emergency calls can always be established under congested network conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support functional number-based addressing, allowing subscribers to be called by their operational role (e.g., train driver on a specific train) rather than by a physical device identifier.
generated_requirements.rationale: Functional addressing decouples identity from hardware, enabling role-based communication regardless of which physical device is in use.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow mobile equipment to register with the GSM-R network using a functional identity (Functional Number, FN) in addition to the hardware MSISDN.
generated_requirements.rationale: Functional identity registration is the foundation of role-based addressing and interoperable operation across network boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support re-registration of mobile equipment with a new functional identity when a train driver or operational staff member changes role or train assignment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Re-registration ensures that functional addressing remains accurate as operational assignments change during a shift.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automatic handover of active calls and registered functional identities as mobile equipment moves between GSM-R cells within a national network, without call interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless intra-network handover is required to maintain continuous communication as trains travel at operational speeds.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support cross-border handover of mobile equipment registrations and active calls when a train crosses from one national GSM-R network to another, preserving functional identity associations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-border handover is essential for uninterrupted communication on international railway corridors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support interoperability between national GSM-R networks by conforming to the EIRENE functional and interface specifications, enabling calls to be routed across network boundaries.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standardized interoperability is the core mandate of EIRENE to enable pan-European railway communication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized subscribers from public fixed and mobile telephone networks to initiate and receive calls to and from on-train personnel via defined gateway interfaces.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration with public networks enables coordination between railway staff and external stakeholders such as emergency services.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access from public fixed and mobile networks to only those subscribers who have been explicitly authorized by the railway network operator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Access control prevents unauthorized parties from disrupting safety-critical railway communications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide cab radio equipment for installation in train driving cabs, supporting all EIRENE call types and functional identity registration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cab radios are the primary communication device for train drivers and must support the full EIRENE feature set.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide handheld operational radios for use by shunting teams and trackside staff, supporting voice calls and functional identity registration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Handheld radios enable mobile operational staff to participate in the EIRENE communication framework away from fixed infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support data communication services over the GSM-R network, including transmission of train control and operational data messages.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data services extend EIRENE beyond voice to support automated train control and operational management systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the train driver with a dedicated, single-action emergency call button on the cab radio that initiates an emergency call without requiring additional steps.
generated_requirements.rationale: A one-action emergency trigger minimizes response time in life-threatening situations where cognitive load is high.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically include the calling train's functional identity and location information in all emergency call setups.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic identity and location reporting enables controllers to respond immediately without requiring the driver to verbally identify themselves.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the assignment of multiple functional numbers to a single physical mobile equipment unit to represent different operational roles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-role assignment on a single device supports flexible operational configurations without requiring additional hardware.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all call events, including call type, initiating functional identity, target identity, start time, end time, and priority level, in a tamper-evident audit record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive call logging supports post-incident investigation and regulatory compliance for safety-critical railway operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the initiating subscriber with an audible and visual indication when a call setup attempt fails or is rejected due to network unavailability or access denial.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clear failure feedback prevents operators from assuming a call was connected when it was not, avoiding dangerous miscommunication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall establish an emergency call within 2 seconds of the emergency call button being activated under normal network load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A 2-second setup time for emergency calls is a recognized safety benchmark ensuring rapid response in critical incidents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall establish a standard point-to-point voice call within 5 seconds of call initiation under normal network load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast call setup for routine calls maintains operational efficiency and reduces delays in time-sensitive coordination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve end-to-end voice call audio quality with a Mean Opinion Score (MOS) of no less than 3.5 under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adequate voice quality is essential to ensure that safety-critical verbal instructions are clearly understood by all parties.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a call drop rate of no greater than 0.5% for active voice calls during normal operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: A low call drop rate is critical to ensuring continuous communication during train operations where interruptions could be hazardous.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a network availability of no less than 99.999% (five nines) for the core GSM-R infrastructure supporting safety-critical voice services.
generated_requirements.rationale: Five-nines availability ensures that the communication backbone is virtually always operational for safety-critical railway use.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete intra-network cell handover without interrupting an active voice call, with a handover execution time not exceeding 150 milliseconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sub-150ms handover prevents perceptible audio gaps that could cause miscommunication during high-speed train movement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all mobile equipment and subscriber identities before granting access to any network service, using GSM-R standard authentication mechanisms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication prevents unauthorized devices from accessing the railway communication network and disrupting operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all voice and data transmissions over the radio interface using the GSM-R standard A5 encryption algorithm or stronger.
generated_requirements.rationale: Over-the-air encryption protects sensitive operational communications from interception by unauthorized parties.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall protect functional identity registration data from unauthorized modification, ensuring that only authenticated and authorized users can register or deregister a functional number.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrity of functional identity data is critical to ensure that calls reach the correct operational role and not a spoofed identity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) requirements specified in EN 50121 for railway applications to prevent interference with signalling and control systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: EMC compliance ensures that GSM-R radio equipment does not interfere with safety-critical railway signalling infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: All mobile and fixed EIRENE equipment shall operate correctly across an ambient temperature range of -25°C to +70°C for trackside installations and -25°C to +55°C for in-cab equipment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Railway equipment is exposed to extreme temperatures across European climates and must remain operational throughout.

generated_requirements.text: All EIRENE mobile equipment shall meet the ingress protection rating of at least IP54 to withstand dust and water exposure in railway operating environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: IP54 protection ensures equipment reliability in outdoor and trackside environments subject to weather and contamination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to the EIRENE System Requirements Specification (SRS) and MORANE functional specifications to ensure interoperability across all participating national networks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adherence to published EIRENE/MORANE standards is the contractual and technical basis for cross-border interoperability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support roaming of GSM-R subscribers across all participating national networks without requiring manual reconfiguration of the mobile equipment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent roaming eliminates operational burden on train crews when crossing national boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a human-machine interface (HMI) on cab radios that presents call status, functional identity, and network registration state in a clear and unambiguous manner readable by the train driver without distraction from driving duties.
generated_requirements.rationale: A clear HMI reduces cognitive load on train drivers, minimizing the risk of operational errors during communication.

generated_requirements.text: The cab radio HMI shall require no more than three user actions to initiate any non-emergency call type from an idle state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Limiting interaction steps reduces distraction and speeds up call setup in time-sensitive operational scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide redundant core network nodes such that the failure of any single network element does not result in loss of service for any active call or registered subscriber.
generated_requirements.rationale: Single-point-of-failure elimination is mandatory for a safety-critical communication system with five-nines availability targets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from a core network node failure and restore full service within 30 seconds through automatic failover to a redundant node.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid automatic failover minimizes service disruption and maintains safety-critical communication continuity.

generated_requirements.text: The system software and configuration shall be structured in modular components to allow updates to individual functional areas (e.g., call routing, identity management) without requiring full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the risk and cost of applying updates and patches to the live railway communication system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide network management interfaces that allow operators to monitor call volumes, network load, equipment status, and alarm conditions in real time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time network management visibility enables operators to detect and respond to degraded conditions before they affect safety.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate automated alarms to network operations staff when any network element's availability falls below defined thresholds or when call failure rates exceed configured limits.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive alerting enables rapid intervention to restore service before safety-critical communication is compromised.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support simultaneous registration of a functional number on no more than one physical device at any given time, with the network rejecting or deregistering duplicate registrations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Preventing duplicate functional identity registrations ensures that calls are unambiguously routed to the correct operational role holder.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10 simultaneous active calls per GSM-R cell without degradation of call setup time or voice quality below specified thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sufficient per-cell call capacity ensures that dense operational areas such as marshalling yards can be served without congestion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a mean time between failures (MTBF) of no less than 50,000 hours for cab radio units installed in train driving cabs.
generated_requirements.rationale: High MTBF for cab radios minimizes the frequency of in-service failures that could leave train drivers without communication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support software and firmware updates to mobile equipment over-the-air (OTA) or via a standardized maintenance interface without requiring removal of the equipment from service.
generated_requirements.rationale: In-service update capability reduces maintenance downtime and enables rapid deployment of security patches and feature updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall withstand mechanical vibration and shock levels as specified in EN 61373 Category 1 Class B for equipment mounted in railway vehicles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vibration and shock resistance ensures that cab-mounted equipment remains operational under the mechanical stresses of railway traction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that all inter-network signalling and user data exchanged between national GSM-R networks is protected against unauthorized interception and replay attacks at network interconnect points.
generated_requirements.rationale: Securing inter-network interfaces prevents adversaries from exploiting cross-border interconnects to disrupt or eavesdrop on railway communications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a fallback communication mode that allows basic voice calls to be maintained between train drivers and controllers in the event of partial GSM-R network degradation, using available residual network capacity.
generated_requirements.rationale: A degraded-mode fallback ensures that safety-critical voice communication is preserved even when the network is partially impaired.

ground_truth_requirements.text: 1) Call Type (CT) This prefix defines how to interpret the User Number that follows. It shall consist of one or two digits as defined in table 9-1. 2) User Number (UN) The UN is variable in length and depends on the information on which it is based. The following formats are defined: Train Function Numbers (TFN) shall take one of the forms as defined in table 9-2. Leading zeros shall be used in those situations where the train number is less than five digits. Engine Function Numbers (EFN) shall take the format as defined in table 9-3. Coach Function Numbers (CFN) shall take the format as defined in table 9-4. Maintenance and Shunting Team Members. The UIN field format for calls to shunting teams and maintenance teams shall consist of a Location Number (LN), which identifies the location where the called party is registered, followed by a Function Code (FC). The Location Number shall consist of 5 digits and shall be assigned on a national basis. The Function Code shall consist of 4 digits. The first digit of the FC is related to the Team Type (TT) and is specified by table 9-5. The composition of the Function Code field for calls to shunting teams and maintenance teams shall be as defined in table 9-6. Train Controllers. The UIN field for calls to train controllers shall be a Location Number (LN) which identifies the location where the called party is registered. The Location Number shall consist of 5 digits and shall be assigned on a national basis. The FC field for calls to train controllers shall consist of two digits and be as defined in table 9-7. Mobile Subscriber Number (MSN). The UN of the MSN shall consist of the Subscriber Number. Group and broadcast calls. The UIN field format for group calls shall be a Service Area (SA) indicator, which identifies the area in which the group call is to be active. Each Service Area shall be allocated on a national basis. In network boundary areas, the Service Area shall be allocated on a bilateral basis. (See also subsection 9.9.) The FC field format for group calls shall consist of three digits and be as defined in table 9-8.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR
