generated_requirements.text: The system shall support individual point-to-point voice calls between any two registered EIRENE users, including cab radio to cab radio, cab radio to controller, and portable radio to controller.
generated_requirements.rationale: Individual voice calls are a fundamental communication mode required across all user roles in railway operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support group voice calls that simultaneously connect all members of a pre-defined call group, enabling a single originator to address multiple recipients across a designated coverage area.
generated_requirements.rationale: Group calls are essential for coordinating shunting teams, field staff, and controllers operating within the same operational zone.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support broadcast calls that allow a network controller to transmit a one-way voice message to all radios within a specified coverage area or group without requiring recipient acknowledgement.
generated_requirements.rationale: Broadcast calls enable rapid dissemination of operational instructions or warnings to large numbers of users simultaneously.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a Railway Emergency Call (REC) function that, when initiated by a train driver, simultaneously alerts the relevant network controller and all train drivers operating within the same radio block section or designated emergency group.
generated_requirements.rationale: The REC is a safety-critical function requiring immediate, simultaneous notification of all parties who may be affected by a railway emergency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require explicit acknowledgement of a Railway Emergency Call from the network controller, and shall continue to re-alert the controller at defined intervals until acknowledgement is received or a configurable timeout expires.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mandatory acknowledgement ensures that safety-critical emergency alerts are not silently lost and that the controller is confirmed to be aware of the emergency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall assign a priority level to every call, supporting at minimum the following priority classes in descending order: Railway Emergency Call, Operational Emergency, High Priority Operational, Normal Operational, and Administrative.
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined priority hierarchy is required to ensure that safety-critical communications always take precedence over routine traffic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall pre-empt an active lower-priority call by releasing the radio channel and immediately establishing a higher-priority incoming call, notifying the displaced user of the pre-emption event via an audible and visual indication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-emption logic guarantees that network resources are always available for higher-priority safety communications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support functional addressing, allowing calls to be directed to a role-based identity (e.g., 'Driver of Train 1234' or 'Controller Zone A') rather than a specific physical device or SIM identity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Functional addressing decouples communication from physical equipment, ensuring continuity of contact regardless of which device a role-holder is using.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support location-dependent addressing, automatically routing calls to the controller or group responsible for the geographic area in which the calling or called party is currently located.
generated_requirements.rationale: Location-dependent addressing ensures that communications are automatically directed to the operationally relevant authority without requiring the caller to know specific identities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow network administrators to create, modify, and delete call groups, assigning member radios and defining group attributes (e.g., coverage area, priority, talk-back permission) through a centralised network management interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralised group management is required to maintain accurate and up-to-date operational communication structures across the network.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow network administrators to assign, reassign, and revoke functional identities for individual users and devices, with changes taking effect within 30 seconds of being committed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dynamic functional identity management supports shift changes and operational reconfigurations without requiring physical device swaps.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, ensuring that each user role (train driver, shunting operator, field staff, network controller, network administrator) is restricted to only the call types, groups, and management functions permitted for that role.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control prevents unauthorised initiation of safety-critical calls and protects administrative functions from misuse.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support Push-To-Talk (PTT) operation on all cab radios and portable operational radios, with the PTT control activating the transmit channel within 300 milliseconds of being pressed.
generated_requirements.rationale: PTT is the primary interaction modality for operational radio users, and rapid channel activation is essential for time-sensitive communications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a visual display on all cab radios and portable radios showing at minimum: current functional identity, active call status, call priority, incoming call identity, and signal strength.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visual feedback ensures that drivers and operators are always aware of their communication status and identity without relying solely on audio cues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide distinct audible alerts for incoming calls differentiated by call type (individual, group, broadcast, emergency), with the Railway Emergency Call alert being distinguishable from all other alert tones.
generated_requirements.rationale: Differentiated audible alerts allow users to immediately identify the urgency and nature of an incoming call without looking at the display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support data communication services, including the transmission of pre-defined short messages and structured operational data messages between radios and control centre systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data messaging supplements voice communication for non-verbal operational instructions and status reporting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support seamless handover of active voice calls between base stations as a mobile radio moves through the network, with no perceptible interruption to the call audio.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless handover is critical for maintaining continuous communication with train drivers travelling at operational speeds.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support cross-border roaming, allowing a train radio registered on one national railway network to initiate and receive calls when operating on an adjacent national network, subject to bilateral interoperability agreements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-border roaming is essential for international train services operating across European national railway boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support network-to-network call handover at national boundaries, transferring active calls from the originating national network to the destination national network without requiring the driver to re-initiate the call.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent cross-border call handover prevents communication gaps at national boundaries where operational conditions may be most critical.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow each national railway operator to configure the preferred display language for user interface text on radios and controller terminals independently of other national operators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Language configurability ensures that operational staff interact with the system in their native language, reducing the risk of misinterpretation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow individual national railway operators to enable or disable supplementary services (e.g., call forwarding, call waiting, calling line identification restriction) on a per-network or per-user-role basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable supplementary services allow national operators to tailor the system to their specific operational and regulatory requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a pre-defined messaging application framework that allows national operators to define a library of structured short messages selectable by users from a menu on their radio display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-defined messages reduce the cognitive load on drivers and operators and ensure standardised, unambiguous operational communications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all call events — including call initiation, connection, pre-emption, termination, and failed call attempts — with timestamps accurate to within one second, and retain these logs for a minimum of 90 days.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive call logging supports incident investigation, regulatory compliance, and operational auditing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide network controllers with the ability to monitor the active call status and group membership of all radios within their designated control zone in real time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time visibility of radio status enables controllers to manage communications proactively and respond to anomalies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the simultaneous monitoring of multiple group calls by a network controller terminal, allowing the controller to listen to and intervene in any active group call within their zone.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-group monitoring is essential for controllers managing complex operational areas with multiple concurrent communication streams.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall establish a Railway Emergency Call connection within 2 seconds of the emergency call being initiated by the driver, measured from PTT activation to the first audible alert at the controller terminal.
generated_requirements.rationale: A 2-second setup time for emergency calls is a safety-critical latency requirement ensuring that life-threatening situations are communicated without delay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall establish a normal operational voice call connection within 5 seconds of call initiation under nominal network load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prompt call setup for routine operational calls maintains operational efficiency and user confidence in the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver end-to-end voice call audio with a one-way mouth-to-ear latency not exceeding 300 milliseconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low audio latency is required to prevent conversational confusion and ensure natural, effective voice communication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum radio coverage availability of 99.9% along all designated main line railway routes, measured as the proportion of route length where the received signal level meets the minimum usable field strength.
generated_requirements.rationale: Near-continuous coverage along main lines is a safety prerequisite, as communication gaps could leave train crews unable to report or receive emergency information.

generated_requirements.text: The system's core network infrastructure components (switching nodes, home location registers, authentication centres) shall achieve a minimum service availability of 99.999% (five nines) on an annual basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Five-nines availability for core infrastructure ensures that the safety-critical communication platform is effectively always operational.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from a single base station failure within 60 seconds, automatically rerouting affected calls and re-registering affected mobile radios to adjacent base stations without user intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid automatic recovery from base station failures minimises communication outages and maintains safety-critical coverage continuity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain Railway Emergency Call functionality even when the mobile radio is operating in degraded network conditions, including when only one base station is reachable and normal call services are impaired.
generated_requirements.rationale: Emergency call resilience under degraded conditions is a fundamental safety requirement, as emergencies are more likely to occur in challenging environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all mobile radios and user identities using GSM-A3/A8 cryptographic algorithms or equivalent, preventing unauthorised devices from registering on the network.
generated_requirements.rationale: Device and user authentication prevents rogue radios from accessing the safety-critical communication network.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all voice and data transmissions over the radio interface using GSM-A5 encryption or an equivalent algorithm providing at least equivalent cryptographic strength.
generated_requirements.rationale: Over-the-air encryption protects sensitive operational communications from interception by unauthorised parties.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce mutual authentication between roaming mobile radios and visited national networks during cross-border operations, preventing man-in-the-middle attacks at network boundaries.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mutual authentication at cross-border handover points is critical to maintaining security integrity across heterogeneous national networks.

generated_requirements.text: The network management system shall require multi-factor authentication for all administrator logins and shall enforce a role-based permission model that prevents any single administrator from making changes to both user access rights and audit logs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Separation of duties and strong authentication for administrators reduces the risk of insider threats and accidental misconfiguration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock a user account after five consecutive failed authentication attempts and require administrator intervention or a time-based unlock after a minimum of 15 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout policies mitigate brute-force credential attacks against the network management and controller interfaces.

generated_requirements.text: All cab radio and portable radio user interfaces shall conform to human factors guidelines for safety-critical railway equipment, ensuring that the Railway Emergency Call function is accessible via a dedicated, physically distinct, and clearly labelled control that can be activated within 3 seconds by a user wearing standard operational gloves.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ergonomic design of the emergency call control is a safety requirement to ensure it can be reliably activated under stress and in adverse physical conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface for train drivers shall require no more than three user actions (button presses or menu selections) to initiate any pre-defined call type from an idle state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Minimising interaction steps reduces driver distraction and the risk of communication errors during safety-critical operations.

generated_requirements.text: The network management interface shall provide context-sensitive help and validation feedback for all configuration operations, clearly indicating the operational impact of changes before they are committed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Guided configuration reduces the likelihood of administrative errors that could disrupt operational communication groups or access controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to the EIRENE Functional Requirements Specification (FRS) and System Requirements Specification (SRS) as published by the UIC, ensuring interoperability with all compliant GSM-R equipment from any manufacturer.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adherence to published EIRENE standards is the primary mechanism for achieving multi-vendor and multi-national interoperability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support interoperability testing against a published EIRENE conformance test suite, and all network components shall pass conformance testing before being placed into operational service.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conformance testing provides objective evidence that equipment meets interoperability standards before deployment.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be modular, with clearly defined interfaces between the radio access network, core switching network, and network management subsystems, enabling individual components to be upgraded or replaced without requiring changes to other subsystems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future upgrades and technology refreshes across the long operational lifetime of railway infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a centralised network management platform that supports remote configuration, software updates, and fault diagnosis for all base stations and core network nodes without requiring on-site engineer attendance for routine operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Remote management capability reduces operational costs and enables faster response to faults across geographically dispersed railway infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate structured fault alarms for all detected hardware and software failures, classifying each alarm by severity (critical, major, minor, warning) and automatically notifying the network operations centre within 10 seconds of fault detection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid, classified fault notification enables the operations centre to prioritise and dispatch maintenance resources efficiently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support planned maintenance windows during which individual base stations or core nodes can be taken out of service for maintenance without causing a complete loss of coverage, by automatically redistributing load to adjacent nodes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation during planned maintenance ensures that safety-critical coverage is maintained even while infrastructure work is carried out.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 simultaneously registered mobile radios per national network instance without degradation of call setup times or voice quality below specified thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability to large numbers of registered users is required to support the full operational fleet of a national railway operator.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete cross-border functional identity re-registration and group membership restoration within 10 seconds of a mobile radio entering a visited national network, ensuring uninterrupted operational communication capability.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast re-registration at national boundaries prevents communication gaps during the critical period when a train crosses into a new network jurisdiction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide redundant, geographically separated core network nodes such that the failure of any single node or data centre does not result in loss of service for any coverage area, with automatic failover completing within 30 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Geographic redundancy with automatic failover is essential to meet the high availability requirements of a safety-critical national communication infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident, write-once audit trail of all network management actions — including configuration changes, identity assignments, group modifications, and access control changes — with each entry recording the administrator identity, timestamp, and nature of the change.
generated_requirements.rationale: A tamper-evident audit trail supports post-incident investigation, regulatory compliance, and accountability for all administrative actions on the safety-critical network.

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.1.2 Three distinct mobile radio types are required, based on the type of role they will perform and the environment in which they will operate, as follows: (I) a) Cab radio for use by the driver of a train and/or by other on-train systems, eg ERTMS/ETCS; b) General purpose radio for general use by railway personnel; c) Operational radio for use by railway personnel involved in train operations such as shunting and trackside maintenance. Note: It is possible that the General purpose and Operational radios may have a number of variants to meet railway requirements (eg handheld and vehicle mounted). 4.1.2 Three distinct mobile radio types are required, based on the type of role they will perform and the environment in which they will operate, as follows: (I) a) Cab radio for use by the driver of a train and/or by other on-train systems, eg ERTMS/ETCS; b) General purpose radio for general use by railway personnel; c) Operational radio for use by railway personnel involved in train operations such as shunting and trackside maintenance. Note: It is possible that the General purpose and Operational radios may have a number of variants to meet railway requirements (eg handheld and vehicle mounted).

ground_truth_requirements.text: 15.2.2 Principal requirements are: (M see above) 15.2.2 Principal requirements are: (M see above) a range of up to at least 2000m in open terrain between a direct mode transmitter and receiver; a voice only capability that supports the use of the link assurance signal; open channel mode of operation such that all users employing direct mode receive transmissions when in range of the transmitting user; it is sufficient that the user may talk or listen only, but not both. The ability to talk shall be achieved through use of the Push-To-Talk function; a minimum of one channel is to be available for use of direct mode facilities; if more than one channel is provided, there must be a function allowing the user to manually select the channel; where equipment provides both normal and direct mode capability, the user shall be able to switch between the two modes in a straightforward manner (but not by accident); the user may only select direct mode when the normal mobile telephony services are not available; controls shall be simple to use, eg: direct mode on/off switch; Push-To-Talk function; channel selection; volume control; battery warning for handportables; battery life for handportables operating in direct mode shall be a minimum of 8 hours based on the following cycle: 20% transmit; 20% receive; 60% stand-by; when direct mode is used as a fall-back, an audible and visual indication shall be provided of normal mobile telephony services returning.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL continuously calculate the Most Restrictive Speed Profile (MRSP) and derive braking curves (warning, service brake intervention, emergency brake intervention) based on current train position, speed, gradient, train characteristics, and Movement Authority (MA) received from trackside.
generated_requirements.rationale: Braking curve calculation is the core safety-critical function mandated by ERA/ERTMS Baseline 3 specifications to prevent overspeed and overrun.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL automatically command a service brake intervention when the train speed reaches the Service Brake Intervention (SBI) curve, and SHALL escalate to an emergency brake command if the train speed reaches the Emergency Brake Intervention (EBI) curve, without requiring any driver action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic brake intervention is the primary safety enforcement mechanism to ensure trains do not exceed permitted speed or distance limits.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL apply an emergency brake command immediately and unconditionally when the train passes the End of Authority (EoA) or a Stop-if-in-Danger (SID) location without a valid Movement Authority extension.
generated_requirements.rationale: Preventing overrun of the EoA is a fundamental safety requirement to avoid train collisions and buffer stop impacts.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL enforce a ceiling speed limit at all times and SHALL apply an emergency brake if the train speed exceeds the Maximum Speed (V_MAX) for the train or the track section, whichever is lower.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ceiling speed enforcement ensures compliance with both train-specific and infrastructure-specific speed restrictions at all times.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL implement a state machine governing transitions between operational levels: Level 0 (no ETCS), Level NTC (national train control), Level 1 (infill/spot transmission), Level 2 (continuous radio communication with RBC), and Level 3 (moving block). Transitions SHALL only occur upon receipt of a valid Level Transition Order or driver acknowledgement as specified in ERA/ERTMS Baseline 3.
generated_requirements.rationale: A well-defined level transition state machine ensures safe and interoperable handover between different national and ETCS control regimes.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL implement a state machine governing ETCS modes including Full Supervision (FS), Limited Supervision (LS), On Sight (OS), Shunting (SH), Unfitted (UN), Sleeping (SL), Stand By (SB), Trip (TR), Post Trip (PT), System Failure (SF), Isolation (IS), and National System (NL). Mode transitions SHALL follow the transition rules defined in ERA/ERTMS Baseline 3 SRS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Correct mode management ensures the system applies the appropriate level of supervision and protection for each operational scenario.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL request, receive, and process Movement Authorities (MAs) from the Radio Block Centre (RBC) in Level 2 and Level 3 operations, and SHALL request MA extensions before the current MA expires, using the GSM-R or GPRS radio communication channel.
generated_requirements.rationale: Continuous MA management via RBC communication is essential for safe train movement authorisation in Level 2/3 operations.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL perform a safe position report to the RBC at defined intervals and upon request, including train front position, train rear position (if known), confidence interval, speed, direction, and ETCS train ID.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accurate and timely position reporting enables the RBC to maintain a correct picture of train occupancy and grant safe MAs.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL establish, maintain, and re-establish radio communication sessions with the RBC according to the RBC/RBC handover protocol, including initiation of a new session when entering a new RBC area and safe termination of the previous session.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless RBC handover is required to maintain continuous supervision across RBC boundaries without gaps in movement authority.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL read and process Eurobalise telegrams upon passage, extracting track description data, national values, linking information, and level/mode transition orders, and SHALL use this data to update the internal track model and supervision parameters.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eurobalise data is the primary source of trackside information in Level 1 and a supplementary source in Level 2, critical for accurate supervision.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL accept and process infill information from Euroloops or radio infill units in Level 1 operations to extend the Movement Authority ahead of the train without requiring a new balise passage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Infill capability improves operational performance in Level 1 by allowing MA extension before the train reaches the next balise group.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL calculate and maintain a safe worst-case train position, including an odometry confidence interval (d_loc), accounting for wheel slip, slide, measurement errors, and distance since last known reference point.
generated_requirements.rationale: Safe position estimation with a confidence interval is required to ensure the system always supervises based on the most conservative position assumption.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL display the following information on the Driver Machine Interface (DMI) in real time: current speed (digital and analogue), permitted speed, target speed, target distance, ETCS mode, ETCS level, and active supervision status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Providing the driver with accurate real-time supervision data is essential for safe train operation and situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The DMI SHALL display a visual warning indication (pre-indication marker and overspeed indication) when the train speed approaches the warning curve (W), and SHALL display a distinct visual and audible alert when the service brake intervention curve (SBI) is reached.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely and distinct driver alerts allow the driver to take corrective action before automatic brake intervention, reducing unnecessary brake applications.

generated_requirements.text: The DMI SHALL present all driver acknowledgement requests (e.g., mode change acknowledgement, level transition acknowledgement, track-ahead free confirmation) as modal prompts that require explicit driver interaction within a defined timeout period, after which the system SHALL take the safe default action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mandatory acknowledgement with safe defaults ensures the driver is aware of critical state changes and prevents silent mode transitions.

generated_requirements.text: The DMI SHALL display text messages received from the RBC or trackside with a priority classification (normal, important, urgent) and SHALL require driver acknowledgement for messages classified as important or urgent before they are cleared from the display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prioritised message handling ensures safety-critical communications from the RBC are not missed or dismissed without driver awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The DMI SHALL provide a data entry interface for the driver to input train data (train category, length, maximum speed, brake percentage, axle load category) at start of mission, and SHALL validate all entered values against permitted ranges before accepting them.
generated_requirements.rationale: Correct train data entry is a prerequisite for accurate braking curve calculation and safe supervision throughout the mission.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL support the Start of Mission (SoM) procedure, including driver ID entry, train data entry, position confirmation via balise or driver input, and establishment of radio communication, before entering Full Supervision mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: A complete and validated SoM procedure ensures the system has all necessary data to provide safe supervision from the outset of a train journey.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL record a Journal of Events (JRU data) including, at minimum: train position and speed at defined intervals, all ETCS mode and level transitions, all brake commands issued, all DMI driver inputs and acknowledgements, all balise passages, all radio messages sent and received, and all system failures.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive event recording is required to support post-incident safety investigations and regulatory compliance auditing.

generated_requirements.text: The Juridical Recording Unit (JRU) SHALL timestamp all recorded events with a resolution of at least 10 milliseconds, synchronised to a reliable time source (e.g., GPS or network time), and SHALL store data in a tamper-evident format.
generated_requirements.rationale: High-resolution, tamper-evident timestamping is essential for accurate reconstruction of event sequences during safety investigations.

generated_requirements.text: JRU data SHALL be retained onboard for a minimum of 72 hours of continuous operation and SHALL be downloadable by authorised maintenance personnel only, using authenticated access via a standardised interface (e.g., STM or dedicated download port).
generated_requirements.rationale: A minimum retention period and controlled access ensure data is available for post-incident analysis while protecting against unauthorised access or tampering.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL detect and handle communication loss with the RBC in Level 2/3 by initiating a safe stop procedure if no valid MA is available and the train approaches the EoA, and SHALL notify the driver of the communication failure via the DMI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation on radio loss prevents unsafe train movement beyond the last known authority in the absence of RBC communication.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL support integration with National Train Control (NTC/STM) systems via the standardised STM interface, allowing simultaneous supervision by both ETCS and national systems during level NTC operation, with ETCS maintaining a minimum level of protection at all times.
generated_requirements.rationale: STM integration is required for cross-border interoperability and safe operation on national networks not yet fully equipped with ETCS.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL accept, store, and apply national values received from trackside (e.g., Q_NVDRIVER_ADHES, V_NVSHUNT, V_NVSTFF) and SHALL use these values to override default ETCS parameters for the applicable national network section.
generated_requirements.rationale: National value management enables adaptation to country-specific operational rules and infrastructure characteristics while maintaining ETCS supervision.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL perform a self-test and integrity check at power-on and SHALL enter System Failure (SF) mode if any safety-critical hardware or software component fails the self-test, preventing train movement until the fault is resolved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Power-on self-test ensures the system does not operate in a degraded or unsafe state without the driver and maintenance staff being aware.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL implement a two-out-of-two (2oo2) or equivalent voting architecture for all safety-critical processing channels, such that any single hardware or software failure results in a safe state (emergency brake application) rather than an unsafe output.
generated_requirements.rationale: A 2oo2 or equivalent fail-safe architecture is required to achieve SIL 4 integrity as mandated by EN 50128 and EN 50129 for ETCS onboard equipment.

generated_requirements.text: The entire onboard ETCS software SHALL be developed, verified, and validated in accordance with EN 50128:2011 (or later) at Software Safety Integrity Level 4 (SIL 4), with full traceability from requirements through design, implementation, and test evidence.
generated_requirements.rationale: SIL 4 compliance is a mandatory regulatory requirement for ETCS onboard software under the European Railway Safety Directive and TSI CCS.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL achieve a Tolerable Hazard Rate (THR) of no greater than 10⁻⁹ per hour for any single hazardous failure mode that could lead to a catastrophic accident, in accordance with EN 50126 RAMS requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: The THR target quantifies the required safety integrity and is the basis for the probabilistic safety case submitted to the National Safety Authority.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL achieve a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of no less than 200,000 hours for the complete onboard system under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: High MTBF minimises service disruptions caused by onboard equipment failures and supports the operational availability targets of railway operators.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS system SHALL achieve an operational availability of no less than 99.9% (measured over a rolling 12-month period), excluding planned maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is essential to prevent ETCS-induced train delays and to maintain the operational capacity of the rail network.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL process a received Eurobalise telegram and update all supervision parameters (speed profile, braking curves, MA) within 100 milliseconds of balise passage at the maximum line speed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid processing of balise data is required to ensure supervision parameters are updated before the train has travelled a safety-critical distance past the balise.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL recalculate braking curves and update the DMI speed display at a cycle time of no greater than 100 milliseconds under all operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A fast recalculation cycle ensures the driver and the brake intervention logic always act on current, accurate supervision data.

generated_requirements.text: The end-to-end latency for a brake command from the detection of an EBI curve exceedance to the energisation of the brake relay output SHALL not exceed 50 milliseconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Minimal brake command latency is critical to ensure the train stops within the safety margin defined by the braking curve calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL conform to ERA/ERTMS Baseline 3 Release 2 (or the applicable baseline specified in the project TSI) and SHALL pass all mandatory ERA conformance test cases defined in the ETCS FFFIS and SUBSET-076 test specifications before deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conformance to the ERA baseline and test specifications is the legal and technical basis for cross-border interoperability across the European rail network.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL be interoperable with trackside equipment (Eurobalises, Euroloops, RBCs) from any ERA-certified supplier without requiring supplier-specific configuration, in accordance with the applicable FFFIS and SUBSET specifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supplier-agnostic interoperability is a core objective of the ERTMS programme to eliminate national system fragmentation across Europe.

generated_requirements.text: All safety-critical communication messages exchanged between the onboard equipment and the RBC SHALL be protected by the ETCS Euroradio safe communication layer (SUBSET-037), including sequence numbering, message authentication codes (MAC), and session management to detect replay, insertion, deletion, and corruption attacks.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Euroradio safe communication protocol provides the cryptographic integrity and authenticity guarantees required for safety-critical radio messaging.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL use cryptographic keys for Euroradio message authentication that are managed through a Key Management System (KMS) compliant with ERA/ERTMS security requirements, and SHALL support secure key provisioning, rotation, and revocation without requiring physical access to the onboard unit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Robust key management prevents unauthorised parties from injecting or spoofing safety-critical RBC messages.

generated_requirements.text: Access to onboard ETCS configuration parameters, software update interfaces, and JRU download ports SHALL be restricted to authenticated and authorised personnel only, using role-based access control with at least two distinct roles: maintenance technician and safety auditor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control prevents unauthorised modification of safety-critical configuration data and protects the integrity of recorded evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS software SHALL be structured in accordance with a documented software architecture that enforces strict separation between safety-critical (SIL 4) and non-safety (SIL 0) software partitions, with no uncontrolled data flow from lower SIL partitions to higher SIL partitions.
generated_requirements.rationale: SIL partitioning prevents non-safety software updates from inadvertently compromising the integrity of safety-critical functions.

generated_requirements.text: Every software requirement SHALL be uniquely identified and SHALL have bidirectional traceability to at least one design element, one implementation unit, and one verification test case, maintained in a configuration-managed requirements traceability matrix.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full bidirectional traceability is mandated by EN 50128 SIL 4 and is essential for impact analysis during change management.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS software SHALL be designed to allow field software updates (patches and version upgrades) to be applied without requiring removal of the onboard unit from the vehicle, and each update SHALL be validated by a cryptographic signature check before installation.
generated_requirements.rationale: In-situ update capability reduces maintenance downtime and fleet-wide update costs while the signature check prevents installation of unauthorised software.

generated_requirements.text: The DMI display SHALL be legible under all ambient lighting conditions encountered in railway cab environments, including direct sunlight (minimum luminance contrast ratio of 7:1) and low-light conditions (minimum display brightness of 300 cd/m²).
generated_requirements.rationale: Display legibility under all lighting conditions is required to ensure the driver can always read safety-critical information without distraction.

generated_requirements.text: The DMI SHALL conform to the ERA ETCS Driver Machine Interface specification (ERA_ERTMS_015560) for all screen layouts, colour coding, symbol definitions, and interaction sequences, ensuring a consistent driver experience across all ETCS-equipped vehicles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standardised DMI design reduces driver training burden and prevents mode confusion errors when drivers operate different ETCS-equipped vehicles.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL operate correctly across the full environmental range specified in EN 50155 for railway rolling stock, including temperatures from -25°C to +70°C, humidity up to 95% non-condensing, and vibration and shock levels defined in EN 61373 Category 1.
generated_requirements.rationale: Environmental robustness ensures the onboard equipment functions correctly throughout its service life in the harsh railway cab environment.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL be electromagnetically compatible with all other onboard systems and trackside equipment in accordance with EN 50121-3-2, and SHALL not cause or be susceptible to interference that could affect safety-critical functions.
generated_requirements.rationale: EMC compliance prevents electromagnetic interference from corrupting safety-critical signals or causing spurious brake commands.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL detect any loss of odometry input (tachometer failure, radar failure) and SHALL respond by applying a conservative position uncertainty model and notifying the driver, entering a degraded supervision mode if the uncertainty exceeds a configurable threshold.
generated_requirements.rationale: Odometry fault detection and graceful degradation prevents the system from supervising based on an undetected position error.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL support the Shunting mode, in which speed is supervised against a configurable national shunting speed limit (V_NVSHUNT), and SHALL require explicit driver request and, where configured, RBC authorisation before entering Shunting mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: Controlled entry into Shunting mode with speed supervision prevents unsafe shunting movements in depot and station areas.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL implement the Trip and Post-Trip mode sequence: upon passing a Stop-if-in-Danger location, the system SHALL enter Trip mode, apply emergency brakes, and only allow transition to Post-Trip mode after the train has come to a standstill and the driver has acknowledged the trip event.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Trip/Post-Trip sequence ensures the driver is fully aware of a safety-critical overrun event before the train is allowed to move again.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL validate the integrity of all received Eurobalise telegrams using the CRC and telegram structure checks defined in SUBSET-036, and SHALL discard and log any telegram that fails integrity validation without applying its content to the supervision model.
generated_requirements.rationale: Telegram integrity validation prevents corrupted or spoofed balise data from being applied to safety-critical supervision parameters.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard ETCS equipment SHALL support a documented and tested degraded mode of operation for each defined failure scenario (radio loss, balise read failure, odometry degradation), with each degraded mode maintaining the maximum achievable level of safety supervision and being clearly indicated to the driver on the DMI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined and tested degraded modes ensure predictable and safe system behaviour under partial failure conditions, supporting the overall safety case.

ground_truth_requirements.text: ETCS shall provide the driver with information to allow him to drive the train safely. ETCS shall provide the driver with information to allow him to drive the train safely.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: ETCS shall be able to supervise train and shunting movements. ETCS shall be able to supervise train and shunting movements.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the supervision is performed by a RBC it shall be possible to prevent movements of a traction unit in its area if not authorised by the RBC. If the supervision is performed by a RBC it shall be possible to prevent movements of a traction unit in its area if not authorised by the RBC.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: ETCS is required to be functional to a maximum train speed of 500 km/h. ETCS is required to be functional to a maximum train speed of 500 km/h.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The following definitions shall apply for the ETCS application levels: The following definitions shall apply for the ETCS application levels: Level 0: ETCS active for limited train control function; trackside not fitted with any train control system or fitted with a train control system for which no STM is available onboard. Level 1: Basic track to train information via intermittent transmission media, e.g. balises. This information can be supported by infill, transmitted via balise, loop or radio. Level 2: Basic track to train and train to track information via continuous transmission media, i.e. radio. The train detection is provided by trackside. Level 3: Same as level 2 except that train integrity is provided by onboard and therefore trackside. train detection is optional. Level STM (Specific Transmission Module): Track to train information provided by national system. Onboard functions provided by national system (STM) in co-operation with onboard ETCS.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: It shall be possible to implement one or more of the ETCS application levels on a line. It shall be possible to implement one or more of the ETCS application levels on a line.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 3 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 3, 2, 1 and 0, trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 2 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 2, 1 and 0 and trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 and 0. Trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 3 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 3, 2, 1 and 0, trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 2 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 2, 1 and 0 and trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 and 0.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The current application level shall be indicated on the DMI. The current application level shall be indicated on the DMI.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The driver shall acknowledge the level transitions, if requested from trackside. If the driver does not acknowledge after the transition the brake shall be applied. If the driver acknowledges afterwards, the brake can be released The driver shall acknowledge the level transitions, if requested from trackside. If the driver does not acknowledge after the transition the brake shall be applied. If the driver acknowledges afterwards, the brake can be released
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: ETCS shall be compatible with existing national systems listed in the CCS TSI such that it does not interfere with the national systems and is not interfered with by the national systems. ETCS shall be compatible with existing national systems listed in the CCS TSI such that it does not interfere with the national systems and is not interfered with by the national systems.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The ETCS trainborne equipment shall be capable of supervising the following operational states: The ETCS trainborne equipment shall be capable of supervising the following operational states: Full Supervision operation Partial Supervision operation Staff Responsible operation On Sight operation Unfitted Line operation Train Trip operation Post Trip operation National operation (STM) Tandem operation Multiple operation Shunting operation Stand By operation Reversing operation
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Any transition which occurs while the train is moving shall in principle occur automatically. Any transition which occurs while the train is moving shall in principle occur automatically.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Transitions which occur while the train is stationary, shall be initiated automatically or manually as appropriate. Transitions which occur while the train is stationary, shall be initiated automatically or manually as appropriate.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If, as a result of an automatic transition, except for transitions to and from National Operation (STM), the responsibility for the driver increases, the ETCS shall seek an acknowledgement from the driver, whether the train is stationary or not. If, as a result of an automatic transition, except for transitions to and from National Operation (STM), the responsibility for the driver increases, the ETCS shall seek an acknowledgement from the driver, whether the train is stationary or not.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: For transitions to and from National Operation (STM) the ETCS shall request, an acknowledgement by the driver. For transitions to and from National Operation (STM) the ETCS shall request, an acknowledgement by the driver.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: In case the transition has to be acknowledged and the driver fails to acknowledge as required, the ETCS shall initiate a brake application In case the transition has to be acknowledged and the driver fails to acknowledge as required, the ETCS shall initiate a brake application
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: During the transition period between two operational states (including two different national operations) the supervision provided shall at least ensure the same protection provided by the least restrictive state. During the transition period between two operational states (including two different national operations) the supervision provided shall at least ensure the same protection provided by the least restrictive state.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If an ETCS equipped train passes a level transition to a line fitted with more than one level, the onboard shall switch to the highest level, according to the priority given by trackside, for which it is equipped. If an ETCS equipped train passes a level transition to a line fitted with more than one level, the onboard shall switch to the highest level, according to the priority given by trackside, for which it is equipped.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If an ETCS equipped train passes a level transition to one or more levels for which it is not equipped, ETCS shall initiate a brake application. If an ETCS equipped train passes a level transition to one or more levels for which it is not equipped, ETCS shall initiate a brake application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The current operational status shall be indicated to the driver on the DMI The current operational status shall be indicated to the driver on the DMI
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The ETCS on-board shall be capable of receiving National values from the trackside to adapt to National requirements The ETCS on-board shall be capable of receiving National values from the trackside to adapt to National requirements
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: National values shall be applicable to a defined area National values shall be applicable to a defined area
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Once received onboard the national values shall remain valid even if the onboard equipment is switched off Once received onboard the national values shall remain valid even if the onboard equipment is switched off
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the on-board has no valid national values for the current location, default values shall be used by the onboard equipment. If the on-board has no valid national values for the current location, default values shall be used by the onboard equipment.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Default values shall be harmonised values, permanently stored in all ERTMS/ETCS on board equipment Default values shall be harmonised values, permanently stored in all ERTMS/ETCS on board equipment
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: At Start Up, the on board equipment shall perform an automatic self-test. At Start Up, the on board equipment shall perform an automatic self-test.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The test shall require no action on the part of the driver. The test shall require no action on the part of the driver.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The DMI shall indicate the result of the self-test. The DMI shall indicate the result of the self-test.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Train data shall be entered before the on-board ETCS equipment allows train movement. Train data shall be entered before the on-board ETCS equipment allows train movement.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The driver shall be able to select Train Data Entry on the DMI. The driver shall be able to select Train Data Entry on the DMI.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Entering or overwriting data manually by the driver shall be possible but only when stationary. Entering or overwriting data manually by the driver shall be possible but only when stationary.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Train data may be entered automatically from a railway management system or from train memory. Train data may be entered automatically from a railway management system or from train memory.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The driver shall be able to consult train data when the train is stationary or moving. The driver shall be able to consult train data when the train is stationary or moving.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Current train data shall be stored (except at transition to shunting) in the ETCS equipment until the traction unit is not operative. Current train data shall be stored (except at transition to shunting) in the ETCS equipment until the traction unit is not operative.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Stored train data shall be offered to the driver to be confirmed when Data Entry starts. Stored train data shall be offered to the driver to be confirmed when Data Entry starts.

This may require additional items, not required for ETCS, to be entered.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The entry of driver identification and the selection of the language shall be possible. The entry of driver identification and the selection of the language shall be possible.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The change of driver identification during a journey or a Train Running Number shall be possible The change of driver identification during a journey or a Train Running Number shall be possible

ground_truth_requirements.text: Following successful completion of Train Data Entry, the driver shall be able to perform shunting movements or train movements. Following successful completion of Train Data Entry, the driver shall be able to perform shunting movements or train movements.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The following data may be entered manually by the driver or from train memory: The following data may be entered manually by the driver or from train memory: Driver identification Train identification (train number) STM ready for use Data required for brake calculation Maximum train speed Train length Status of air tight system Type of electric power accepted Data additional required for STM (if any) International train category Train gauge Maximum axle load of the train with a resolution of 0,5 t

ground_truth_requirements.text: The following data may be entered manually by the driver provided by external sources : The following data may be entered manually by the driver provided by external sources : Driver identification Train identification (train number) STM ready for use Data required for brake calculation Maximum train speed Train length Status of air tight system Type of electric power accepted Data additional required for STM (if any) International train category Train gauge Maximum axle load of the train with a resolution of 0,5 t
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the onboard fails to contact the RBC when awakening the driver shall be asked to enter the RBC contact details If the onboard fails to contact the RBC when awakening the driver shall be asked to enter the RBC contact details
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: An ETCS equipped traction unit shall be capable of being moved in Shunting without train data, track data or movement authority. An ETCS equipped traction unit shall be capable of being moved in Shunting without train data, track data or movement authority.

To prevent unauthorised use of the function permission shall be obtained from the RBC if the train is operating under the control of the RBC.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Before authomatic transition to Shunting, ETCS shall request confirmation from the driver. Before authomatic transition to Shunting, ETCS shall request confirmation from the driver.

ground_truth_requirements.text: It shall be possible to apply the train trip function, if the shunting movement passes a signal showing "danger for shunting". It shall be possible to apply the train trip function, if the shunting movement passes a signal showing "danger for shunting".

ground_truth_requirements.text: Exit from Shunting shall only be possible when the train is stationary. Exit from Shunting shall only be possible when the train is stationary.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Exit from Shunting shall take place when the driver selects exit from shunting. Exit from Shunting shall take place when the driver selects exit from shunting.

ground_truth_requirements.text: If acknowledgement is specified the driver shall acknowledge transfer from Full Supervision to Partial Supervision within 5 seconds If acknowledgement is specified the driver shall acknowledge transfer from Full Supervision to Partial Supervision within 5 seconds
