ground_truth_requirements.text: It shall be possible to revoke a Movement Authority that has already been issued to a train in a co-operative way between RBC and train. It shall be possible to revoke a Movement Authority that has already been issued to a train in a co-operative way between RBC and train.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The co-operative revocation of the MA shall be possible to a new target location, proposed from RBC. The co-operative revocation of the MA shall be possible to a new target location, proposed from RBC.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The new target location shall be checked for acceptance by the on board. The new target location shall be checked for acceptance by the on board.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If a train cannot stop at the proposed new target location it shall reject the request and keep the old target location. If a train cannot stop at the proposed new target location it shall reject the request and keep the old target location.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Reversing function shall only be possible in one active cab which is not closed at any time during the procedure. The Reversing function shall only be possible in one active cab which is not closed at any time during the procedure.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Reversing shall be possible as defined by a value given with the MA Reversing shall be possible as defined by a value given with the MA
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The driver shall be able to use the Reversing function without needing to re-confirm the train data. The driver shall be able to use the Reversing function without needing to re-confirm the train data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Reversing shall be supervised to a distance and speed set as National Values Reversing shall be supervised to a distance and speed set as National Values
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The distance supervised can be extended from the trackside. The distance supervised can be extended from the trackside.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Once the train starts reversing the MA shall be cancelled. Once the train starts reversing the MA shall be cancelled.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The train shall be able to automatically pass from one RBC area to another without driver intervention. The train shall be able to automatically pass from one RBC area to another without driver intervention.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the train is equipped with two operational radios there shall be no performance penalty as a result of a transition from one RBC to another (train spacing and train speed). If the train is equipped with two operational radios there shall be no performance penalty as a result of a transition from one RBC to another (train spacing and train speed).
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the train is equipped with only one operational radio, passing from one RBC to another shall still be possible but might result in a performance penalty. If the train is equipped with only one operational radio, passing from one RBC to another shall still be possible but might result in a performance penalty.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: In the event of a Transmission Failure the following reactions, shall be capable of being applied in accordance with a National Value: In the event of a Transmission Failure the following reactions, shall be capable of being applied in accordance with a National Value: Option 1. The ETCS trainborne equipment shall immediately command the emergency brake. The failure shall be shown on the DMI. Option 2. The ETCS trainborne equipment shall immediately command the full service brake. The failure shall be shown on the DMI. Option 3. The train may proceed unrestricted to the end of its movement authority. The indication on the DMI shall remain, and the driver shall be informed about the loss of transmission.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If there are failures of the trainborne equipment which compromise the safety of train supervision, the ETCS trainborne equipment shall immediately command the brake and bring the train to a stop. If there are failures of the trainborne equipment which compromise the safety of train supervision, the ETCS trainborne equipment shall immediately command the brake and bring the train to a stop.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The occurrence of a failure shall be displayed on the DMI. The occurrence of a failure shall be displayed on the DMI.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: In ETCS with RBC this restriction on performance shall, if possible be transmitted to the RBC. In ETCS with RBC this restriction on performance shall, if possible be transmitted to the RBC.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to create a new encrypted password database protected by a master password.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core functionality enabling users to establish a secure, centralized credential store.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to create a new encrypted password database protected by a key file.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supports key-file-only authentication as an alternative to master password protection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to create a new encrypted password database protected by a combination of a master password and a key file.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables multi-factor authentication for the highest level of database security.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt the entire password database using AES-256 encryption before writing it to disk.
generated_requirements.rationale: AES-256 is an industry-standard cipher ensuring strong at-rest data protection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall derive the database encryption key from the master password using a configurable key-derivation function (e.g., Argon2 or AES-KDF) with a user-configurable iteration count.
generated_requirements.rationale: A strong KDF with high iteration counts mitigates brute-force and dictionary attacks against the master password.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a strict no-recovery, no-backdoor policy: if all authentication credentials (master password and/or key file) are lost, the database shall be permanently inaccessible and no recovery mechanism shall exist.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminating any recovery path ensures no attacker can exploit a backdoor to gain unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall clearly warn users during database creation that loss of authentication credentials will result in permanent, irrecoverable loss of access to the database.
generated_requirements.rationale: Informed users can take appropriate precautions (e.g., backing up key files) before data loss occurs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall never store the master password in plaintext in memory for longer than the minimum time required to derive the encryption key, and shall securely zero the memory region immediately after use.
generated_requirements.rationale: Minimizing the plaintext password's memory lifetime reduces exposure to memory-scraping attacks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to open an existing encrypted password database by providing the correct authentication credentials (master password, key file, or both).
generated_requirements.rationale: Unlocking an existing database is the primary day-to-day workflow for credential retrieval.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock the database and clear all decrypted data from memory automatically after a user-configurable idle timeout period.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-lock prevents unauthorized access when the user leaves the application unattended.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to create credential entries containing at minimum: title, username, password, URL, notes, and custom fields.
generated_requirements.rationale: A rich entry schema accommodates the diverse credential types users need to store.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to edit any field of an existing credential entry and save the changes to the database.
generated_requirements.rationale: Credentials change over time and users must be able to keep stored data current.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to delete one or more credential entries from the database, with a confirmation prompt before permanent deletion.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deletion with confirmation prevents accidental loss of credential data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to duplicate an existing credential entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Duplication speeds up creation of similar entries and reduces repetitive data entry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to organize credential entries into hierarchical groups and subgroups of unlimited nesting depth.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hierarchical grouping enables logical organization of large numbers of credentials.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to create, rename, move, and delete groups and subgroups within the database.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full CRUD operations on groups give users complete control over their organizational structure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to move or copy credential entries between groups via drag-and-drop and via a context menu.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple interaction methods for reorganizing entries improve usability for different user preferences.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a full-text search capability that searches across all fields of all entries in the database and returns results in real time as the user types.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast, comprehensive search is essential for quickly locating credentials in large databases.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support advanced search filters including field-specific search, regular expressions, and case-sensitivity options.
generated_requirements.rationale: Advanced filters allow power users to perform precise queries across complex databases.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include a built-in password generator that produces passwords according to user-configurable rules (length, character sets, entropy requirements).
generated_requirements.rationale: An integrated generator encourages use of strong, unique passwords for every credential.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a password strength indicator when a user enters or generates a password for an entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visual feedback on password strength guides users toward more secure credential choices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support auto-type sequences that automatically type a user-defined sequence of keystrokes (e.g., username, Tab, password, Enter) into a target application window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-type enables seamless, hands-free login to applications without exposing credentials via clipboard.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to define custom auto-type sequences per entry, overriding the global default sequence.
generated_requirements.rationale: Custom sequences accommodate non-standard login forms that differ from the default username/password pattern.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall match auto-type target windows to entries using configurable window-title association rules, and shall prompt the user to select the correct entry when multiple matches exist.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accurate window matching prevents credentials from being typed into the wrong application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support Transaction Authentication Number (TAN) entries, marking each TAN as used/expired automatically after it has been accessed or auto-typed once.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic TAN expiry enforces one-time-use semantics and prevents accidental reuse of spent TANs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall visually distinguish expired/used TAN entries from valid ones in the entry list.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clear visual differentiation prevents users from attempting to use an already-expired TAN.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a command-line interface (CLI) that supports opening a database, querying entries, and exporting data, authenticated via master password and/or key file supplied as arguments or via stdin.
generated_requirements.rationale: CLI access enables scripting, automation, and integration into power-user and DevOps workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that passwords or sensitive values passed via CLI arguments are not exposed in process listings or shell history by preferring stdin or environment-variable input mechanisms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Preventing credential leakage through process listings is critical when using the CLI in shared environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to print a summary of selected credential entries or groups, with an option to mask password fields in the printed output.
generated_requirements.rationale: Printed records serve as offline backups, and masking passwords prevents inadvertent exposure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support import and export of database entries in standard formats including CSV and XML, with the ability to encrypt exported files.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standard format support enables interoperability with other password managers and backup tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store databases in the KDBX 4.x file format as the default, while maintaining backward-compatible read support for KDBX 3.x and KDB formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Format compatibility ensures users can migrate from older versions without data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and fully functional on Windows (10 and later), macOS (12 and later), and major Linux distributions without recompilation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-platform support maximizes the user base and allows credential sharing across operating systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a plugin/extension API that allows third-party developers to add functionality without modifying the core application, and shall validate plugin integrity via digital signatures.
generated_requirements.rationale: A signed plugin API enables extensibility while preventing malicious plugins from compromising security.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall copy passwords and other sensitive field values to the clipboard and automatically clear the clipboard after a user-configurable timeout (default: 12 seconds).
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic clipboard clearing prevents other applications from silently reading copied credentials.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a recycle bin for deleted entries and groups, allowing recovery of accidentally deleted items before the bin is explicitly emptied.
generated_requirements.rationale: A recycle bin provides a safety net against accidental deletion without compromising the no-backdoor security model.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record a full revision history for each entry, allowing users to view and restore any previous version of a credential.
generated_requirements.rationale: Entry history enables recovery from unintended edits and provides an audit trail of credential changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect concurrent modifications to the same database file and warn the user before overwriting changes made by another session.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict detection prevents silent data loss when the same database is accessed from multiple locations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform an atomic write operation when saving the database (write to a temporary file, then rename) to prevent database corruption in the event of a crash or power failure during save.
generated_requirements.rationale: Atomic saves ensure the database file is never left in a partially written, unreadable state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall open and fully decrypt a database containing up to 100,000 entries within 3 seconds on reference hardware (quad-core CPU, 8 GB RAM), excluding KDF computation time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Acceptable load times ensure the application remains usable even with very large databases.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return search results for a full-text query across a 10,000-entry database within 500 milliseconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sub-second search response maintains a fluid user experience during credential lookup.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a localized user interface supporting at minimum English, German, French, Spanish, and Chinese (Simplified), with a documented process for community-contributed translations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multilingual support broadens accessibility and lowers the barrier to adoption for non-English speakers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility guidelines, including full keyboard navigability and screen-reader compatibility.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance ensures the application is usable by individuals with visual or motor impairments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual help and tooltips for all major UI controls, and shall include a searchable offline user manual.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrated help reduces the learning curve and supports users without requiring internet access.

generated_requirements.text: The system's source code shall be organized into clearly separated modules (UI, encryption, database I/O, plugin host) with documented public interfaces to facilitate independent testing and maintenance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future changes to any single subsystem.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include an automated test suite covering at minimum 80% of core business logic (encryption, CRUD operations, search, auto-type), executed on every build.
generated_requirements.rationale: High test coverage enables confident refactoring and early detection of regressions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all database open, close, lock, and save events to an in-memory audit log accessible within the session, which is discarded when the database is locked.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session-scoped audit logging supports user awareness of database activity without persisting sensitive metadata to disk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the integrity of the database file using a stored HMAC or hash on every open operation and alert the user if tampering or corruption is detected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrity verification detects unauthorized offline modifications or file corruption before decryption proceeds.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support secure desktop (isolated input desktop) mode for the master password entry dialog on Windows to protect against keyloggers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Isolated input desktops prevent malicious software from intercepting the master password at the OS level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to configure and save multiple database profiles (e.g., personal, work) and switch between them without restarting the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-profile support enables users to maintain separate, isolated credential stores for different contexts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall protect in-memory decrypted database contents using process-memory protection mechanisms (e.g., VirtualLock on Windows, mlock on POSIX) to prevent sensitive data from being written to the swap file.
generated_requirements.rationale: Preventing swap-file writes ensures decrypted credentials cannot be recovered from disk after the application exits.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a primary CDN operator to initiate a peering request to one or more peer CDNs through the mediator component, specifying required resource types, capacity, and duration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Peering initiation is the entry point of the federation lifecycle and must be explicitly supported.

generated_requirements.text: The mediator component shall coordinate all interactions between service requesters, policy agents, and peer CDNs during the negotiation lifecycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: The mediator is the central coordination hub and must orchestrate all parties involved in a peering arrangement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support both short-term (on-demand, surge-driven) and long-term (pre-arranged, contractual) peering agreements between the primary CDN and peer CDNs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Both anticipated and unanticipated traffic surges require different peering arrangement durations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable dynamic renegotiation of an active peering agreement when traffic conditions, resource availability, or SLA parameters change during the agreement period.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dynamic renegotiation ensures the federation platform can adapt to real-time changes without service interruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow policy administrators to define, update, and delete governance policies that control negotiation rules, renegotiation triggers, and SLA violation consequences.
generated_requirements.rationale: Policy administrators must have full lifecycle control over governance rules to enforce federation contracts.

generated_requirements.text: The policy agent shall evaluate all incoming and outgoing peering requests against the currently active governance policies before forwarding them to the mediator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Policy enforcement at the request level ensures no peering agreement is established in violation of governance rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the definition of Service Level Agreements (SLAs) that specify at minimum: bandwidth guarantees, latency thresholds, availability targets, content delivery success rates, and penalty clauses for violations.
generated_requirements.rationale: SLAs form the contractual backbone of the federation and must capture all measurable quality commitments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall continuously monitor active peering agreements against their associated SLA parameters and generate a violation event when any SLA threshold is breached.
generated_requirements.rationale: Continuous SLA monitoring is required to detect violations in real time and trigger appropriate consequences.

generated_requirements.text: Upon detection of an SLA violation, the system shall automatically execute the pre-defined consequence actions, which may include issuing penalty notifications, triggering renegotiation, or terminating the peering agreement.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated consequence handling ensures consistent enforcement of contractual obligations without manual intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provision external resources from a selected peer CDN upon successful completion of a peering negotiation, including bandwidth allocation, cache node assignment, and routing configuration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Resource provisioning is the operational outcome of a successful negotiation and must be automated.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support resource delegation, allowing the primary CDN to grant a peer CDN the authority to sub-delegate a portion of the agreed resources to a third-party CDN.
generated_requirements.rationale: Delegation enables multi-hop federation chains necessary for handling large-scale or geographically distributed surges.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support advance resource reservation, enabling CDN operators to pre-book peer CDN capacity for anticipated future traffic surges with a defined reservation window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Advance reservation reduces response latency during anticipated surge events by securing capacity ahead of time.

generated_requirements.text: The mediator shall maintain a prioritised queue of competing peering requests and resolve conflicts using a configurable priority policy (e.g., first-come-first-served, SLA tier, traffic urgency).
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict resolution logic is essential when multiple requesters compete for limited peer CDN resources simultaneously.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and classify incoming requests as potentially malicious based on configurable detection rules (e.g., rate anomalies, invalid credentials, malformed payloads) and automatically reject such requests.
generated_requirements.rationale: Malicious request detection protects the federation platform from abuse and denial-of-service attacks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all participating CDN entities using mutual TLS (mTLS) or an equivalent cryptographic protocol before any negotiation message is processed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mutual authentication prevents impersonation of CDN peers and ensures only trusted entities participate in federation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authorise each action (negotiation, renegotiation, delegation, reservation, policy update) based on the role of the requesting entity (CDN operator, policy administrator, peer CDN).
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based authorisation ensures that each actor can only perform actions within their defined scope of authority.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, tamper-evident audit log of all negotiation events, SLA violations, policy changes, and resource provisioning actions, retaining logs for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logs provide accountability and are required for dispute resolution and compliance verification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose a standardised northbound API (RESTful or equivalent) that CDN operators and policy administrators use to interact with the mediator and policy agent components.
generated_requirements.rationale: A standardised API ensures consistent and interoperable access for all primary users of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use a standardised inter-CDN communication protocol and data format (e.g., CDNI as per IETF RFC 8006/8008 or equivalent) for all messages exchanged between federated CDN peers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standardised protocols and formats are mandatory for interoperability across heterogeneous CDN implementations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide CDN operators with a real-time dashboard displaying the status of all active, pending, and terminated peering agreements, including current SLA compliance metrics.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational visibility enables CDN operators to make informed decisions about active federation arrangements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support graceful termination of a peering agreement, including resource de-provisioning, notification to all parties, and archival of the agreement record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful termination prevents resource leaks and ensures all parties are informed when a peering arrangement ends.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the mediator to discover available peer CDNs and their advertised resource capabilities through a peer registry or discovery service.
generated_requirements.rationale: Peer discovery is a prerequisite for dynamic negotiation, enabling the mediator to identify suitable federation candidates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify all relevant stakeholders (CDN operators, policy administrators, peer CDNs) via configurable notification channels (e.g., webhook, email, API callback) upon key lifecycle events such as agreement establishment, SLA violation, and termination.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications keep all stakeholders informed and enable rapid response to critical federation events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support versioning of SLA templates and governance policies, preserving historical versions and allowing rollback to a previous version.
generated_requirements.rationale: Policy versioning ensures traceability of governance changes and supports recovery from erroneous policy updates.

generated_requirements.text: The mediator shall complete the end-to-end negotiation workflow — from request receipt to agreement confirmation or rejection — within 5 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast negotiation completion is critical for responding to unanticipated traffic surges before service quality degrades.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process SLA monitoring data and detect violations within 10 seconds of a threshold breach occurring.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low-latency violation detection minimises the window during which SLA breaches go unaddressed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support at least 500 concurrent active peering agreements without degradation in negotiation throughput or SLA monitoring accuracy.
generated_requirements.rationale: High concurrency support is required for large-scale CDN federations managing many simultaneous peering arrangements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally to handle a tenfold increase in peering request volume without requiring architectural changes, by adding mediator and policy agent instances.
generated_requirements.rationale: Horizontal scalability ensures the platform can accommodate growth in federation activity without redesign.

generated_requirements.text: All inter-CDN communication shall be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher, and all stored sensitive data (SLA terms, credentials, policy rules) shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or equivalent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit and at rest protects sensitive federation data from interception and unauthorised access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce rate limiting on all API endpoints to prevent denial-of-service attacks, rejecting requests that exceed configurable per-entity thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rate limiting is a fundamental defence against volumetric attacks targeting the federation platform's APIs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate and sanitise all input data received from external CDN peers before processing, rejecting any payload that does not conform to the defined schema.
generated_requirements.rationale: Input validation prevents injection attacks and malformed data from corrupting negotiation or policy state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an overall platform availability of 99.9% or higher, measured on a rolling 30-day basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is essential because CDN federation must remain operational during the traffic surges it is designed to handle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from any single component failure (mediator, policy agent, or peer registry) within 30 seconds through automatic failover to a redundant instance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid automatic failover ensures that component failures do not disrupt active peering agreements or ongoing negotiations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement idempotent negotiation message handling so that duplicate or retransmitted messages do not result in duplicate peering agreements or resource provisioning actions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Idempotency prevents data inconsistency caused by network retries in distributed inter-CDN communication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain data consistency across all mediator instances in a distributed deployment, ensuring that all instances have a consistent view of active agreements and policy state within 2 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eventual consistency with a bounded window prevents conflicting decisions across distributed mediator nodes.

generated_requirements.text: All system components shall be deployable as containerised microservices (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) to support deployment across heterogeneous cloud and on-premises infrastructure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Containerisation enables CDN operators to deploy the platform on their existing infrastructure regardless of the underlying environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to open interoperability standards (e.g., CDNI, OpenAPI 3.x) so that any standards-compliant peer CDN can integrate without requiring custom adapters.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standards conformance maximises the ecosystem of compatible peer CDNs and reduces integration costs.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall achieve a minimum test coverage of 80% (unit and integration tests combined), and all public APIs shall have complete, up-to-date documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: High test coverage and documentation reduce the cost and risk of future changes to the federation platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use a modular architecture with clearly defined interfaces between the mediator, policy agent, peer registry, SLA monitor, and notification components, so that each module can be updated or replaced independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular design with stable interfaces minimises the blast radius of changes and supports independent component evolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose structured health-check and metrics endpoints (e.g., Prometheus-compatible) for all components, enabling integration with standard monitoring and alerting tools.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standardised observability endpoints allow operators to monitor platform health using existing tooling without custom instrumentation.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface and API error messages shall be clear, actionable, and free of internal implementation details, enabling CDN operators and policy administrators to diagnose and resolve issues without vendor support.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clear error messaging reduces operator frustration and accelerates resolution of configuration or negotiation issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a simulation or dry-run mode that allows policy administrators to test the effect of new governance policies against historical negotiation data before activating them in production.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dry-run capability reduces the risk of unintended consequences when deploying new or updated governance policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate periodic (at minimum daily) and on-demand reports summarising peering activity, SLA compliance rates, violation counts, and resource utilisation per peer CDN.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reporting provides CDN operators and administrators with the data needed for capacity planning and contractual review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multi-tenancy, allowing multiple CDN operators to use the platform simultaneously with strict isolation of their peering agreements, policies, and audit logs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-tenancy enables the platform to serve multiple CDN operators from a shared infrastructure without data leakage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable maximum negotiation timeout, after which an unresolved negotiation is automatically cancelled and all parties are notified.
generated_requirements.rationale: Negotiation timeouts prevent indefinitely pending agreements from consuming mediator resources and blocking other requests.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the ingestion of real-time traffic telemetry from the primary CDN to automatically trigger peering initiation when traffic thresholds defined by the operator are exceeded.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated surge detection and peering initiation reduces response time to unanticipated traffic spikes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall cryptographically sign all negotiation messages and SLA documents exchanged between parties to ensure non-repudiation of agreements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Digital signatures provide non-repudiation, ensuring that no party can deny having agreed to the terms of a peering arrangement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement circuit-breaker patterns on all outbound calls to peer CDN endpoints, preventing cascading failures when a peer CDN becomes unresponsive.
generated_requirements.rationale: Circuit breakers isolate failures in peer CDN integrations and protect the mediator from being overwhelmed by timeout accumulation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support configuration-driven deployment, where all tunable parameters (timeouts, thresholds, rate limits, priority weights) are externalised in configuration files or a configuration management service and do not require code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalised configuration enables operators to tune system behaviour without redeployment, reducing operational overhead.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with relevant data protection regulations (e.g., GDPR) by ensuring that any personally identifiable information collected during federation operations is minimised, stored securely, and subject to defined retention and deletion policies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory compliance is a non-negotiable obligation for any platform operating across jurisdictions that handle user-related data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a pre-VCD mapping tool that translates national evidence and attestation types from all participating EU Member States into the common framework defined by EU Directive 2004/18/EC.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-border interoperability depends on a canonical mapping layer that normalises heterogeneous national evidence into a single EU-level framework.

generated_requirements.text: The pre-VCD mapping tool shall allow economic operators to identify which national documents from their home Member State satisfy the selection and exclusion criteria specified by a contracting authority in a different Member State.
generated_requirements.rationale: Economic operators must be able to determine document equivalence across borders without legal expertise in every Member State.

generated_requirements.text: The pre-VCD mapping tool shall allow contracting authorities to view the EU Directive 2004/18/EC equivalents of evidence types submitted by economic operators from any participating Member State.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contracting authorities need a standardised view of foreign documents to evaluate qualification criteria consistently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support automatic collection of evidence and attestations directly from authoritative issuing bodies, including public registries, tax authorities, and financial institutions, on behalf of economic operators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated retrieval from issuing bodies eliminates manual effort and reduces the risk of forged or outdated documents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow economic operators to manually upload qualification documents, attestations, and certificates when automatic retrieval from an issuing body is not available.
generated_requirements.rationale: Not all issuing bodies will have electronic interfaces, so manual upload is required as a fallback mechanism.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall compile collected and uploaded evidence into a standardised, digitally secure VCD package that conforms to the defined VCD data schema.
generated_requirements.rationale: A standardised package format ensures that all contracting authorities can parse and evaluate VCD submissions uniformly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable economic operators to submit a completed VCD package to a specified contracting authority through the platform, without requiring physical document exchange.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminating physical exchange is a core objective of the platform, reducing administrative burden and processing time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated VCD viewing interface for contracting authorities to receive, open, and evaluate submitted VCD packages.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contracting authorities require a purpose-built interface to review structured qualification evidence efficiently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support reuse of an existing VCD package across multiple tender procedures without requiring the economic operator to reassemble or resubmit documents already on file.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reuse of VCD packages reduces duplication of effort for economic operators participating in multiple tenders.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support on-demand retrieval of individual attestations from issuing bodies at the time a contracting authority requests them, without prior action by the economic operator.
generated_requirements.rationale: On-demand retrieval represents the highest maturity stage of the platform and ensures attestations are always current.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a staged maturity model with at least three levels: (1) manual document upload, (2) automated evidence collection with structured data, and (3) fully automated on-demand attestation retrieval.
generated_requirements.rationale: A staged model allows incremental adoption by Member States with varying levels of digital infrastructure maturity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support machine-readable, structured data formats (e.g., XML or JSON) for VCD package content at maturity levels 2 and above, enabling automated processing by contracting authority systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Machine-readable formats are required for automated evaluation and integration with back-office procurement systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a registry of all registered National VCD Service Providers, including their accreditation status, supported Member States, and supported evidence types.
generated_requirements.rationale: A central registry is necessary for routing, trust validation, and governance oversight of national service providers.

generated_requirements.text: The European VCD Service Provider shall maintain and publish the master mapping framework, and national service providers shall be able to submit proposed updates to national evidence mappings for approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: Governance of the mapping framework must be centralised at the European level while allowing national input to reflect legal changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, distinguishing at minimum the roles of Economic Operator, Contracting Authority, National VCD Service Provider, European VCD Service Provider, and Issuing Body.
generated_requirements.rationale: Each actor has distinct permissions and responsibilities that must be enforced to prevent unauthorised access or data manipulation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users and service providers using qualified electronic certificates or equivalent trust services compliant with the eIDAS Regulation.
generated_requirements.rationale: eIDAS-compliant authentication ensures legally recognised identity verification across all EU Member States.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply qualified electronic signatures or seals to all VCD packages at the point of compilation to guarantee document integrity and non-repudiation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Digital signatures ensure that VCD packages cannot be tampered with after compilation and provide legal non-repudiation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, tamper-evident audit log of all actions performed on a VCD package, including creation, modification, submission, retrieval, and viewing events, with timestamps and actor identities.
generated_requirements.rationale: An audit trail is essential for legal accountability, dispute resolution, and regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce explicit, informed consent from economic operators before any of their qualification documents or personal data are shared with a contracting authority or third party.
generated_requirements.rationale: GDPR and data protection principles require that data subjects consent to the sharing of their personal information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow economic operators to define and manage access permissions for each VCD package, specifying which contracting authorities may view it and for what duration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Granular access control gives economic operators sovereignty over their qualification data and limits unnecessary exposure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the expiry dates of all evidence items within a VCD package and notify the economic operator when any document is approaching or has passed its validity period.
generated_requirements.rationale: Expired documents render a VCD package non-compliant; proactive notification allows timely renewal.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a search and filter interface enabling contracting authorities to query submitted VCD packages by economic operator identifier, Member State, evidence type, and compliance status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Efficient search capabilities reduce the time contracting authorities spend locating and evaluating relevant qualification evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support integration with national issuing body APIs through a standardised connector interface, allowing new issuing bodies to be onboarded without changes to the core platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: A plug-in connector model enables scalable expansion to new issuing bodies across Member States.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a human-readable summary report of a VCD package, listing all included evidence items, their mapped EU Directive 2004/18/EC criteria, validity status, and issuing body.
generated_requirements.rationale: A summary report aids contracting authority evaluators who may not be familiar with the structured data format.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multilingual user interfaces in all official EU languages relevant to the participating Member States, with the mapping framework labels available in at least English and the national language of each Member State.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multilingual support is mandatory for a pan-European platform serving users across diverse linguistic jurisdictions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all VCD package data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent, and all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strong encryption protects sensitive qualification and personal data from unauthorised access both in storage and during transmission.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) for all personal data processed, including implementing data minimisation, purpose limitation, and the right to erasure.
generated_requirements.rationale: GDPR compliance is a legal obligation for any system processing personal data of EU residents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve and maintain compliance with EU Directive 2004/18/EC and any superseding procurement directives (e.g., 2014/24/EU) as they apply to electronic qualification procedures.
generated_requirements.rationale: Legal compliance with applicable EU procurement directives is a foundational obligation of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be interoperable with the European Single Procurement Document (ESPD) standard, allowing VCD package data to be mapped to and from ESPD-compliant formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Interoperability with ESPD ensures alignment with the broader EU e-procurement ecosystem and avoids data silos.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an end-to-end VCD package submission response time of no more than 5 seconds under normal load conditions (up to 1,000 concurrent users).
generated_requirements.rationale: Acceptable response times are critical for user adoption and operational efficiency in time-sensitive procurement procedures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 concurrent economic operator sessions and 1,000 concurrent contracting authority sessions without degradation of core functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: A pan-European platform must handle high concurrency during peak procurement periods across all Member States.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% uptime on a rolling 12-month basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 72 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is essential because procurement deadlines are legally binding and downtime could cause operators to miss tender submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from any unplanned outage and restore full service within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 1 hour.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined RTO and RPO targets ensure that data loss and service interruption are bounded within acceptable limits for a critical public infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain data integrity of all VCD packages through cryptographic hash verification at each stage of the package lifecycle, detecting any unauthorised modification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hash-based integrity checks provide a tamper-evident mechanism that is essential for the legal validity of submitted qualification documents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed using a modular, service-oriented architecture that allows individual national service provider connectors, mapping modules, and viewing components to be updated or replaced independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of updating components as national legislation or technical standards evolve.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide comprehensive API documentation, data dictionaries, and integration guides to enable national service providers and issuing bodies to implement compliant connectors independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clear documentation reduces onboarding time and errors for new national participants integrating with the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on standard cloud infrastructure and shall not depend on any single vendor's proprietary services, ensuring portability across hosting environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vendor independence protects the platform from lock-in and supports sovereignty requirements of EU public sector infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards to ensure usability for economic operators and contracting authority staff with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance is a legal requirement under EU Web Accessibility Directive and ensures inclusive access to public procurement.

generated_requirements.text: A first-time economic operator with no prior system experience shall be able to assemble and submit a complete VCD package within 30 minutes, as validated by usability testing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ease of use is critical for SME adoption, as small businesses may lack dedicated procurement staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement penetration-testing-validated protection against OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, with mandatory remediation of critical and high findings before each major release.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive security testing ensures the platform is hardened against the most prevalent web application attack vectors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally to accommodate a 300% increase in transaction volume above baseline within 15 minutes through automated infrastructure scaling mechanisms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Elastic scalability is required to handle unpredictable spikes in usage around major procurement deadlines.

generated_requirements.text: All inter-service communications between national VCD service providers and the European VCD Service Provider shall use mutually authenticated TLS connections with certificates issued by a trusted European PKI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mutual TLS with a trusted PKI ensures that only accredited service providers can exchange data within the federated trust network.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain VCD packages and associated audit logs for a minimum of 10 years after the last associated tender procedure closes, in compliance with applicable public procurement record-keeping obligations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term retention supports post-award audits, legal challenges, and regulatory inspections of procurement decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The mapping framework shall be versioned, and the system shall preserve historical versions so that VCD packages compiled under a prior framework version remain evaluable against the framework version active at the time of submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Framework versioning ensures legal certainty by preventing retroactive reinterpretation of submitted qualification evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a sandbox environment that mirrors the production platform, allowing national service providers and issuing bodies to test integrations without affecting live data.
generated_requirements.rationale: A sandbox reduces the risk of integration errors reaching production and accelerates onboarding of new national participants.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose a standardised, versioned RESTful API that allows contracting authority back-office systems to programmatically retrieve and process VCD package data.
generated_requirements.rationale: A public API enables contracting authorities to integrate VCD data directly into their existing e-procurement workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify economic operators, contracting authorities, and relevant service providers of all significant lifecycle events (e.g., package submission, document expiry, access grant/revocation) via configurable email and in-platform alerts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications keep all actors informed of status changes and prevent missed deadlines or compliance lapses.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a governance dashboard for the European VCD Service Provider displaying real-time metrics on platform usage, mapping framework coverage per Member State, service provider accreditation status, and SLA compliance.
generated_requirements.rationale: A governance dashboard enables the European service provider to monitor platform health and enforce compliance obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a formal accreditation and onboarding process for National VCD Service Providers, requiring submission of legal registration, technical compliance evidence, and acceptance of the platform governance agreement before activation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formal accreditation ensures that only trusted, legally compliant entities operate within the federated service network.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and reject duplicate or conflicting evidence submissions for the same economic operator and evidence type, prompting the operator to confirm the intended version before finalising the VCD package.
generated_requirements.rationale: Duplicate detection preserves data integrity and prevents ambiguous or contradictory qualification evidence from reaching contracting authorities.

ground_truth_requirements.text: A VCD will facilitate electronic Tendering by providing cross-border data and document solution that contains the necessary attestations and certificates typically required in eTendering. Thus it can be seen as a container for documents. Yet up to now, the tendering documents such as attestations and certificates required in public procurement procedures differ between Member States. When a contracting authority publishes a contract notice, it shall include the selection, qualification and nonexclusion criteria. The economic operators have to submit evidence and proof in respect of these criteria. Hence, during the preparation of the tender an economic operator needs to collect the respective evidences (i.e. certificates and qualification documents) from a number of issuing bodies (e.g. public registries, banks, pre-qualification bodies, etc.) to prove conformance with the given selection and exclusion criteria. The overall aims and expectations of a virtual company dossier solution as set out in the description of work can be described as follows: A VCD will facilitate electronic Tendering by providing cross-border data and document solution that contains the necessary attestations and certificates typically required in eTendering. Thus it can be seen as a container for documents. Yet up to now, the tendering documents such as attestations and certificates required in public procurement procedures differ between Member States. When a contracting authority publishes a contract notice, it shall include the selection, qualification and nonexclusion criteria. The economic operators have to submit evidence and proof in respect of these criteria. Hence, during the preparation of the tender an economic operator needs to collect the respective evidences (i.e. certificates and qualification documents) from a number of issuing bodies (e.g. public registries, banks, pre-qualification bodies, etc.) to prove conformance with the given selection and exclusion criteria. The overall aims and expectations of a virtual company dossier solution as set out in the description of work can be described as follows: A major challenge of PEPPOLs’ work package 2 is to integrate various stakeholders in the development process and to set up an IT system which supports a common set of evidences based on electronic business certificates and qualification documents that are most frequently required. The criteria must be consistent with directive 2004/18/EC (EC 30.04.2004): 1. The VCD will support any authorised entities (economic operator, intermediary, contracting authority or IT service such as an eTendering system) in creating an electronic information package consisting of the required documentation, evidences, proof, attestations, certificates, declarations and metadata. 2. In order to create the VCD, an implemented IT system will have to collect certificates and attestations from existing registries. It also enables the economic operator to add self-declarations or other documents of formal qualification. 3. Furthermore, the VCD solution supports economic operators in producing a VCD and in enabling them to submit the required documentation (assembled as an information package) to any contracting authority in Europe. 4. In the same way the VCD as an implemented IT system will enable contracting authorities or their eTendering systems to interpret and accept the documentation submitted by the economic operator. 5. Therefore the contracting authority must either specify the documentation that has to be submitted by economic operator or the criteria of qualitative selection and exclusion that have to be fulfilled by the economic operator. 6. For all parties (economic operator, intermediary, contracting authority, issuing bodies) it will be of high importance that the VCD Service Providers are trustworthy; this implies that the services are precise, up-to-date, available and reliable. Criteria are listed in the Directive and can be established by the contracting authority within the contract notice. A key aspect in the VCD development is to support a common set of criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion derived from the directive and their fulfilment through evidences. Work package 2 will not implement a common set of attestations. Thereby it must be taken into account that some countries do not issue such documents or certificates (e.g. in Norway extracts from the judicial record may be produced in respect of economic operators, but not in respect of private persons, for the purpose of a tender procedure) and this hinders economic operators to evidence certain criteria. In such cases, economic operators can only provide similar evidences that fulfil the same criteria. Where the country in question does not issue such documents or certificates, or where these do not cover all the cases they may be replaced by a declaration on oath, a solemn declaration, a notary or a competent professional or trade body, in the country of origin or in the country whence that person comes. So the link between individual evidences and the respective criteria they may approve for (as listed above) must be precisely indicated and mapped in order to support economic operators in their activity. Besides the mapping of criteria and evidences in a respective country, directive 2004/18/EC indicates also the need for accessibility of “Official lists of approved economic operators and certification by bodies established under public or private law” (cf. Article 52). Article 52 indicates a potential solution which may be addressed in the VCD development too. Article 45 - Personal situation of the candidate or tenderer: e.g. absence of conviction,bankruptcy, fulfilment of payments of social security contributions or taxes, etc. Article 46 - suitability to pursue a professional activity: e.g. certificate of registration from thecommercial register Article 47 - economic and financial standing: e.g. balance sheets Article 48 - technical and/or professional ability of economic operators, e.g. certificates ofsatisfactory execution of past works Article 49 - quality assurance standards, e.g. ISO certificates Article 50 - environmental management standards (Article 51 - Additional documentation and information)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A major precondition for the enforcement of stage 1 is the definition of a standard representation of European criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion. These criteria should be derived directly from 09/11/2009 – version 1.1 (approved by EC) the Articles 45-50 of the Directive 2004/18/EC in order to ensure employment in Pan-European public procurement procedures. The right granularity and consistency of criteria will be important to ensure legitimacy and to retrieve adequate mapping results. Thus the criteria may have to be iteratively redefined according to the results given by specific mappings. A major precondition for the enforcement of stage 1 is the definition of a standard representation of European criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion. These criteria should be derived directly from 09/11/2009 – version 1.1 (approved by EC) the Articles 45-50 of the Directive 2004/18/EC in order to ensure employment in Pan-European public procurement procedures. The right granularity and consistency of criteria will be important to ensure legitimacy and to retrieve adequate mapping results. Thus the criteria may have to be iteratively redefined according to the results given by specific mappings. A standard representation of common criteria of qualitative selection and exclusion MUST be defined according to Articles 45-50 of Directive 2004/18/EC. The right granularity and consistency of criteria MUST be found through testing and iterative redefinition in order to ensure legitimacy and to retrieve adequate mapping results. Input will come from the Articles 45-50 of Directive 2004/18/EC
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: National requirements of qualitative selection and exclusion for qualitative selection and exclusion have to adequately represent the national legal conditions. They have to be formulated in a way that they can be mapped to common European criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion mentioned by the Articles 45-50. National requirements of qualitative selection and exclusion for qualitative selection and exclusion have to adequately represent the national legal conditions. They have to be formulated in a way that they can be mapped to common European criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion mentioned by the Articles 45-50. National requirements for qualitative selection and exclusion MUST correspond to the EC criteria given in Article 45-50. National criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion SHOULD have the right granularity and should be consistent among themselves. Input for the current function is taken from the National Public Procurement Acts.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: In a first step, all member states do have to establish a mapping of the criteria defined in the relevant EU directive 2004/18/EC to the national attestations giving evidence to these criteria. To ensure a degree of comparability and reliability among the results, the mapping should be based on a common template. For the overall activity it will be important to have the appropriate legal support in order to ensure legitimacy of mappings. In a first step, all member states do have to establish a mapping of the criteria defined in the relevant EU directive 2004/18/EC to the national attestations giving evidence to these criteria. To ensure a degree of comparability and reliability among the results, the mapping should be based on a common template. For the overall activity it will be important to have the appropriate legal support in order to ensure legitimacy of mappings. A template that ensures comparable and reliable mappings between criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion and national attestations MUST be defined. The members of this work package MUST perform the mapping between national attestations and European criteria of qualitative selection and exclusion. The resulting template has to be computable by machines and readable by humans allowing the assembly of VCD packages through manual and/or automated operations.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: In order to achieve trust and an agreement on the mutual recognition of qualification documents as demanded in the directive, all artefacts and mapping results that relate to the mapping of evidence have to be approved from a legal point of view. Compliances to national and European conditions must be ensured in particular for the definition of a standard representation of common criteria of qualitative selection and exclusion, the definition of a mapping template and the mapping between national attestations and the common criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion. In order to achieve trust and an agreement on the mutual recognition of qualification documents as demanded in the directive, all artefacts and mapping results that relate to the mapping of evidence have to be approved from a legal point of view. Compliances to national and European conditions must be ensured in particular for the definition of a standard representation of common criteria of qualitative selection and exclusion, the definition of a mapping template and the mapping between national attestations and the common criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion. Legitimacy of standard representation of common criteria of qualitative selection and exclusion MUST be ensured. Legitimacy of standard mapping template MUST be ensured. Legitimacy of mapping between national attestations and common criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion MUST be ensured.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The commonly approved national mappings have to be made accessible to the different stakeholders as especially economic operators, contracting authorities and national VCD service providers. For that purpose, a basic infrastructure or system architecture or a mapping facility has to be defined. The mapping facility, independent of how such as “system” is actually implemented, has to provide the services with regard to the pre-VCD mapping tool The commonly approved national mappings have to be made accessible to the different stakeholders as especially economic operators, contracting authorities and national VCD service providers. For that purpose, a basic infrastructure or system architecture or a mapping facility has to be defined. The mapping facility, independent of how such as “system” is actually implemented, has to provide the services with regard to the pre-VCD mapping tool It MUST be defined by which technical means the mapping service will be provided. Centralized or decentralized infrastructure? Will there be one centralized system on a European level hosting and providing the mapping or a decentralized system consisting of interconnected national systems? In the latter case, one could define that each national system only hosts the national mapping or that each one hosts all the different national mappings which would make a synchronization of the different systems necessary. A decision to deploy either a centralised or a decentralized infrastructure that provides the mapping service MUST be made. By which technical means will the mapping be provided? The technical means to provide the mapping service has to be defined in dependence on the choice of the pilot solution. The simplest form without any technical support could for example consist in the one-time distribution of mapping tables in paper-based form. Another solution could consist of a web-based access to a system providing the mapping by querying an underlying database which contains the mapping table. A more advanced solution would be an automated processing of the mapping according to the relevant criteria that therefore have to be specified by the contracting authority in a dedicated electronic format. Also the higher maturity levels defined in this vision (stage 2-4) can use the mapping service in order to retrieve the mapping information. An entity that hosts the mapping facility MUST be defined in dependence of the type of infrastructure and the dedicated governing model. Which entity will host the mapping facility? In dependence of the choice of infrastructures (centralised – decentralized) and the organisation that governs the infrastructure and mapping facility it has to be defined who will be responsible to host the mapping facility.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A governing model has to be defined in order to deploy the infrastructure, to maintain its state and sustainability over time. In this respect the composition, structure and scope of the governing model has to be formally defined to ensure fulfilment of functions like liability, sustainability and maintainability. The dedicated governing model should have the necessary status and power to govern its assigned functions. A governing model with the necessary status and power MUST be formally defined in order to ensure fulfilment of functions like liability, sustainability and maintainability. A governing model has to be defined in order to deploy the infrastructure, to maintain its state and sustainability over time. In this respect the composition, structure and scope of the governing model has to be formally defined to ensure fulfilment of functions like liability, sustainability and maintainability. The dedicated governing model should have the necessary status and power to govern its assigned functions. A governing model with the necessary status and power MUST be formally defined in order to ensure fulfilment of functions like liability, sustainability and maintainability. Liability: In law a legal liability is a situation in which a person or organisation is financially and legally responsible. The liability of the organisation that governs infrastructure and mapping facility has to be defined in order to ensure trust into the system and particular mapping results? The Liability of organisation that governs the infrastructure and mapping facility MUST be defined Sustainability: Sustainability is the capacity to maintain a certain process or state into the future. The governing model must ensure that the state of the mapping system will be kept over time. Therefore the mapping system has to be based on the commonly agreed mapping and no changes are allowed without a common agreement. The mapping facility MUST ensure sustainability of the system over time based on commonly agreed mapping schemes. Maintainability: The mapping has to be easily maintainable which could be of interest in the case of changes of the legal basis on national or European level. A formal procedure has to be found how changes are applied to the system. Moreover an organisation or institution that governs the process of change management has to be defined. A formal procedure to make changes to the system MUST be defined.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Stage 1 is the key concept for ensuring interoperability between the different national systems of evidence provision in public procurement procedures. Every higher stage will be depended on the mapping model defined in this stage. Thus this stage will ensure the integration of different stages and interoperability as such. Therefore each national service provider that provides a VCD (stage 2-4) and all relevant key other stakeholder such as economic operators, contracting authorities and certifiers must be able to retrieve the latest mapping information from the mapping facility. The mapping facility MUST be accessible to all relevant key stakeholders such as economic operators, contracting authorities, certifiers and national service providers. Stage 1 is the key concept for ensuring interoperability between the different national systems of evidence provision in public procurement procedures. Every higher stage will be depended on the mapping model defined in this stage. Thus this stage will ensure the integration of different stages and interoperability as such. Therefore each national service provider that provides a VCD (stage 2-4) and all relevant key other stakeholder such as economic operators, contracting authorities and certifiers must be able to retrieve the latest mapping information from the mapping facility. The mapping facility MUST be accessible to all relevant key stakeholders such as economic operators, contracting authorities, certifiers and national service providers.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Attention has to be paid to a number of requirements such as reliability, accessibility, usability, maintenance, authenticity, actuality, compliance of mapping to laws and national structures, etc. The pre-VCD mapping tool has to be accredited by the European Union and the Member States. A number of functionalities of the mapping tool are: Attention has to be paid to a number of requirements such as reliability, accessibility, usability, maintenance, authenticity, actuality, compliance of mapping to laws and national structures, etc. The pre-VCD mapping tool has to be accredited by the European Union and the Member States. A number of functionalities of the mapping tool are: Besides such functionalities of the tool, some general requirements regarding the operation of the pre- VCD mapping tool have to be implemented: Provision of an update mechanism maintained by the individual Member State. The pre-VCD mapping tool is openly accessible for economic operators and contracting authorities etc. The tool interface is available in the official languages of the European Union. A powerful search mechanism is implemented to deliver good results when economic operators query the pre-VCD mapping tool in order to understand what evidences he can deliver to confer to the selection and exclusion criteria set in a public tender in another Member State. The results are delivered in a format simple to use for the economic operators; e.g. a check list of evidences he can collect from respective issuing bodies in his country, which fulfil the selection criteria and prove the non-conformance with exclusion criteria set by the contracting authority from a different Member State. The Member States shall agree on a common representation format for the information they intend to supply into the knowledge base supporting the tool’s operations. The Member States shall supply the information generated from the analysis of the national context they represent. The Member States need to update the mapping in case of any change within a short time frame (e.g. five days after a legal change has been put into force). The European service provider for the pre-VCD mapping tool has to ensure that European legal changes are implemented quickly (e.g. within five days after enforcement). The European service provider takes responsibility of the operative implementation and compliance of the mapping model implemented in the pre-VCD mapping tool with the respective European and national conditions.
